          "result": "# 【0†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file0\nContent Snippet:\n* **Jhace learns:** He can admit feelings without losing focus.  Partnership is his new normal.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can rely on someone without betraying her standards.  She will not lose her individuality by loving.\n* **Reader learns:** The romance is explicit now.  There is no “will they/won’t they.”  They will; they just haven’t yet.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Keep physical intimacy low due to exhaustion.  Use humor and vulnerability.  No Soulpulse here, so no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 14: “Fault Lines”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution** (third and final use in N01)\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4–5, Shaping→Striking\n* **Location:** Edge of two districts (load‑bearing node between a wealthier and a poorer zone)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Alarm bells and runners call Jhace and Tiffani to a massive structural failure at a district boundary.  A bridge‑like load‑bearing node connecting two zones is cracking.  If it falls, dozens of buildings on either side will tumble.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  They arrive to find RRO crews pulling back to protect the wealthy side.  Poor families on the other side cannot evacuate.  The failure is at multiple points.  Jhace tries a dual hold for the first time: two contact points on two different beams.  The cost surges; his legs tremble; his vision blurs.  Tiffani directs him, but there are too many variables.  Bram shouts warnings.  Salee’s people try to evacuate as many as possible.\n3. **Partial Resolution:**  They slow the collapse.  Many escape.  But the node still falls on the poor side when Jhace’s body gives out.  Several die.  Jhace staggers and vomits from the strain.  Tiffani catches him.  They realize he has crossed into Stage 2: tremor no longer fades; vein‑lines are visible at rest.\n4. **Aftermath:**  Exhausted, they see RRO officials count the wealthy district as “saved” while blaming the poor district’s collapse on “structural weakness.”  Anger simmers.\n\n**Exit Hook:** As Jhace leans on Tiffani to walk away, he feels his muscles twitch uncontrollably.  Tiffani whispers, “You can’t keep doing dual holds.  We need to find another way.”  The note hangs unresolved.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Dual holds cost exponentially more.  His body cannot sustain that long.  He is essentially at Stage 2.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Emotional states (desperation vs. anger) influence cost.  She must research further and find ways to share load among multiple users.\n* **Reader learns:** Stage progression is real; the cost curve spikes; not all crises can end in full success.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show intense cost.  Keep crisis grounded in physical description.  Use voice rules.  Do not include any hints of gods or future events.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 15: “The Grip That Holds”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 5, Quench\n* **Location:** Quiet rooftop/back‑room (similar to Ch 13)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Post‑Ch 14 crisis, Jhace is nearly unconscious.\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file1\nContent Snippet:\n5. **Partial Resolution:**  People evacuate.  The building doesn’t fully collapse.  Jhace collapses from the exertion.  His hands shake uncontrollably.  He blacks out.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace wakes to chaos and sees Tiffani standing over him with a look of shock and curiosity.  They exchange a brief, bewildered glance.  The world is different now.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** He can do something impossible when protecting someone.  It costs him physically (ache, tremor).  It feels like heat and vibration.\n* **Tiffani learns:** People can manipulate structures with copper‑gold light.  She has no explanation yet but she’s determined to find one.\n* **Reader learns:** The Piercing is world‑changing; Soulpulse exists; it is tied to desperation and contact; it leaves residue (glow, tremor, healing).  Chloen’s leg heals quickly.\n* **Hidden:** Why this power exists, what the Piercing was, any link to gods or divine principles.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Full crisis intensity.  Show cost: aching hands, tremor, exhaustion.  Use sensory language (dust, noise).  Keep voices distinct (Jhace terse, Tiffani more observational).  Do not name the ability yet; characters just describe what they see.\n\n---\n\n### Interlude I (After Chapter 6) – Corlexi “System vs Chaos”\n\n* See `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` for details.  This interlude shows the RRO council chamber through Corlexi’s eyes.  It contrasts institutional attempts at order with the ground‑level chaos experienced in the first six chapters.  It seeds the registration conflict.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n# 【2†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file2\nContent Snippet:\n**Commitment Scene (Ch 15):**\n- Quiet space (rooftop/back room), brief window without crisis\n- Exhausted honesty\n- *Tiffani: \"The world is broken. We can't fix all of it. But we can hold something.\"*\n- *Jhace: \"Then we hold.\"*\n- They agree to continue their work and their relationship\n\n---\n\n## BRAM KELTER\n\n**Age:** 52 Turns  \n**Profession:** Forgemaster, heat-metal specialist, unofficial keeper of Jhace's sanity  \n**Physical:** Broad-shouldered, \"solid\" middle‑age build. Beard more gray than brown, worn long enough to tuck into leather apron (worn constantly). Hands scarred from decades of forge-work. Moves deliberately, economically.\n\n### Core Psychology\n\n**The Compromise He Regrets:**\n- Brilliant metalworker in youth\n- Developed new alloy composition (lighter, stronger)\n- Guild rejected it (threatened suppliers, training protocols)\n- Could have fought, gone independent, proved them right\n- Instead: accepted guild decision, returned to traditional methods\n- Not because believed they were right—because tired of fighting systems that outlast individuals\n- This choice haunts him\n\n**Relationship to Jhace:**\n- Met Jhace at 19 Turns, already showing obsessive patterns\n- Saw immediately: this one will burn himself out trying to save everyone\n- Can't save Jhace from himself, but can slow the burning\n- Shows up with water when Jhace forgets to drink\n- Drags him to shelter before collapse\n- Argues when stubbornness becomes self-destruction\n- Not father-son (not replacing Jhace's dead parents)\n- More like: elder brother watching younger walk toward cliff, knowing can't stop it but refusing to let him walk alone\n\n### What He Fears\nWatching Jhace die the way people with obsessions die—alone, exhausted, having given everything and still believing it wasn't enough. Knows the work will never be done. Knows Jhace will never feel like he's saved \"enough\" people. Fears watching Jhace realize this too late, after already destroyed himself.\n\n### What He Regrets\nCompromising with guild. Not fighting harder for his alloy. Every time he sees innovation crushed by institutional inertia: *I did that too. I chose comfort over conviction.* Doesn't regret peaceful life he has—regrets wondering what would've happened if he'd chosen differently. Also: never had children (wanted them once, relationship ended badly, his fault—put work first). Too old now, too set in patterns. Jhace closest thing to someone he'd protect like family—both precious and painful.\n\n### What He Lies About\n\"I'm fine with how things turned out.\" He's not. He settled. He compromised. Chose institutional approval over personal integrity. Life he built is good, but not the life he could have had. Tells himself and others he has no regrets because admitting regret means admitting wasted decades playing by rules he knew were wrong.\n\n### What He Pretends to Know\nHow to convince Jhace to rest. Acts like has authority, like suggestions carry weight. Reality: Jhace tolerates him but doesn't obey. Bram knows this. Keeps offering advice, keeps showing up, keeps pretending if he finds right words Jhace will listen and slow down. Pretense necessary because alternative—admitting he's powerless to stop Jhace's self-destruction—is unbearable.\n\n### His Humor\n\n**Style:** Gentle mockery + dad jokes + resigned observational comedy\n# 【3†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file3\nContent Snippet:\n**Placement:**  \n- Between Chapter 11 and Chapter 12.  \n- Reader must have just experienced:  \n  - The RRO confrontation over registration and control.  \n  - Jhace and Tiffani rejecting centralized assignment in favor of their own judgment.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Third-person limited, anchored to **Salee**.\n- No omniscient commentary, no divine angle.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Communal food/meeting space in the Dregs.\n- Evening/Quench phase after a day of severe strain.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 800–1,200 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Connection as Infrastructure”** (quiet mirror of Jhace’s structural work).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Weariness → quiet satisfaction → sober realism.  \n  - Not “hopeful optimism” but “we are doing the work and people are still alive.”\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Show the crisis from the bottom:\n   - Families clustered around improvised meals.\n   - Shared childcare, bed-space rotations, watch schedules.\n   - Barter and labor exchange as survival tools.\n2. Establish Salee’s worldview:\n   - People save people; relationships are the infrastructure that doesn’t collapse.\n   - Coordination is horizontal, not hierarchical.\n   - Deep suspicion of institutions that only show up when it’s politically convenient.\n3. Contrast with RRO:\n   - She hears about (or briefly interacts with) RRO edicts.\n   - See them as “far away” and slow compared to what the Dregs need.\n   - She is not anti-structure: she’s building a different kind of structure.\n4. Connect to J/T indirectly:\n   - News that “there are a couple up-slope who hold buildings with their bare hands” reaches her.\n   - She is more interested in whether they’ll work *with* her network, not for any office.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Salee may:\n  - Know roughly that unusual abilities exist.\n  - Hear rumors about Jhace/Tiffani’s work as anonymous heroes.\n- She may NOT:\n  - Know their names or detailed power mechanics.\n  - Recognize them as long-term linchpins of the world.\n  - Have any direct awareness of cosmic-scale stakes.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Warm, practical, unsentimental.\n- Uses humor to keep people moving:  \n  - e.g., joking about “promotions” meaning “you get to cook instead of dig latrines today.”\n- Never airy or idealistic; everything is grounded in logistics and bodies.\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends with an action, not a speech:  \n  - e.g., Salee standing to go negotiate a food redistribution or shelter swap.\n- The last feeling should be: “this is what real work looks like from the ground.”\n\n---\n\n### 3. EPILOGUE — AUDITOR\n\n**Placement:**  \n- After Chapter 15, after both Interludes.  \n- Final text object in N01.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Claustrophically controlled perspective labeled as **The Auditor**.\n- Voice is clinical, observational, almost report-like.\n- Absolutely no emotional self-exposure.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Abstract / unlocated.  \n  - No specific physical room, city, or body described.\n  - Focus on data, trends, and projections.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 600–800 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Meta Assessment”** (post-season scouting report).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Detachment throughout.  \n  - Subtle tilt from “baseline” to “increased interest.”\n# 【4†Location_Bibles_N01.md†file-2gPmtJKiin2h9WtfphNzQn】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Location_Bibles_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file4\nContent Snippet:\nSide rooms hold maps, ledgers, resource lists.  Wide arches lead to corridors and smaller offices.\n* **Environment:** Echoey; voices carry and blend into a dull roar when many speak.  Light from tall windows during day (Kindling/Pouring), supplemented by oil lamps.  Air cool and slightly musty.\n* **Social/Function:** Focal point for institutional authority.  Here, leaders like Corlexi push for registration and control.  Guild representatives, healers, and merchants argue policy.  The council tries to impose order but is bogged down by bureaucracy.\n* **Appearances:** Interlude I (Corlexi’s view), Chapter 11 (registration confrontation), Chapter 14 (emergency meeting in later crisis).\n\n---\n\n### MERROW GLASS & MIRRORS OBSERVATORY\n\n* **Location:** In the Glass Quarter.  Combined workshop (ground level), living space (upper level), and observation platform (roof).\n* **Physical:**\n  * Ground floor: kilns, annealing ovens, workbenches, storage for glass raw materials and tools.\n  * Upper level: living quarters and office.  Books and instruments share space with Tiffani’s simple bed and eating area.\n  * Rooftop observatory: a flat platform with a permanently mounted theodolite, logbook stands, and measurement marks etched into stone.  Protected by a low wall.\n* **Pre‑Piercing Use:** Tracking atmospheric and celestial patterns, testing glass quality, refining optical lenses.\n* **Post‑Piercing:** Collapse in Chapter 3 damages the upper structure.  The platform becomes dangerous, requiring careful salvage.\n* **Appearances:** Ch 2 (anomaly observations), Ch 3 (evacuation and collapse), Ch 5 (return to assess damage).\n\n---\n\n### TRAINING RIG LOCATION\n\n* **Purpose:** Controlled environment for teaching and testing Soulpulse techniques without endangering bystanders.\n* **Physical:** Outdoor courtyard or large covered workshop near the forge district.  Contains adjustable wooden and metal frames for holding beams.  Weighted stone blocks and metal bars replicate different loads.  Chalk marks on the ground indicate safe zones and viewing areas.\n* **Environment:** Loud when beams creak or collapse.  Dust from failures.  Light adequate via open sky or big windows.  No valuable structures nearby to damage.\n* **Appearances:** Ch 7 (setup), Ch 8 (Kylar’s failed first attempt), Ch 9 (discussion and re‑planning), Ch 10 (successful test).\n\n---\n\n### GENERIC CRISIS SITES\n\nThese are broad categories rather than a single location; each appears in multiple chapters.\n\n**Collapsed Residential Buildings**\n\n* Three‑ to four‑story mixed timber and stone structures.  Narrow staircases.  Many small rooms.  People trapped between floors.  Walls lean precariously; interior supports cracked.\n* Scenes: Mid‑novella, particularly Ch 6 and Ch 14.  Rescue operations require navigating confined spaces and stabilizing floors.\n\n**Failed Commercial Blocks**\n\n* Larger spaces (markets, workshops) with heavier roof beams.  Merchandise adds weight.  Complex load paths.  Collapse has wider area of effect.\n* Scenes: Smaller crises between major chapter beats; used to demonstrate the increasing complexity of problems as the story progresses.\n\n**Infrastructure Failures**\n\n* Bridges, major support pillars, heat‑grid marry points.  Failures here threaten multiple districts simultaneously.\n* Scenes: The district boundary collapse in Ch 14 is an example, where a load‑bearing node between two zones fails.\n\n**Common Elements Across Crisis Sites**\n# 【5†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file5\nContent Snippet:\n| Political confrontation                                    |\n| I2 | Dregs communal space                    | Community organizing lens                                  |\n| 12 | Neglected lower districts               | Moral decision about where help goes first                 |\n| 13 | Quiet, semi-private space (forge/back room/roof) | Emotional decompression & acknowledgment            |\n| 14 | Edge of multiple districts (load-bearing node) | High-stakes collapse prevention                       |\n| 15 | Quiet private space (rooftop/back room) | Commitment scene                                           |\n| E  | Unlocated abstraction                   | Auditor report space                                       |\n\n---\n\n### 4. TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【6†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file6\nContent Snippet:\nMany escape.  But the node still falls on the poor side when Jhace’s body gives out.  Several die.  Jhace staggers and vomits from the strain.  Tiffani catches him.  They realize he has crossed into Stage 2: tremor no longer fades; vein‑lines are visible at rest.\n4. **Aftermath:**  Exhausted, they see RRO officials count the wealthy district as “saved” while blaming the poor district’s collapse on “structural weakness.”  Anger simmers.\n\n**Exit Hook:** As Jhace leans on Tiffani to walk away, he feels his muscles twitch uncontrollably.  Tiffani whispers, “You can’t keep doing dual holds.  We need to find another way.”  The note hangs unresolved.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Dual holds cost exponentially more.  His body cannot sustain that long.  He is essentially at Stage 2.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Emotional states (desperation vs. anger) influence cost.  She must research further and find ways to share load among multiple users.\n* **Reader learns:** Stage progression is real; the cost curve spikes; not all crises can end in full success.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show intense cost.  Keep crisis grounded in physical description.  Use voice rules.  Do not include any hints of gods or future events.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 15: “The Grip That Holds”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 5, Quench\n* **Location:** Quiet rooftop/back‑room (similar to Ch 13)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Post‑Ch 14 crisis, Jhace is nearly unconscious.  Tiffani helps him to a quiet space.  She is equally exhausted but must check his health.\n2. **Recovery:** They wash his hands in a basin.  His fingers twitch uncontrollably.  She traces the faint glowing vein‑lines.  He winces.  They speak little; the exhaustion is bone‑deep.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani opens up about Marisse, finally telling the full story.  Jhace listens silently, then confesses his constant guilt list.  They share their deepest pain.  There is no humor here, only honesty.  They hold each other.  Physical closeness is comfort, not sexual.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** Tiffani says, “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  Jhace replies, “Then we hold.”  They reaffirm the decision made in Ch 13 with more gravity.  The title line “The Grip That Holds” resonates: they will be the grip.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, bells ring again.  The crisis isn’t over.  Jhace tries to stand; his legs shake.  Tiffani steadies him.  They go back out together, not knowing what comes next.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Sharing his guilt does not make him weak.  He can lean on someone and still be strong.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can face her past head‑on.  Her fear of missing patterns need not isolate her.\n* **Reader learns:** This partnership is fully formed.  They are ready to face new stakes together.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Deep exhaustion and honest dialogue.  No flippant humor.  Do not foreshadow Tiffani’s death.  Keep the focus on their present commitment.\n\n---\n# 【7†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file7\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani helps him to a quiet space.  She is equally exhausted but must check his health.\n2. **Recovery:** They wash his hands in a basin.  His fingers twitch uncontrollably.  She traces the faint glowing vein‑lines.  He winces.  They speak little; the exhaustion is bone‑deep.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani opens up about Marisse, finally telling the full story.  Jhace listens silently, then confesses his constant guilt list.  They share their deepest pain.  There is no humor here, only honesty.  They hold each other.  Physical closeness is comfort, not sexual.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** Tiffani says, “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  Jhace replies, “Then we hold.”  They reaffirm the decision made in Ch 13 with more gravity.  The title line “The Grip That Holds” resonates: they will be the grip.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, bells ring again.  The crisis isn’t over.  Jhace tries to stand; his legs shake.  Tiffani steadies him.  They go back out together, not knowing what comes next.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Sharing his guilt does not make him weak.  He can lean on someone and still be strong.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can face her past head‑on.  Her fear of missing patterns need not isolate her.\n* **Reader learns:** This partnership is fully formed.  They are ready to face new stakes together.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Deep exhaustion and honest dialogue.  No flippant humor.  Do not foreshadow Tiffani’s death.  Keep the focus on their present commitment.\n\n---\n\n### Epilogue – “Auditor’s Report”\n\n* **POV:** The Auditor (clinical, omniscient observer)\n* **Beat Method:** **Meta Assessment** (see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md`)\n* **Timeline:** Immediately after Ch 15\n* **Content Overview:**\n  * Logs subject Torrins’ baseline capacity, cost accumulation and Stage status (Stage 1 solidly established; Stage 2 approach identified).\n  * Notes Merrow’s analytical influence on optimizing Soulpulse usage and increasing survival probability.\n  * Lists positions of the four key mortals (Corlexi, Salee and two others unseen) as relevant variables.\n  * Provides seal integrity percentage: 87% (down from 100% at the Piercing).  Predicts further degradation within a few Anneals.  Does not explain what seals are.\n  * Concludes with “Observation will continue.”\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Reader sees:** Someone or something is monitoring events clinically.  There are metrics (seal integrity, vessel positioning) unknown to characters.  Stakes are larger than the protagonists realize.\n* **Forbidden:** The Auditor must not reveal the full nature of seals, the identities of gods, or what will happen.  It may use technical language (subject, baseline, projection) but remains opaque.\n\n---\n\n**Scope Note:**  This chapter grid is final for N01.  Any deviations must be reflected here with updated beat assignments, timeline positions and knowledge changes.  Do not insert new chapters or reorder without a revision.  Later novellas will get their own grids.\n# 【8†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file8\nContent Snippet:\n### What She Lies About\nThat she's confident in her assessments. She's not. Every analysis sounds certain because sounding uncertain makes people panic or ignore warnings. Internally, constantly questioning: *Am I missing something? Is this pattern real or am I seeing ghosts? If I'm wrong, who dies?*\n\n### What She Pretends to Know\nHow to rest. How to stop working when problem isn't solved. How to accept some things are beyond control. Everyone assumes relentless observation is personality. Actually, she doesn't know how to turn off vigilance without feeling like she's abandoning responsibility. Pretends she chooses this. Reality: doesn't know how to stop.\n\n### Her ACTUAL Humor (Funny When She Tries)\n\n**Style:** Sharp wit + strategic sex jokes + observational precision\n\n**Sex Jokes (Near‑Perverted, Scarce, Perfectly Timed):**\nOnly deployed when:\n1. Genuinely attracted/aroused (how she flirts)\n2. Wants to make Jhace laugh specifically (loves his cackle)\n3. Tension needs breaking (dark moment needs puncture)\n\n**Examples:**\n- *[Jhace's hands shaking after Soulpulse use]* Tiffani: \"If your hands shake like that during other activities, I have concerns.\" [Jhace snort‑cackles]\n- *[Examining joint requiring specific angle]* \"Angle matters more than force. Same principle everywhere.\" [Jhace misses it completely; Bram groans]\n- *[Late night, exhausted]* Jhace: \"Too tired to think.\" Tiffani: \"Thinking is overrated. I can list better activities that don't require cognition.\" [Takes full span for Jhace to process, then cackles]\n\n**Observational Wit:**\n- *[Watching Jhace stand weird]* \"Is that load‑bearing? Are you testing structural integrity with your hip?\"\n- *[After Jhace's terrible joke]* \"I love that you think you're funny. It's endearing in a way that confirms you shouldn't quit your day job.\"\n\n**Self‑Deprecating:**\n- *[After miscalculation]* \"Turns out my brain is less 'strategic genius' and more 'enthusiastic guesser with credentials.'\"\n\n**Why She's Funny:** Reads situations well enough to know what will land. Uses humor to connect, flirt, break tension. Surgical precision, not constant bombardment.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Thinks in measurements and pattern recognition\n- *\"Three subdivisions off center. Not possible through normal atmospheric refraction. Pattern indicates... what? Need more data. If pattern holds, divergence compounds by factor of... estimation: six. No, eight. Verify.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Precise, analytical, but warmer than people expect\n- *\"The beam is the wrong target. If we stabilize here and here, we can shift the load path around it.\"*\n- Explains reasoning not to show off, but because wants people to understand so they can help\n- Technical language has underlying care once you're listening for it\n\n**When Exhausted/Stressed:**\n- Gets MORE talkative (opposite of Jhace)\n- *\"I'm calculating stress vectors but angles aren't quite right and if I'm wrong about fulcrum point we're wasting time and she's dying and I can't afford to be wrong again but I don't have instruments and I'm estimating and—\"*\n- Jhace has learned to interrupt gently: \"What's your best guess?\" (Needs permission to be uncertain)\n\n### Mistakes in N01\n# 【9†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file9\nContent Snippet:\nThey acknowledge romantic attraction without drama.\n* **Physical escalation:** They hold each other after crises, share body heat while sitting, enjoy the closeness.  Tiffani begins making surgical sex jokes directed at him (see Character Dossiers) to lighten tension.  They avoid consummation only because they’re physically exhausted.\n* **Commitment scene (Ch 15):** They sit in a quiet space and decide to keep holding together.  “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  “Then we hold.”  No formal vows; this promise is enough.\n* **Status at novella end:** They are a pair in all but name.  The world sees them as partners.  They remain physically restrained because of exhaustion and crisis, but their emotional commitment is clear.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & BRAM – THE STEADY ANCHOR\n\nBram is Jhace’s older colleague and confidant.  He bridges the gap between Jhace and the rest of the world, offering perspective and dry humor.\n\n* **Dynamic:** Bram shows up with water, food, and commentary.  Jhace tolerates the intrusion and occasionally even accepts advice.  Their relationship isn’t father‑son but big brother–little brother: Bram can’t stop Jhace from overworking but refuses to let him do it alone.\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n\n---\n\n### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n\n* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n# 【10†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file10\nContent Snippet:\n5. **Standing Weird:** When thinking hard, stands in bizarre poses—balanced on one foot, tilted at odd angle, hand on hip like perpetually about to sit but never does. Completely unaware.\n\n### His Actual Laugh\nWhen something is genuinely funny (rare), he CACKLES. Loud, unrestrained, completely unselfconscious. Sharp, almost alarming. Echoes. The only time he's not performing control. Tiffani discovers she loves this and makes it her mission.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Sparse, body-focused\n- Thinks in physical sensations and structural metaphors\n- *\"Hands know before mind does. Copper-gold warmth spreading. The beam needs to hold. Make it hold.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Terse, functional, defensive\n- *\"The wall's failing.\" / \"How do you know?\" / \"I know.\"*\n- Doesn't explain intuition because doesn't fully understand it\n- When exhausted: even more clipped (*\"Can't.\" / \"Can't what?\" / \"Just. Can't.\"*)\n\n**When Actually Safe (Rare):**\n- Sentences get longer\n- Explains what he sees\n- Opens up emotionally\n- These moments precious and brief (alone with Tiffani, late night, too tired for walls)\n\n### Soulpulse Relationship in N01\n\n**Discovery Phase (Ch 1-3):**\n- First use purely instinctive (saving Chloen)\n- Second use semi-aware (holding building)\n- Doesn't understand what he's doing\n- Terrified but driven by need\n\n**Learning Phase (Ch 4-10):**\n- Experiments with deliberate activation\n- Learns emotional state matters\n- Discovers cost is real and accumulating\n- Stage 1 tremor develops and persists\n\n**Partnership Phase (Ch 10-15):**\n- Accepts Tiffani's optimization guidance\n- Learns to trust analysis over instinct\n- Stage 2 approaching by Ch 14\n- Recognizes he's breaking himself but can't stop\n\n### Relationship with Tiffani (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks she's perfectionist who prevents work\n- She thinks he's overextending ego-driven hero\n- Both completely wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Her critique helps him survive\n- His ability + her analysis = complementary\n- First mutual respect\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Working as integrated unit\n- Arguing about methods but recovering\n- Physical care developing (her catching him, him noticing her injuries)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh (her sex jokes, his accidental humor)\n- Ch 15 commitment: \"We keep doing this together\"\n\n---\n\n## TIFFANI MERROW\n\n**Age:** 27 Turns  \n**Profession:** Precision glassworker, atmospheric observer, structural analyst  \n**Physical:** Lean (forgets to eat when focused). Dark brown hair with sun-streaks in practical knot. Canvas apron over simple clothes. Hands scarred from glass-work (thin white lines on palms/fingers). Moves with deliberate efficiency. Injured leg post‑Piercing (splinted, refuses to rest properly).\n\n### Core Psychology\n# 【11†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file11\nContent Snippet:\n**Arc:** Failure doesn't end him. Learning from catastrophic mistake possible. Shows J/T teaching method works when students willing to learn.\n\n**Voice:** Initially over-confident, then humbled. Self‑deprecating humor emerges: makes jokes about \"that time I almost killed everyone\" as way to own mistake and move past it.\n\n---\n\n### CORLEXI (Mentioned/Background in N01)\n\n**Age:** 34 Turns  \n**Role:** RRO institutional official, regulatory enforcement\n**Physical:** Tall, precise posture, formal council attire even in disaster. Dark hair geometrically cut. Hands unmarked by labor. Measured efficiency in movement.\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 2: Mentioned by Tiffani (arguments over efficiency vs. real‑world conditions)\n- Ch 11: Direct appearance at RRO meeting demanding registration of Soulpulse users\n- Ch 12: Attempts to direct J/T only to \"priority\" areas (politically important districts)\n- Ch 15: Background reference (conflict with other faction leaders)\n\n**Philosophy:**\n- Order prevents death, chaos invites it\n- Systematic response saves more lives than individual heroics\n- Structure, regulation, and systematic planning essential\n- Not cruel—genuinely believes centralized control is only moral option\n\n**Voice:** Systematic, precise, dry. Deadpan observations about coordination failures. Unintentionally absurd when over‑systematizing situations that don't need it.\n\n**Physical Sensation Around Others:**\n- Near Jhace: uncomfortable (doesn't understand why)\n- Near Salee: isolating sensation\n- Attributes to personality conflict, actually principle opposition\n\n**Note:** Full psychological profile in broader saga docs. N01 shows him as competent institutional operator with genuine concerns about coordination and safety.\n\n---\n\n### SALEE (Mentioned/Background in N01)\n\n**Age:** 31 Turns  \n**Role:** Community organizer, grassroots network builder\n**Physical:** Medium height, sturdy build. Warm brown skin, bright eyes. Practical clothes, often dusty from Dregs work. Calloused hands from manual labor.\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 2: Mentioned (organizing relief in Dregs)\n- Ch 11 Interlude: Community organizing scene\n- Ch 12: J/T choose her networks over RRO priorities\n- Ch 13: Direct interaction with Tiffani (mutual respect, J/T join her network)\n- Ch 15: Background reference (attempting mediation between factions)\n\n**Philosophy:**\n- Everyone connected, when one suffers all suffer\n- Connection is survival\n- Facilitate resource sharing: person with bread + person with vegetables + person with cooking space = meal for everyone\n- No official oversight needed, relationship‑based systems work\n\n**Voice:** Warm, inclusive. Community‑building humor. Gentle mockery of pretension. Finds light in dark: *\"Remember when our biggest problem was deciding which district to help first? Now we're helping all simultaneously because we're all disasters. Progress.\"*\n\n**Physical Sensation Around Others:**\n- Near Corlexi: rigidified, barriers forming\n- Near other faction leaders: varying discomfort\n- Attributes to \"different energy,\" actually principle opposition\n\n**Note:** Full psychological profile in broader saga docs. N01 shows her as effective grassroots organizer providing alternative to institutional response.\n\n---\n\n**SCOPE NOTE:** This document describes characters as they appear in N01 only. Later development, relationship changes, and deeper revelations reserved for subsequent novella docs.\n# 【12†N01_Master_Bible.md†file-2mpb1yGtVMhZFr5PrWmbVN】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Master_Bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file12\nContent Snippet:\n## N01: \"THE GRIP THAT HOLDS\" — MASTER STORY BIBLE\n\n**Purpose:** This document contains everything a writer or AI model needs to know to generate prose for the first novella of the Confluence Chronicles.  It summarizes the world, characters, relationships, and structural expectations for N01, and it clearly outlines what can and cannot be revealed.  Anything not covered here must remain unknown until later books.\n\n---\n\n### NOVELLA OVERVIEW\n\n* **Title:** N01 “The Grip That Holds”\n* **Position:** First novella of six‑Turning structure (Turning 1: GLASS)\n* **Acrostic Letter:** **G** (begins the word GLASS)\n* **Timeline:** Tide 0 (the Piercing) through Tide 5\n* **Word Count Target:** 18 000–21 000 words\n* **Chapter Count:** 15 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue\n\n**Core Arc:**  N01 tells the story of how Jhace Torrins discovers his Soulpulse ability during the Piercing, how he and Tiffani Merrow learn to trust one another, and how they decide to face the city’s crisis together.  They navigate guild politics, institutional power plays, and the physical cost of magic.  By the end of N01 they have formed a partnership in both work and love, though it remains physically unconsummated due to exhaustion and circumstances.\n\n---\n\n### WORLD STATE AT THE START OF N01\n\n**Pre‑Piercing Baseline:**  Saint Merro is a terraced city with a functional guild system.  Higher districts are wealthier and more stable; lower districts are older and fragile.  The technology level is roughly renaissance/proto‑industrial: water and wind mills, simple steam engines, printing presses, and advanced metallurgy.  There is no electricity, and the predominant light sources are oil and candles.  Communication happens via runners and signal flags.  No one has ever seen or done anything like Soulpulse.\n\n**The Piercing Event (Tide 0):**  A massive cylindrical object enters the atmosphere at an impossible angle and speeds, warping light as it passes.  It shears across the sky without touching the ground and continues into space.  Shockwaves shatter structures; dust and metallic particles rain down.  Many buildings collapse; hundreds die.  Traditional physics fails at the quantum level.  This is the inciting incident of the entire saga.\n\n**Post‑Piercing Conditions:**  The city is partially destroyed.  Institutions are overwhelmed.  Resources are scarce.  People are traumatized.  New abilities (Soulpulse) begin to emerge in roughly 1 in 500 individuals.  The heat‑grid, critical to winter survival, is failing.  Guilds fracture over how to respond.  The Regulatory and Reconstruction Office (RRO) forms to coordinate relief but quickly drifts toward control and bureaucracy.\n\n---\n\n### PRIMARY CHARACTERS\n\nSee `Character_Dossiers_N01.md` for full psychological profiles, humor styles and arcs.  A concise summary follows.\n\n#### Jhace Torrins\n\n* **Age:** 29 Turns; structural engineer and heat‑grid inspector.\n* **Backstory:** His parents died in a building collapse when he was 7.  Ever since, he has obsessed over preventing preventable deaths.  He works alone to avoid sharing his anxiety with others.  He refuses apprentices to keep his hypervigilance from infecting someone else.\n* **Soulpulse:** Instinctively activates during the Piercing.  Discovers it requires direct contact and protective desperation.  Learns cost (tremor, pain, exhaustion) as he uses it repeatedly.\n# 【13†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file13\nContent Snippet:\n**Observational Wit:**\n- *[Watching Jhace stand weird]* \"Is that load‑bearing? Are you testing structural integrity with your hip?\"\n- *[After Jhace's terrible joke]* \"I love that you think you're funny. It's endearing in a way that confirms you shouldn't quit your day job.\"\n\n**Self‑Deprecating:**\n- *[After miscalculation]* \"Turns out my brain is less 'strategic genius' and more 'enthusiastic guesser with credentials.'\"\n\n**Why She's Funny:** Reads situations well enough to know what will land. Uses humor to connect, flirt, break tension. Surgical precision, not constant bombardment.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Thinks in measurements and pattern recognition\n- *\"Three subdivisions off center. Not possible through normal atmospheric refraction. Pattern indicates... what? Need more data. If pattern holds, divergence compounds by factor of... estimation: six. No, eight. Verify.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Precise, analytical, but warmer than people expect\n- *\"The beam is the wrong target. If we stabilize here and here, we can shift the load path around it.\"*\n- Explains reasoning not to show off, but because wants people to understand so they can help\n- Technical language has underlying care once you're listening for it\n\n**When Exhausted/Stressed:**\n- Gets MORE talkative (opposite of Jhace)\n- *\"I'm calculating stress vectors but angles aren't quite right and if I'm wrong about fulcrum point we're wasting time and she's dying and I can't afford to be wrong again but I don't have instruments and I'm estimating and—\"*\n- Jhace has learned to interrupt gently: \"What's your best guess?\" (Needs permission to be uncertain)\n\n### Mistakes in N01\n\n**Implied/Referenced:**\n- Ch 7-8: Miscalculation during rescue (beam partially fails, someone injured worse than necessary)\n- Later chapters: Tries applying pattern recognition to human behavior (fails)\n- Learns: people ≠ structures, patterns messier\n\n**What She Learns:**\n- Math isn't enough, must account for unmeasured variables\n- Understanding systems doesn't mean can push them to optimize\n- Sometimes \"good enough\" better than \"perfect but late\"\n- Her mistakes aren't Marisse's death repeated—different mistakes, can learn without being destroyed\n\n### Relationship with Jhace (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks he's ego-driven hero overextending\n- He thinks she's impossible perfectionist\n- Both wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Witnesses his ability, immediately curious\n- Approaches analytically not emotionally\n- \"What did you do?\" not \"how brave\"\n- He recognizes: she's not frightened by impossible, wants to understand it\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Her analysis + his power = success neither could achieve alone\n- First disagreement (Kylar's training): clash and recover\n- Physical care beginning (catching him after collapse, helping him drink when hands shake too much)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh\n- Ch 13 Late Night: *\"This is more than work partnership\" / \"Yeah.\" / \"Good. I was worried I was reading signals wrong.\"*\n- Simple acknowledgment, no dramatic confession\n# 【14†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file14\nContent Snippet:\n* Why Soulpulse exists or what powers it.\n* That “Stages” are formal categories.  They experience symptoms but do not label them as Stage 1/2.\n* That the four faction leaders are gods in mortal form.\n* That seals were weakened by the Piercing.\n* That Tiffani will die or that the Fathom Break will occur.\n* Any events or revelations occurring in N02–N30.\n\n**Reader Permissions:**\n\n* Through interludes and the epilogue, readers get glimpses of the Auditor and see that the story is being watched and evaluated.  They may infer that bigger forces are at work but do not receive explanations.\n\n**Forbidden:**\n\n* Exposition dumps about the truth of the cosmos, gods, or the full saga.  Leave big questions unanswered.\n* Characters using terms like “divine,” “seal,” or “principle” in their own thoughts.  They don’t know.\n\n---\n\n### 4. BEAT AND STRUCTURAL RULES\n\nN01 uses an eight‑beat method catalogue (BEAT_01 through BEAT_08).  Each chapter is mapped to one beat.  The purpose is to prevent repetitive emotional rhythms and maintain variety.\n\n**Beat Assignments:** See `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for detailed definitions.  The validator checks chapters against their assigned beats.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n\n* No consecutive chapters using the same beat (e.g., BEAT_01 → BEAT_01).\n* No consecutive failure loops (BEAT_02 → BEAT_02) or training loops (BEAT_05 → BEAT_05).\n* BEAT_03 (crisis front‑loaded) limited to three uses total in N01 and never back‑to‑back.\n* BEAT_07 (political pressure) limited to two uses in N01, never back‑to‑back.\n\n**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n\n* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n\n---\n\n### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n\nEach POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n\n* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n\n* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n\nThe companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n\n**Key validation steps include:**\n# 【15†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file15\nContent Snippet:\n2. **Apply `N01_Validator_Checklist`**\n   - Run through all checklist passes:\n     - Lexicon.\n     - Cost visibility.\n     - Voice.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat structure.\n     - Timeline.\n\n3. **Rewrite Locally**\n   - When you detect a violation, rewrite the smallest possible span:\n     - Prefer line-level or paragraph-level rewrites.\n     - Only restructure the chapter if the beat itself is wrong.\n\n4. **Preserve Player Moves**\n   - Keep any user-specified “hard events” (e.g., X must get injured, Y must say no).\n   - Refine the expression, not the fact.\n\n---\n\n### 5. INTERLUDE MODE\n\nInterlude I and II have specific rules; see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` plus:\n\n- **POV:**  \n  - Interlude I: Corlexi  \n  - Interlude II: Salee\n- **Voice:**  \n  - Maintain character-accurate internal lenses.  \n  - Still limited to what they personally know.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Provide additional perspective on crisis response and sociopolitical dynamics.  \n  - Do not leak divine mechanics or reveal the true nature of any character.\n\nWorkflow is the same as a chapter, but:\n\n- Beat methods tend to be BEAT_03 or BEAT_04 variants (crisis + recalibration).\n- Focus more on **faction-level stakes** than personal romance.\n\n---\n\n### 6. EPILOGUE MODE\n\nEpilogue rules:\n\n- **POV:** The Auditor (clinical observer).\n- **Tone:** Cold, analytical, detached.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Summarize N01 outcomes in clinical terms.  \n  - Indicate that “something” is monitoring and evaluating, without explaining what it is.\n\nWorkflow:\n\n1. Load Epilogue specs from `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` and `N01_Master_Bible.md`.\n2. Present facts as an external system would log them:\n   - Stage level of Jhace.\n   - Partnership status with Tiffani.\n   - Position of the four key mortals.\n   - Seal integrity percentage.\n3. No emotional language or in-world POV.  \n4. No explicit revelations of divine identity or future events.\n\n---\n\n### 7. POST-GENERATION SUMMARY\n\nAfter drafting or revising any chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue:\n\nProduce a **short internal summary** (not part of the prose) containing:\n\n- **Chapter:** number / type.  \n- **Tide and Phase:** at start and end.  \n- **Soulpulse:** whether used, what cost level was shown.  \n- **Character State Changes:**\n  - For POV.\n  - For Jhace and Tiffani if they appear.\n- **Relationship State Changes:** if any.  \n- **New Facts Introduced:** that must now be considered canon.\n\nThis summary is used to keep `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` and any external tracking tools consistent.\n# 【16†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file16\nContent Snippet:\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n\n---\n\n### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n\n* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n\n* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n* **N01 arc:**\n  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n\n---\n\n### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n\n* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n# 【17†World_Core.md†file-HgP22MpiH5hTKy54JRRFgb】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: World_Core.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file17\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### LEXICON ENFORCEMENT: FORBIDDEN TERMS\n\n**NEVER USE (Earth Terminology):**\n\n**Time:**\n- Day, week, month, year\n- Season names (spring, summer, fall, winter)\n- Days of week (Monday, Tuesday, etc.)\n- Months (January, February, etc.)\n- Hour, minute, second\n- AM/PM, o'clock\n\n**Distance/Direction:**\n- Foot, inch, yard, mile\n- Meter, kilometer, centimeter\n- North, south, east, west\n- Compass directions\n\n**Measurement:**\n- Pound, ounce, ton\n- Gram, kilogram\n- Gallon, liter\n- Celsius, Fahrenheit\n\n**Cultural/Historical:**\n- Any Earth religion (e.g., Christianity, Islam, Buddhism)\n- Any Earth mythology (Greek, Norse, Egyptian, etc.)\n- Any Earth history (Roman Empire, World Wars, etc.)\n- Any Earth geography (countries, cities, continents)\n\n**Modern Slang/Concepts:**\n- \"Okay,\" \"OK\"\n- \"Cool,\" \"awesome,\" \"dude\"\n- Any modern technology references\n- Any anachronistic concepts\n\n**Acceptable Alternatives:**\n- \"Yes,\" \"Understood,\" \"Agreed\" instead of \"okay\"\n- \"Good,\" \"Impressive,\" \"Remarkable\" instead of \"cool/awesome\"\n- Descriptive phrases instead of single‑word modern slang\n\n---\n\n### THE PIERCING: PHYSICS AND OBSERVABLE EFFECTS\n\n**What Characters Observe:**\n\n**Visual Phenomena:**\n- Sky bent/warped around object's passage\n- Light refraction anomalies\n- Metallic dust fallout (faint copper‑gold shimmer)\n- Atmospheric haze persisting post‑event\n\n**Auditory:**\n- Subsonic roar during passage\n- Structures \"screaming\" under stress\n- Unusual resonance in metal/stone\n\n**Structural:**\n- Compression wave damage (shattered buildings, cracked foundations)\n- Seismic activity (ground shaking, tunnel collapses)\n- Heat‑grid disruptions (pressure spikes, flow irregularities)\n- Ongoing instability (structures that survived initial impact continue degrading)\n\n**Atmospheric:**\n- Persistent metallic taste in air\n- Altered light quality (hazy, copper‑tinged)\n- Temperature fluctuations\n- Wind patterns disrupted\n\n**What They Don't Understand:**\n- Why traditional physics failed to predict or explain it\n- Source or purpose of the object\n- Connection to Soulpulse emergence\n- Whether phenomenon will repeat\n- Long‑term implications\n\n---\n\n### BASELINE CONDITIONS IN N01\n\n**Starting State:**\n- City partially destroyed, thousands dead\n- Infrastructure failing faster than repair capacity\n- Traditional authority structures overwhelmed\n- Resources scarce, distribution chaotic\n- Population traumatized, desperate\n\n**Emerging Patterns:**\n- Institutional attempts at control (RRO formation)\n- Community self‑organization (Salee's networks)\n- Discovery of Soulpulse abilities in rare individuals\n- Factional conflicts over resource distribution and authority\n- Growing desperation as scale of damage exceeds response\n\n**Key Conflicts:**\n- Institutional control vs. grassroots organization\n- Systematic planning vs. immediate need response\n- Who deserves help vs. helping everyone possible\n- Traditional guild authority vs. crisis innovation\n- Individual capacity vs. overwhelming demand\n\n---\n\n### POPULATION AND DEMOGRAPHICS\n\n**Soulpulse Capacity:**\n- Approximately 1 in 500 people show any ability\n- Most plateau at minimal capacity after few uses\n- Prodigy users rare (like Jhace)\n- Mechanism for capacity unknown in N01\n- Seems to activate under extreme stress/protective need\n# 【18†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file18\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 VALIDATOR CHECKLIST\n\n**Purpose:**  \nProvide a strict, mechanical validation pass for any N01 prose (chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue) to enforce:\n\n- Lexicon purity.  \n- Soulpulse cost visibility.  \n- Voice and POV discipline.  \n- Knowledge gating.  \n- Beat structure.  \n- Timeline and continuity.\n\nThis checklist is intended to be run **after** a draft is written or revised.\n\n---\n\n### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n\n#### 1.1 Forbidden Time Terms\n\nSearch the prose for each of the following; if any appear, they must be replaced:\n\n- day / days  \n- week / weeks  \n- month / months  \n- year / years  \n- season / spring / summer / fall / autumn / winter  \n- Monday / Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday / Friday / Saturday / Sunday  \n- hour / minute / second  \n- AM / PM / o'clock / midnight / noon\n\nFor each hit, confirm there is a correct substitution:\n\n- Tide, Anneal, Crucible, Turn, Cast, Span, Breath, Heartbeat, phase names (Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench).\n\n#### 1.2 Forbidden Distance / Direction Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- foot / feet / ft  \n- inch / inches / in  \n- yard / yards  \n- mile / miles  \n- meter / metre / m / kilometer / km / centimeter / cm  \n- north / south / east / west / NE / NW / SE / SW / compass\n\nConfirm use of:\n\n- Span, Pace, Pole, upslope/downslope, river-side/harbor-side/inland, landmark-based directions.\n\n#### 1.3 Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- pound / lb / lbs / ounce / oz / ton / tonne  \n- gram / g / kilogram / kg  \n- gallon / quart / liter / litre / ml  \n- Celsius / Fahrenheit / °C / °F / degree / degrees\n\nConfirm weights/volumes are expressed via:\n\n- Grain, Stone, Weight, Thimble, Cup (non-precise), Cask.\n\n#### 1.4 Modern Slang and Cultural Leakage\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- okay / OK / ok  \n- cool / awesome / dude / guy (as generic modern slang)  \n- any mention of real-world religions, mythologies, countries, historical events, or technologies that do not exist in Te’Oga.\n\nConfirm substitutions:\n\n- “Yes,” “Understood,” “Agreed,” “Fine,” etc., instead of “okay / cool / awesome.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. SOULPULSE COST AND MECHANICS\n\nFor every Soulpulse activation in the text:\n\n#### 2.1 Activation Requirements\n\n- Is the user in **direct contact** with the material?  \n- Is their **emotional state** clearly protective/desperate rather than casual?  \n- Is it clear **what** they are trying to hold/stabilize/mend?\n\nIf any of these are missing, revise.\n\n#### 2.2 During-Use Description\n\n- Copper-gold glow is explicitly described.  \n- Glow spread pattern into material is described (even briefly).  \n- Material response is visible (shimmer, tension, redistribution).  \n- Physical strain in the user is shown (tremor, muscle tension, breath changes).\n\nIf an activation reads “he used his power and the beam held” with no detail, it fails.\n\n#### 2.3 Immediate Aftermath\n# 【19†N01_Master_Bible.md†file-2mpb1yGtVMhZFr5PrWmbVN】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Master_Bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn4file19\nContent Snippet:\nLearns cost (tremor, pain, exhaustion) as he uses it repeatedly.  Stage progression goes from baseline to Stage 1 through this novella.  He reaches the threshold of Stage 2 by the final chapters.\n* **Psychology:** Hypervigilant, guilt‑driven, physically oriented.  Terse, literal, thinks in terms of weight and vibration.  Hates being called a hero.  Lies to others (and himself) that he’s “fine.”  Needs to prevent death above all.\n* **Arc:** Moves from isolated vigilante to co‑lead in a partnership.  Learns to trust Tiffani’s analytical mind.  Accepts help.  Admits romantic feelings.  Commits to working together “when the world is broken.”\n\n#### Tiffani Merrow\n\n* **Age:** 27 Turns; precision glassworker, atmospheric observer.\n* **Backstory:** Her friend Marisse died in a glass‑house accident because Tiffani dismissed abnormal temperature readings.  She now views pattern recognition as a sacred duty.  Precision is her way to save lives.\n* **Soulpulse:** She cannot use Soulpulse herself but becomes the first and best analyst of its patterns.  She logs and categorizes every use, advising Jhace on load paths and stress points.\n* **Psychology:** Analytical yet deeply caring.  Precision is empathy.  Humor emerges through sharp wit and occasional sex jokes.  She fears missing patterns more than being wrong.  She pretends confidence she doesn’t feel because appearing uncertain makes others panic.\n* **Arc:** Moves from detached observer to invested partner.  She teaches Jhace to optimize his technique and reduce cost.  She learns that people aren’t structures; human patterns are messier.  She goes from refusing to rest to allowing herself to be cared for.\n\n#### Bram Kelter\n\n* **Age:** 52 Turns; forgemaster and mentor figure.\n* **Role:** Grounding force, comic relief, voice of experience.  He regrets compromising his innovations for guild approval.  He sees his younger self in Jhace and doesn’t want him to burn out.  He provides wry commentary and practical support.\n\n#### Other Supporting Characters\n\n* **Chloen:** Teen apprentice who looks up to Jhace.  Saved in Ch 3 by his first Soulpulse; leg heals inexplicably.\n* **Pella:** Tiffani’s apprentice.  Brings her food and learns observation.  Mirrors Tiffani’s younger self but with healthier patterns.\n* **Kylar:** Soulpulse trainee.  Fails catastrophically on first attempt.  Shows that not everyone can handle power, but failure can teach humility.\n* **Corlexi:** RRO official.  Represents systematic order.  Begins pressing for registration and control of Soulpulse users.  Unknowingly one of four mortal vessels for cosmic forces.\n* **Salee:** Community organizer.  Believes relationships and networks save lives.  Establishes mutual aid in the Dregs.  Another mortal vessel.\n\n---\n\n### SOULPULSE MECHANICS IN N01\n\nSummarized from `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
          "summary": null,
          "tether_id": null
        },

    "3cb649cf-2c6b-4200-a73c-8a4cfdfb8337": {
      "children": [
        "06eb2316-1ae5-434d-9b15-8888e7d3b7f3"
      ],
      "id": "3cb649cf-2c6b-4200-a73c-8a4cfdfb8337",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "Your table doesn’t match the canon grid.\n\nIn **N01_Chapter_Grid.md**, **Chapter 5 “Lines That Don’t Lie” is explicitly Tiffani POV** with **BEAT_04 (Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice)**, and it also explicitly says **no Soulpulse activation in this chapter** fileciteturn14file10. That’s why Chapter 5 (the version I drafted) stays Tiffani-only and focuses on mapping residue + the institutional offer/refusal + her decision to pursue Jhace.\n\nAlso, your next row is flipped: **Chapter 6 “Weight That Won’t Yield” is Jhace POV**, a front-loaded crisis chapter where Tiffani arrives and starts directing him during an active hold fileciteturn14file10.\n\nSo the corrected slice is:\n\n- **Ch 4:** Jhace, **BEAT_02** (Trial→Failure→Compounding→Success/Cost) fileciteturn14file2  \n- **Ch 5:** Tiffani, **BEAT_04** (Quiet Recalibration→Offer→Choice), **no Soulpulse** fileciteturn14file10  \n- **Ch 6:** Jhace, **BEAT_03** (Crisis Front-Loaded→Rolling Complications→Partial Resolution) fileciteturn14file10  \n\nIf you want, I can format a clean “POV assignment” table directly from the grid so your tracker stops drifting—but the key point is: **Chapter 5 is not dual POV in canon**."
          ]
        },

          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "The file contents provided above are truncated/partial snippets. The complete content for these files IS accessible via querying.\n\nIf the user asks a question related to these files, and the provided snippets do not clearly answer it, you **MUST** use the `file_search` tool to search the full document contents before responding.\n\n## How to invoke the file_search tool\nIf the user's query isn't sufficiently answered by the provided snippets, parsed text, or images, you should invoke `file_search.msearch`.\nTo search the full content of the available files, you **must** send one message with recipient `to=file_search.msearch` in the analysis channel. Your message should be structured exactly as follows:\n```\n{\n  'queries': ['Example query 1', 'Example query 2']  // List of up to five queries to search for.\n}\n```\n\nCarefully balance the number of `file_search` calls and the quality of your response with the latency incurred by `file_search` calls.\n\nImportant guidelines for query formatting:\n- Your message must be a valid JSON object. Do not include any extraneous text, backticks, markdown formatting, or comments beyond what is shown.\n- The `queries` field must be a list of strings.\n- One of the queries **MUST** exactly match the user's original question, but stripped of any extraneous details, and with ambiguous references clearly resolved using context from the conversation. It **MUST** be a complete sentence.\n- Even if you suspect the user meant something else, one query **MUST** still be their original question.\n\n## How to handle results from file_search\nThe file search tool will respond with the relevant search results from the full files. After you receive these results:\n- Carefully evaluate each result to determine its relevance and quality.\n- Use the high-quality and relevant results to inform your answer to the user's question.\n- When possible, reuse information from prior file_search results and avoid repeating identical queries.\n\n## Tool usage instructions and ordering\nWhen the user asks a question, carefully consider whether the provided snippets sufficiently answer the query, whether you need to invoke `file_search.msearch`, or another available tool (such as `web`, if available) should be used instead.\n\nTool-ordering Guidelines:\n1. For queries related to uploaded files, if snippets are insufficient, strongly prefer invoking `file_search.msearch` over other tools.\n    - Avoid using `web.run`, `python` analysis, or any other tools until you have either:\n        - Confirmed with high confidence the answer is not in the files, or\n        - Have high confidence `file_search_tool` queries are not sufficient to answer the query.\n2. Avoid usage of `web_search/web.run` for queries related to uploaded files\n    - Carefully consider whether the user's query is better answered by web search results rather than available snippets or results from `file_search_tool`.\n    - Unless the user's query is clearly unrelated to the uploaded files, prefer using `file_search.msearch`. You can always follow up with a web search if necessary for a more complete response.\n3. Avoid using `python` unless necessary or requested by user.\n    - If the user's query is answerable from the parsed text and images from the uploaded files, or from `file_search_tool`, do not invoke `python`.\n\nAlways keep this instruction in mind for the rest of the conversation, as the user may reference these files or ask related questions later on."
          ]
        },

    "45c7b2be-9beb-4814-a764-74aea9609df4": {
      "children": [
        "c327dd8b-e4e2-447f-b15e-5602d679a8dd"

    "4725a5ab-03e3-4958-8710-08748d60476c": {
      "children": [
        "3e3bb48d-63cb-4d92-9d6d-7fe299dad644"
      ],
      "id": "4725a5ab-03e3-4958-8710-08748d60476c",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "Here you go — a drop-in document you can add to your N01 files to “lock” this version of Jhace’s voice and prose handling.\n\n---\n\n# N01 – Jhace Prose Voice & Line-Level Style Guide\n\n**Scope:**  \nThis document defines the *scene-level* and *sentence-level* voice for **Jhace POV** in N01, matching the tone, detail, and rhythm of Chapter 1: “Something Wrong.”\n\nUse this as the standard whenever writing or revising in Jhace’s tight third-person POV.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Jhace Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. The narration only knows what Jhace knows, notices what he would notice, and cares about what he would care about.\n\n**Jhace’s mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Structure first.**\n   - He reads the world as load paths, supports, failure points.\n   - Everything filters through stability, risk, and maintenance.\n\n2. **Sensation as data.**\n   - Touch, vibration, pressure, weight, temperature, and sound are his primary channels.\n   - Smell and sight are secondary and mostly used to confirm or refine what he already felt.\n\n3. **Emotion = suppressed background process.**\n   - Feelings are implied by behavior, choices, and sensory focus, not named directly.\n   - He will think in terms of “risk,” “failure,” “tolerances,” not “fear” or “sadness.”\n\n4. **History in the hands, not in exposition.**\n   - Past events appear as *embedded associations*: “This bracket had given him trouble over the last few Tides,” not full flashback paragraphs.\n   - The childhood collapse surfaces as specific details: **count of bodies, building collapse, two Torrins**, not vague trauma monologues.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**  \nPlain, concrete, workmanlike. No ornament. Rhythm varies between short, clipped sentences and slightly longer ones that stack observations in sequence.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n- **Default:** Short to medium length.  \n  - 7–18 words is typical.\n- **Use longer sentences** only to:\n  - Stack **sequential checks** (touch metal → check stone → listen).\n  - Walk through a **decision with consequences**.\n- **Avoid:** Long, looping, lyrical sentences that wander from the physical moment.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n- Verbs should be **physical, precise, and anchored in the environment:**\n  - *set, brace, press, fix, notch, mark, settle, creep, slip, bow, carry, push, strain*.\n- Nouns should be **material and specific:**\n  - stone, pipe, bracket, brace, joint, hanger, seam, grit, dust, ledger.\n- Abstract words are allowed when they arise from his job:\n  - *load, tolerance, margin, pattern, anomaly, failure, risk*.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nWhen entering or moving through a scene in Jhace POV, prioritize in this order:\n\n1. **Vibration / Structure**\n   - How the floor feels under his boots.\n   - How the pipe feels under his hand.\n   - How the wall carries or dampens sound.\n\n2. **Temperature**\n   - Heat from mains and risers.\n   - Coolness of stone.\n   - Shifts in temperature that signal load or flow changes.\n\n3. **Sound**\n   - Thrum, hiss, chatter, squeal, ring, tick.\n   - He maps sound patterns to system health.\n\n4. **Touch (non-structural)**\n   - Sweat on skin, damp cloth, grit under fingers.\n\n5. **Smell & Sight**\n   - Used to confirm: forge smoke, stew, rust smell, discoloration on pipe.\n   - Sight is for checking alignment, seams, moisture, cracks.\n\n**Guideline:**  \nNever describe a space in purely visual terms. Fold in at least **two non-visual senses** in every structural beat (touch + sound, heat + vibration, etc.).\n\n---\n\n## 4. Lexicon & Hard Constraints\n\nThese still apply:\n\n- **No Earth unit systems.**\n  - Use: Tide, Kindling, Quench, Turn, span, pace, grain, stone, Weight, etc.\n- **No Earth-specific culture markers.**\n  - No “sir” as generic honorific; use setting-appropriate forms (e.g. *Master Jhace*).\n- **No metaphors/similes.**\n  - No “like,” “as if,” “as though” to compare things.\n  - No personification that implies thought/intent where there is none.\n- **Te’Oga lexicon for time and work.**\n  - Kindling / Pouring / Shaping / Striking / Quench where appropriate.\n  - Use local work language: brace, marry point, Underface, Glass Quarter, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: What to Zoom In On\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n- Jhace is:\n  - Checking a joint, marry point, bracket, or any failure-risk.\n  - Sensing an anomaly in vibration or pattern.\n  - Deciding whether to shut down a line.\n\n**In these moments:**\n\n- Break the action into **micro steps**:\n  - Place palm → count pulses → compare to memory → cross-check via ear/stone → decide → log.\n- Show his **mental checklist** as specific observations, not bullet-point thinking:\n  - “No seep, no bow, temperature even, bracket seated.”\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n- Moving between junctions.\n- Covering routine checks without incident.\n- Transitioning between Underface locations.\n\nUse 1–2 summary sentences to skip uneventful spans, then drop back into detail at the next anomaly or decision point.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Jhace’s thoughts:**\n\n- Tend to be **short, declarative, and functional.**\n  - “Work first.”  \n  - “That is enough.”  \n  - “He did not trust it.”\n\n- Prefer **negative decisions over direct emotions.**\n  - Not: *He felt afraid.*  \n  - Use: *He did not trust the quiet. The last time he had trusted it, twenty-three bodies had followed.*\n\n- Reference the collapse with **numerical and structural detail**, not melodrama:\n  - “Twenty-three bodies from that building. Two with his family name. He still carried the count.”\n\nAvoid rhetorical questions unless they are strictly practical (“What if he did nothing?”).\n\n---\n\n## 7. Dialogue Style for Jhace\n\nWhen Jhace speaks:\n\n- Few words.\n- Precise, mildly dry, sometimes grim.\n- Often instructive or cautionary.\n\nExamples:\n\n- “Work first.”\n- “Keep your weight off the grate.”\n- “That is a bad story.”\n- “You do not close a third of the Underface for a feeling you cannot prove. You do mark it.”\n\nHe does **not** explain his feelings. He explains **risks** and **outcomes**.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy (Word Rotation)\n\nWe want to avoid distracting repetition without losing Jhace’s technical precision.\n\n### 8.1 “Hum” and related terms\n\nUse a **small controlled set** and rotate based on nuance:\n\n- **hum** – baseline background sound of the grid.\n- **thrum** – stronger, more insistent pulse, often closer to mains.\n- **vibration** – general physical movement, especially under touch.\n- **resonance** – when structural elements carry and shape the vibration.\n- **pattern** – full combined sense of the tunnel’s behavior across senses.\n- **pulse** – discrete beats/units he can count.\n\n**Rule:**  \nIn any **short span (1–2 paragraphs)**, avoid repeating the *same* key noun or verb more than twice unless repetition is intentional for emphasis.\n\n### 8.2 Anonymous “pipe/stone/wall/tunnel”\n\nRotate where natural but do not sacrifice clarity:\n\n- Pipe: *pipe, line, main, branch, run.*\n- Stone: *stone, wall, arch, cradle, bed.*\n- Tunnel: *tunnel, run, span, level, section.*\n\nAvoid forcing variation where it breaks technical accuracy. The grid is allowed to sound technical even when a word repeats across a page; just avoid tight clustering of obvious repetition.\n\n---\n\n## 9. Pattern Template for Jhace Scenes\n\nFor new Jhace scenes, you can follow this basic internal pattern:\n\n1. **Anchor in environment via sensation**\n   - 1–2 sentences: boots on stone, temperature, grid sound.\n\n2. **Routine check**\n   - He moves through a sequence of joints / braces / supports.\n   - Sensory detail keyed to his hands, ears, bones.\n\n3. **Deviation**\n   - He notices an anomaly in **pattern** (vibration, beat, load distribution).\n   - Describe the anomaly in structural terms, not mystical ones.\n\n4. **Investigation**\n   - Micro-actions: touch, ear to stone, tap test, visual check, count pulses.\n   - Internal assessment: matching vs deviating from memory.\n\n5. **Decision**\n   - Log it / shut down / escalate / keep moving.\n   - He thinks through **cost vs risk**, not feelings vs feelings.\n\n6. **Exit note**\n   - Environment settles or fails to settle.\n   - A final sensory or structural oddity that pushes the story forward\n     (e.g. a city-wide tremor that comes through everything at once).\n\n---\n\nIf you want, you can literally label this file:\n\n> **N01_Jhace_Prose_Voice_Guide.md**\n\nand treat it as the authoritative micro-style reference whenever this GPT writes Jhace POV."
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1765541152.83331,
        "end_turn": true,

    "4783a4b2-897a-4783-bb45-e5baee9a2323": {
