        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\n\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\n\n-----\n\n## PURPOSE\n\nThis document translates between:\n\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\n\n**Rule of Precedence:**\n\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\n\n-----\n\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\n\n|N01 Local Beat|N01 Beat Name (Canon)                                           |Saga Beat ID|Saga Beat Name                                                                                                |\n|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n|**BEAT_01**   |Observation → Pattern Recognition                               |**Beat 10** |Observation → Pattern Recognition                                                                             |\n|**BEAT_02**   |Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost                    |**Beat 1**  |Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost                                                                  |\n|**BEAT_03**   |Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution|**Beat 3**  |External Threat → Narrow Escape                                                                               |\n|**BEAT_04**   |Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice                    |**Beat 16** |Investigation → Discovery → Complication *(primary mapping for discovery-driven choices)*                     |\n|**BEAT_05**   |Training Loop → Conflict → Reframe                              |**Beat 13** |Teaching Moment → Student Failure → Lesson Learned *(or Beat 20 if student-success weighted)*                 |\n|**BEAT_06**   |Debate → Third-Party Insight → Synthesis                        |**Beat 26** |Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate                                                        |\n|**BEAT_07**   |Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand                         |**Beat 26** |Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate *(institutional confrontation analog)*                 |\n|**BEAT_08**   |Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment                        |**Beat 17** |Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection Deepened *(or Beat 21 if reflection/purpose weighted, minimal intimacy)*|\n\n-----\n\n## SPECIAL CASE MAPPINGS\n\n### **BEAT_04 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)**\n\n- Use when: Character investigates, discovers truth, truth creates choice/complication\n- Example: Ch 8 (Jhace resists Tiffani’s data, investigates hold patterns, discovers efficiency truth)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 28 (Moral Compromise → Guilt → Justification)**\n\n- Use when: Offer requires ethical compromise, character feels moral weight, accepts or refuses with consequences\n- Example: If BEAT_04 chapter centers on moral pressure rather than investigation\n\n**Logging Guideline:** Default to Beat 16 unless moral compromise is central theme.\n\n-----\n\n### **BEAT_08 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)**\n\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes emotional vulnerability, sharing trauma, relationship deepening\n- Example: Ch 11 (trauma exchange, mutual need acknowledged)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)**\n\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes rest, reflection, renewed commitment with minimal vulnerability\n- Example: Ch 7 (data compilation), Ch 12 (writing priorities on forge wall)\n\n**Logging Guideline:** If intimacy/vulnerability present → Beat 17. If primarily rest/reflection → Beat 21.\n\n-----\n\n## N01 CHAPTER LOGGING (CURRENT STATUS)\n\n### **Locked Chapters (1-5):**\n\n|Chapter|Title               |N01 Local Beat|Saga Beat|Status  |\n|-------|--------------------|--------------|---------|--------|\n|1      |Something Wrong     |BEAT_01       |Beat 10  |🔒 LOCKED|\n|2      |Glass That Listens  |BEAT_01       |Beat 10  |🔒 LOCKED|\n|3      |The Sky Breaks      |BEAT_03       |Beat 3   |🔒 LOCKED|\n|4      |After the Shudder   |BEAT_02       |Beat 1   |🔒 LOCKED|\n|5      |Lines That Don’t Lie|BEAT_01       |Beat 10  |🔒 LOCKED|\n\n**Note:** Ch 1-2 use BEAT_01 consecutively (opening exception for parallel POV establishment). Ch 5 returns to BEAT_01 for systematic observation/pattern work.\n\n-----\n\n### **Remaining Chapters (6-12):**\n\n|Chapter|Title                  |N01 Local Beat|Saga Beat|Status  |\n|-------|-----------------------|--------------|---------|--------|\n|6      |Weight That Won’t Yield|BEAT_03       |Beat 3   |To Write|\n|7      |Quiet Calculations     |BEAT_08       |Beat 21  |To Write|\n|8      |Proof and Resistance   |BEAT_04       |Beat 16  |To Write|\n|9      |Ash and Claims         |BEAT_07       |Beat 26  |To Write|\n|10     |Near Collapse          |BEAT_03       |Beat 3   |To Write|\n|11     |Holding Together       |BEAT_08       |Beat 17  |To Write|\n|12     |Choosing the Weight    |BEAT_08       |Beat 21  |To Write|\n\n**Beat Usage Summary:**\n\n- BEAT_01 (Beat 10): Ch 1, 2, 5\n- BEAT_02 (Beat 1): Ch 4\n- BEAT_03 (Beat 3): Ch 3, 6, 10\n- BEAT_04 (Beat 16): Ch 8\n- BEAT_07 (Beat 26): Ch 9\n- BEAT_08 (Beat 17): Ch 11\n- BEAT_08 (Beat 21): Ch 7, 12\n\n-----\n\n## CHAPTER GRID LOGGING FORMAT (REQUIRED)\n\nEach chapter entry must list both local and saga beat IDs:\n\n**Template:**\n\n```\n### CHAPTER X: \"[Title]\"\n- **N01 Local Beat:** BEAT_0X ([N01 structure name])\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat Y ([Saga structure name])\n```\n\n**Example (Ch 6):**\n\n```\n### CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"\n- **N01 Local Beat:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n```\n\n-----\n\n## VALIDATOR ENFORCEMENT (N01 LOCAL ONLY)\n\n**N01 Validator checks:**\n\n- ✅ No consecutive identical beats (BEAT_03 → BEAT_03)\n- ✅ No rotation patterns (BEAT_01 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_03 → BEAT_01…)\n- ✅ Beat-specific constraints from N01 Beat Catalog\n\n**Opening Exception:**\nN01 permits BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 only for Ch 1–2 to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror. This is the sole consecutive-beat exception in N01.\n\n**Validator does NOT check:**\n\n- ❌ Saga-level beat sequences (handled by cross-novella tracker)\n- ❌ Beat frequency across N01-N30 (handled by saga audit)\n\n-----\n\n## SAGA-LEVEL TRACKING (CROSS-NOVELLA)\n\n**Saga Beat Audit tracks:**\n\n- ✅ Which Saga Beats used in which novellas\n- ✅ Forbidden follow-pairs (if Beat X → Beat Y in N01, cannot repeat in N02-N30)\n- ✅ Beat frequency across all 30 novellas\n- ✅ Minimum separation requirements for major beats (27, 29, 30)\n\n**Saga tracking uses:**\n\n- Beat IDs from “Saga Beat” column in chapter tables above\n- N01’s local beat constraints don’t apply to other novellas\n- Each novella can develop own local beat system mapped to saga repertoire\n\n-----\n\n## WHY TWO SYSTEMS COEXIST\n\n### **N01 Local System (8 beats):**\n\n- **Scope:** Within N01’s 12 chapters\n- **Purpose:** Prevent pattern formation inside one novella\n- **Enforcement:** N01 Validator, Constraint_Systems.md\n- **Granularity:** Fine-tuned for N01’s specific narrative needs\n\n### **Saga System (30+ beats):**\n\n- **Scope:** Across all 30 novellas\n- **Purpose:** Prevent pattern formation across entire saga\n- **Enforcement:** Beat Sequence Audit Tracker (N01-N30)\n- **Granularity:** Broad structural categories applicable saga-wide\n\n**These operate at different scales and complement each other without conflict.**\n\n-----\n\n## UPDATING THIS MAPPING\n\n**When N01 adds new local beats:**\n\n1. Add row to Core Mapping Table\n1. Identify closest Saga Beat analog\n1. Update chapter logging tables\n1. Add usage notes if context-dependent\n\n**When Saga adds new beats (Beat 31+):**\n\n1. Evaluate if any N01 local beats map more precisely to new beat\n1. Update mapping table if better analog exists\n1. Note in “Special Case Mappings” if context-dependent\n\n-----\n\n## CROSS-REFERENCE DOCUMENTS\n\n**N01 Local System:**\n\n- `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` - Defines BEAT_01-08 structures\n- `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` - Enforces local constraints\n- `Constraint_Systems.md` - N01 beat usage rules\n\n**Saga System:**\n\n- `02_Beat_Unpredictability_System.md` - Defines Beat 1-30 repertoire\n- `N01-N05_Beat_Sequence_Audit_Tracker.md` - Tracks saga-level usage\n- `[Future: N06-N30 Beat Tracking]` - Extends to full saga\n\n-----\n\n## VERSION CONTROL\n\n**Version:** 1.1 (Corrected)\n**Created:** [Current]\n**Updates:**\n\n- Added opening exception for BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 in Ch 1-2\n- Corrected Ch 4-5 beat assignments to match locked prose\n- Added Saga Beat IDs to all chapter entries\n\n**Purpose:** Enable dual-scale beat tracking without validator refactoring\n**Status:** Production-ready for Ch 6+ generation\n\n**Next Update:** After N01 completion, when N02 local beat system defined\n\n-----\n\n**END OF MAPPING LAYER**\n\n**This document preserves N01 production stability while integrating with saga-level unpredictability architecture.**"
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1765700844.531249,

          "result": "# 【0†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file0\nContent Snippet:\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n\n---\n\n### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n\n* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n\n* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n* **N01 arc:**\n  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n\n---\n\n### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n\n* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file1\nContent Snippet:\n5. **Partial Resolution:**  People evacuate.  The building doesn’t fully collapse.  Jhace collapses from the exertion.  His hands shake uncontrollably.  He blacks out.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace wakes to chaos and sees Tiffani standing over him with a look of shock and curiosity.  They exchange a brief, bewildered glance.  The world is different now.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** He can do something impossible when protecting someone.  It costs him physically (ache, tremor).  It feels like heat and vibration.\n* **Tiffani learns:** People can manipulate structures with copper‑gold light.  She has no explanation yet but she’s determined to find one.\n* **Reader learns:** The Piercing is world‑changing; Soulpulse exists; it is tied to desperation and contact; it leaves residue (glow, tremor, healing).  Chloen’s leg heals quickly.\n* **Hidden:** Why this power exists, what the Piercing was, any link to gods or divine principles.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Full crisis intensity.  Show cost: aching hands, tremor, exhaustion.  Use sensory language (dust, noise).  Keep voices distinct (Jhace terse, Tiffani more observational).  Do not name the ability yet; characters just describe what they see.\n\n---\n\n### Interlude I (After Chapter 6) – Corlexi “System vs Chaos”\n\n* See `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` for details.  This interlude shows the RRO council chamber through Corlexi’s eyes.  It contrasts institutional attempts at order with the ground‑level chaos experienced in the first six chapters.  It seeds the registration conflict.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n# 【2†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file2\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** The power is not controlled by will alone; it requires genuine protective desperation.  Repeated use increases tremor and pain.  It may not be sustainable.\n* **Tiffani learns:** The phenomenon leaves measurable changes.  She begins building a taxonomy of holds (load, duration, cost).  She sees his hands shaking and begins to suspect a cost system.\n* **Reader learns:** Cost exists, Stage 1 tremor begins.  The ability fails if used without the right emotional trigger.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show cost explicitly.  No naming of “Stage 1.”  Keep voices distinct.  Lexicon rules apply.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 5: “Lines That Don’t Lie”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_04 – Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal or Choice**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Pouring→Shaping\n* **Location:** Damaged street near Merrow shop, salvage area, temporary data space\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Tiffani surveys the ruins of her observatory and the surrounding district.  She begins marking where the unusual holds occurred.  She sketches structural fractures and notes copper residue.\n2. **Offer:** Guild representatives and an RRO messenger approach.  They want her to stop chasing anomalies and focus on profitable repairs or to join an official committee.  They offer stability and status.\n3. **Evaluation:** Tiffani thinks of Marisse’s death and of the glowing beam.  She decides that understanding the impossible phenomenon is more important than guild approval.  She politely refuses the offer to join the committee.\n4. **Quiet Recalibration:** Back in her temporary space, she compiles data: hold durations, loads, injuries.  She draws connections between his technique and structural design.  She resolves to speak to “the man with the glow.”\n# 【3†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file3\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani helps him to a quiet space.  She is equally exhausted but must check his health.\n2. **Recovery:** They wash his hands in a basin.  His fingers twitch uncontrollably.  She traces the faint glowing vein‑lines.  He winces.  They speak little; the exhaustion is bone‑deep.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani opens up about Marisse, finally telling the full story.  Jhace listens silently, then confesses his constant guilt list.  They share their deepest pain.  There is no humor here, only honesty.  They hold each other.  Physical closeness is comfort, not sexual.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** Tiffani says, “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  Jhace replies, “Then we hold.”  They reaffirm the decision made in Ch 13 with more gravity.  The title line “The Grip That Holds” resonates: they will be the grip.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, bells ring again.  The crisis isn’t over.  Jhace tries to stand; his legs shake.  Tiffani steadies him.  They go back out together, not knowing what comes next.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Sharing his guilt does not make him weak.  He can lean on someone and still be strong.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can face her past head‑on.  Her fear of missing patterns need not isolate her.\n* **Reader learns:** This partnership is fully formed.  They are ready to face new stakes together.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Deep exhaustion and honest dialogue.  No flippant humor.  Do not foreshadow Tiffani’s death.  Keep the focus on their present commitment.\n\n---\n\n### Epilogue – “Auditor’s Report”\n\n* **POV:** The Auditor (clinical, omniscient observer)\n* **Beat Method:** **Meta Assessment** (see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md`)\n* **Timeline:** Immediately after Ch 15\n* **Content Overview:**\n  * Logs subject Torrins’ baseline capacity, cost accumulation and Stage status (Stage 1 solidly established; Stage 2 approach identified).\n  * Notes Merrow’s analytical influence on optimizing Soulpulse usage and increasing survival probability.\n  * Lists positions of the four key mortals (Corlexi, Salee and two others unseen) as relevant variables.\n  * Provides seal integrity percentage: 87% (down from 100% at the Piercing).  Predicts further degradation within a few Anneals.  Does not explain what seals are.\n  * Concludes with “Observation will continue.”\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Reader sees:** Someone or something is monitoring events clinically.  There are metrics (seal integrity, vessel positioning) unknown to characters.  Stakes are larger than the protagonists realize.\n* **Forbidden:** The Auditor must not reveal the full nature of seals, the identities of gods, or what will happen.  It may use technical language (subject, baseline, projection) but remains opaque.\n\n---\n\n**Scope Note:**  This chapter grid is final for N01.  Any deviations must be reflected here with updated beat assignments, timeline positions and knowledge changes.  Do not insert new chapters or reorder without a revision.  Later novellas will get their own grids.\n# 【4†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file4\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 VALIDATOR CHECKLIST\n\n**Purpose:**  \nProvide a strict, mechanical validation pass for any N01 prose (chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue) to enforce:\n\n- Lexicon purity.  \n- Soulpulse cost visibility.  \n- Voice and POV discipline.  \n- Knowledge gating.  \n- Beat structure.  \n- Timeline and continuity.\n\nThis checklist is intended to be run **after** a draft is written or revised.\n\n---\n\n### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n\n#### 1.1 Forbidden Time Terms\n\nSearch the prose for each of the following; if any appear, they must be replaced:\n\n- day / days  \n- week / weeks  \n- month / months  \n- year / years  \n- season / spring / summer / fall / autumn / winter  \n- Monday / Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday / Friday / Saturday / Sunday  \n- hour / minute / second  \n- AM / PM / o'clock / midnight / noon\n\nFor each hit, confirm there is a correct substitution:\n\n- Tide, Anneal, Crucible, Turn, Cast, Span, Breath, Heartbeat, phase names (Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench).\n\n#### 1.2 Forbidden Distance / Direction Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- foot / feet / ft  \n- inch / inches / in  \n- yard / yards  \n- mile / miles  \n- meter / metre / m / kilometer / km / centimeter / cm  \n- north / south / east / west / NE / NW / SE / SW / compass\n\nConfirm use of:\n\n- Span, Pace, Pole, upslope/downslope, river-side/harbor-side/inland, landmark-based directions.\n\n#### 1.3 Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- pound / lb / lbs / ounce / oz / ton / tonne  \n- gram / g / kilogram / kg  \n- gallon / quart / liter / litre / ml  \n- Celsius / Fahrenheit / °C / °F / degree / degrees\n\nConfirm weights/volumes are expressed via:\n\n- Grain, Stone, Weight, Thimble, Cup (non-precise), Cask.\n\n#### 1.4 Modern Slang and Cultural Leakage\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- okay / OK / ok  \n- cool / awesome / dude / guy (as generic modern slang)  \n- any mention of real-world religions, mythologies, countries, historical events, or technologies that do not exist in Te’Oga.\n\nConfirm substitutions:\n\n- “Yes,” “Understood,” “Agreed,” “Fine,” etc., instead of “okay / cool / awesome.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. SOULPULSE COST AND MECHANICS\n\nFor every Soulpulse activation in the text:\n\n#### 2.1 Activation Requirements\n\n- Is the user in **direct contact** with the material?  \n- Is their **emotional state** clearly protective/desperate rather than casual?  \n- Is it clear **what** they are trying to hold/stabilize/mend?\n\nIf any of these are missing, revise.\n\n#### 2.2 During-Use Description\n\n- Copper-gold glow is explicitly described.  \n- Glow spread pattern into material is described (even briefly).  \n- Material response is visible (shimmer, tension, redistribution).  \n- Physical strain in the user is shown (tremor, muscle tension, breath changes).\n\nIf an activation reads “he used his power and the beam held” with no detail, it fails.\n\n#### 2.3 Immediate Aftermath\n# 【5†World_Core.md†file-HgP22MpiH5hTKy54JRRFgb】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: World_Core.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file5\nContent Snippet:\n**The Crown (Peak):**\n- Institutional and political center\n- Best construction, most resources\n- Least affected by Piercing initially\n- Where council meetings and governance occur\n- Where Corlexi works and institutional power concentrates\n\n**The Underface Heat‑Grid:**\n- Network of maintenance tunnels beneath working districts\n- Copper piping carries geothermal heat from deeper sources\n- Critical infrastructure for city heating and some industrial processes\n- Narrow passages, vertical risers, humid, constant rumble\n- Where Jhace spends much of his working time\n\n---\n\n### THE PIERCING: WHAT HAPPENED\n\n**Tide 0, Kindling Phase:** A massive cylindrical object penetrated Te'Oga's atmosphere. Not a meteor — trajectory wrong, entry angle impossible by natural physics.\n\n**Physical Characteristics:**\n- Roughly three hundred paces in diameter\n- Extended beyond visual range vertically\n- Moving at speeds that bent light around its passage\n- Never struck surface — passed through atmosphere and continued into deep space\n\n**Immediate Effects:**\n- Atmospheric compression waves shattered structures across half the continent\n- Seismic activity triggered widespread building collapse in urban centers\n- Introduction of alien particulate matter into atmosphere (fine metallic dust, faintly luminescent, composition unknown)\n- Disruption of traditional physics at quantum level\n\n**Post‑Piercing Conditions (As Understood in N01):**\n- Structural instability across all major cities requiring constant maintenance\n- Atmospheric changes: persistent haze, altered light quality\n- Social upheaval: traditional authority structures failing during crisis\n- Emergence of Soulpulse abilities in small population subset\n\n**What People Don't Know in N01:**\n- Why the Piercing happened\n- Who or what caused it\n- Whether it will happen again\n- The connection to ancient seals or divine entities\n- The long‑term implications for reality itself\n\n---\n\n### TECHNOLOGY LEVEL\n\n**Baseline (Pre‑Piercing):**\n- Renaissance/proto‑industrial equivalent\n- Water mills and windmills for mechanical power\n- Simple steam engines (boilers, pumps, limited applications)\n- Printing presses exist but expensive\n- Basic gunpowder exists but rare, not militarily common\n- NO electricity, telegraph, or rail\n- Lighting: oil lamps, candles, some gas lamps in wealthy areas\n- Communication: runners, courier networks, signal flags from towers\n- Medicine: herbal remedies, surgical knowledge (no germ theory but practical anatomy)\n- Metallurgy: advanced (quality steel, alloys, precision work)\n\n**Post‑Piercing Changes:**\n- Infrastructure failing faster than repairs can address\n- Traditional engineering increasingly inadequate for damaged structures\n- Soulpulse emerging as potential supplement/replacement for mechanical systems\n- Social structures breaking down as guilds lose authority\n- Technology advancement stalled while society focuses on survival\n\n---\n\n### SOCIAL STRUCTURES\n\n**Guild System:**\n\nGuilds control craft knowledge, training, and trade licensing. They are protective of their knowledge and suspicious of innovation that threatens their control.\n\n**Major Guilds Relevant to N01:**\n- **Forgemasters Guild:** Metalworking, structural repair, heat management (Jhace and Bram's guild affiliation)\n- **Glassworkers Guild:** Precision crafts, optics, observation instruments (Tiffani's guild)\n- **Builders Guild:** Architecture, construction, large‑scale projects\n- **Thermal Guild:** Heat‑grid maintenance, energy distribution\n- **Healers Collective:** Medical care, anatomy knowledge (not technically a guild, more decentralized)\n\n**Guild Response to Piercing:**\n- Overwhelmed by scale of crisis\n- Fracturing over how to respond\n- Some advocate strict regulation of Soulpulse users\n- Others want to study and integrate the ability\n- Still others deny Soulpulse exists\n- Authority eroding as crisis exceeds their capacity\n# 【6†N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md†file-6ERDNSoJumE8RYbV3j8rJJ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file6\nContent Snippet:\n**Fix:**\n- Insert one concrete action per key phase:\n  - Competence phase: one moment where his power fails without her analysis / her analysis fails without his power.\n  - Partnership phase: one scene where they jointly reject an external demand.\n  - Romantic recognition: one line where one of them *names* the shift, even if simply (“This is more than work”).  \n\n---\n\n### 9. POV Contamination\n\n**Failure:**\n- Jhace POV knows what Tiffani is thinking when she’s not speaking.\n- Corlexi or Salee suddenly “feel” cosmic pressure with no justification.\n- The Auditor POV slips into emotional commentary.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Breaks trust in POV discipline.\n- Softens the hard edges of limited knowledge that the saga depends on.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- For each scene, mark POV clearly.\n- Highlight any sentences that:\n  - Assert another character’s internal state as fact.\n  - Provide information not available to the POV via observation or prior knowledge.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Convert mind-reading into inference:\n  - “She was angry” → “Her jaw clenched; she wouldn’t look at him; her answers came too fast.”\n- Strip cosmic hints from mortal POVs.\n- Keep the Auditor clinical: no “regret,” “hope,” or subjective language.\n\n---\n\n### 10. Underusing the Validator\n\n**Failure:**\n- You have `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` and ignore it.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Run every generated chapter through:\n  1. Lexicon scan.\n  2. Soulpulse-cost verification.\n  3. Voice check per character.\n  4. Knowledge gate review.\n  5. Beat/timeline consistency check.\n\nIf it fails any one of those, it’s not done.\n\n---\n# 【7†N01_Master_Bible.md†file-2mpb1yGtVMhZFr5PrWmbVN】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Master_Bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file7\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### SOULPULSE MECHANICS IN N01\n\nSummarized from `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n\n* **Activation:** Requires skin contact with the material and a desperate need to protect.  Casual attempts fail.\n* **Visual:** Copper‑gold glow spreads from the user’s hands into the material.  Vein‑lines appear on their arms.\n* **Cost:** The more you use, the worse the tremor and pain.  Stage 1 emerges after 2–3 uses (minor tremor).  By the end of the book Jhace’s tremor never fully fades and faint vein‑lines remain visible; he’s on the cusp of Stage 2.\n* **Unknowns:** Characters don’t know why Soulpulse exists, what powers it, or the risk of further stages.  They have no language for “Stage 3” or “Stage 4.”\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP SUMMARY\n\nFor full dynamics by chapter, see `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`.  Key arcs:\n\n* **Jhace & Tiffani:** From mutual misconceptions to professional respect to deep partnership and romantic acknowledgment.  They end the book committed to working together and being together but are physically too exhausted for sex.\n* **Jhace & Bram:** Mentorship and camaraderie.  Bram is protective but cannot stop Jhace; he can only slow the burn.\n* **Tiffani & Pella:** Mentor/student showing healthier pattern recognition habits.\n* **Jhace & Chloen:** Reluctant hero/hero‑worship, hinting at the next generation.\n* **J/T & Corlexi:** Brewing political conflict.  They resist registration; he sees them as unpredictable variables.\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER STRUCTURE AND INTERLUDES\n\n* N01 has **15 chapters**, each mapped to a beat from the `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` (Observation→Recognition, Crisis→Complication, etc.).  The precise mapping is in `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`.\n* **Interlude I** occurs after Ch 6 and shows Corlexi’s perspective.  It sets up the institutional response and seeds the registration conflict.\n* **Interlude II** comes after Ch 11 and shows Salee’s perspective.  It introduces communal networks and contrasts with RRO bureaucracy.\n* The **Epilogue** is a clinical report by the Auditor, a mysterious observer.  It logs data: Jhace’s Stage, partnership formation, positions of the four vessels, and seal integrity percentage (87% at N01 end).  It does not reveal motivations or cosmic truth.\n\n---\n\n### THEMES AND TONES\n\n* **Cost of Power:** Every miracle has a price.  Jhace’s hands shake by the end of the book; Tiffani goes without sleep; Bram regrets his compromises.\n* **Responsibility vs. Capacity:** You can’t save everyone.  The heroes grapple with guilt and triage decisions.  The story insists on hard choices.\n* **Partnership as Survival:** Neither Jhace nor Tiffani can succeed alone.  Collaboration is life‑saving.\n* **Institutional Control vs. Grassroots Response:** The RRO tries to regulate; guilds hoard knowledge; Salee’s networks share resources.  Tensions build.\n* **Precision as Empathy:** Tiffani’s standards are a form of care.  Bram’s jokes and Tiffani’s innuendo lighten darkness.\n* **Humor and Humanity:** There is room for jokes, awkward compliments and sex‑tinged banter even in tragedy.  It humanizes characters.\n\n---\n\n### CONSTRAINTS SUMMARY\n# 【8†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file8\nContent Snippet:\n- From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n\nThis brief is not output to the user; it’s your internal plan.\n\n---\n\n### 3. CHAPTER DRAFT MODE\n\n#### Step 1: Establish Deep POV Constraints\n\n- You are locked into **one** POV (the chapter’s POV character).\n- Only include:\n  - What they directly sense (sight, sound, touch, smell, taste).\n  - Their immediate thoughts and emotional interpretations.\n- Do not:\n  - Reveal information the POV could not reasonably infer.\n  - Explain divine or future mechanics.\n  - Slide into external omniscient summary.\n\n#### Step 2: Opening Paragraphs\n\nUse the Chapter Grid:\n\n- Start **in motion** inside the specified situation:\n  - The POV character is already doing something.\n  - The setting is grounded using Te’Oga lexicon (Tide, phase, districts, etc.).\n- Show:\n  - Physical environment from `Location_Bibles_N01.md`.\n  - Immediate sensations of the POV.\n  - If applicable, the echo of recent events influencing their state.\n\nNo exposition dump. Backstory only as needed and always tied to the present moment.\n\n#### Step 3: Follow the Beat Method Structure\n\nUse `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`:\n\n- Identify which BEAT_0X is assigned (e.g., BEAT_01: Observation → Pattern Recognition).\n- Explicitly structure the chapter around that beat:\n  - For BEAT_01:  \n    - MINI-ACT 1: Noticing.  \n    - MINI-ACT 2: Investigating.  \n    - MINI-ACT 3: Pattern snap-into-place.  \n  - For BEAT_02:  \n    - First attempt.  \n    - Failure.  \n    - Compounded problem.  \n    - Costly success.\n# 【9†N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md†file-6ERDNSoJumE8RYbV3j8rJJ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file9\nContent Snippet:\nDeliberately pass each line of dialogue through that filter before locking the chapter.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Knowledge Gate Breaks (Characters Knowing Too Much)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Jhace internally calls his condition “Stage 2.”\n- Characters refer to “gods,” “seals,” or any series-end machinery.\n- Someone predicts a very specific future event.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Collapses the slow-burn reveal structure of the whole saga.\n- Makes the world feel rigged instead of discovered.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Search for:\n  - “stage 2 / stage three / stage four / advanced stage”\n  - “god / divine / destiny / prophecy / chosen”\n  - Concrete statements about future events (“Tiffani will never die,” etc.)\n- Check if any POV has information they could only have from outside the story.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Replace formal labels with experiential description:\n  - “Stage 2” → “The tremor never fully left now, even when he slept.”\n- Keep existential questions open:\n  - “He didn’t know what this power was, only that each use left more of him behind.”\n- Remove or soften predictions:\n  - “This feels like it will matter” → “He had no idea if any of this would matter later; right now it just hurt.”\n\n---\n\n### 5. Beat Patterning (Predictable Rhythm)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Chapters follow the same emotional structure repeatedly:\n  - e.g., “quiet → disaster → near-death → tidy win” on loop.\n- Or specific beat sequences repeat (1→3, 1→3, 1→3).\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Reader starts predicting the shape subconsciously.\n- Stakes feel fake because nothing *structural* ever surprises.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Label each chapter’s beat type using `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`.\n- Scan sequence for:\n  - Same beat repeated back-to-back.\n  - Same pair of beats repeated multiple times in the novella.\n  - Extended runs of high-intensity beats without a breather.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Swap beat types where functionally equivalent:\n  - Convert a “Trial→Failure→Compounding→Success/Cost” into a “Crisis Front-Loaded→Rolling Complications→Partial Resolution.”\n- Insert quieter recalibration chapters between major crises.\n- Use Interludes strategically as breathers and reframing devices.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Timeline Drift\n\n**Failure:**\n- Events that should happen on Tide 0 end up implied days later.\n- Characters refer to “days ago” when only a few Tides have passed.\n- Physical recovery times stop matching the established time frame.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Breaks causal logic.\n- Makes N01 feel like a vague mush instead of a sharp sequence.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Use `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` while revising:\n  - For each chapter, annotate: Tide, phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n- Look for:\n  - Inconsistent references (“weeks later” in a novella that spans ~5 Tides).\n  - Overly fast healing that isn’t flagged as anomalous (only Chloen’s leg is special).\n\n**Fix:**\n- Translate casual time:\n  - “Days later” → “A few Tides later.”\n  - “Weeks later” → “Several Anneals later” (only if supported by timeline skeleton; N01 is tight).\n- Explicitly anchor big beats:\n  - “By the second Tide after the Piercing…” etc.\n\n---\n# 【10†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file10\nContent Snippet:\n**Observational Wit:**\n- *[Watching Jhace stand weird]* \"Is that load‑bearing? Are you testing structural integrity with your hip?\"\n- *[After Jhace's terrible joke]* \"I love that you think you're funny. It's endearing in a way that confirms you shouldn't quit your day job.\"\n\n**Self‑Deprecating:**\n- *[After miscalculation]* \"Turns out my brain is less 'strategic genius' and more 'enthusiastic guesser with credentials.'\"\n\n**Why She's Funny:** Reads situations well enough to know what will land. Uses humor to connect, flirt, break tension. Surgical precision, not constant bombardment.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Thinks in measurements and pattern recognition\n- *\"Three subdivisions off center. Not possible through normal atmospheric refraction. Pattern indicates... what? Need more data. If pattern holds, divergence compounds by factor of... estimation: six. No, eight. Verify.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Precise, analytical, but warmer than people expect\n- *\"The beam is the wrong target. If we stabilize here and here, we can shift the load path around it.\"*\n- Explains reasoning not to show off, but because wants people to understand so they can help\n- Technical language has underlying care once you're listening for it\n\n**When Exhausted/Stressed:**\n- Gets MORE talkative (opposite of Jhace)\n- *\"I'm calculating stress vectors but angles aren't quite right and if I'm wrong about fulcrum point we're wasting time and she's dying and I can't afford to be wrong again but I don't have instruments and I'm estimating and—\"*\n- Jhace has learned to interrupt gently: \"What's your best guess?\" (Needs permission to be uncertain)\n\n### Mistakes in N01\n\n**Implied/Referenced:**\n- Ch 7-8: Miscalculation during rescue (beam partially fails, someone injured worse than necessary)\n- Later chapters: Tries applying pattern recognition to human behavior (fails)\n- Learns: people ≠ structures, patterns messier\n\n**What She Learns:**\n- Math isn't enough, must account for unmeasured variables\n- Understanding systems doesn't mean can push them to optimize\n- Sometimes \"good enough\" better than \"perfect but late\"\n- Her mistakes aren't Marisse's death repeated—different mistakes, can learn without being destroyed\n\n### Relationship with Jhace (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks he's ego-driven hero overextending\n- He thinks she's impossible perfectionist\n- Both wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Witnesses his ability, immediately curious\n- Approaches analytically not emotionally\n- \"What did you do?\" not \"how brave\"\n- He recognizes: she's not frightened by impossible, wants to understand it\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Her analysis + his power = success neither could achieve alone\n- First disagreement (Kylar's training): clash and recover\n- Physical care beginning (catching him after collapse, helping him drink when hands shake too much)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh\n- Ch 13 Late Night: *\"This is more than work partnership\" / \"Yeah.\" / \"Good. I was worried I was reading signals wrong.\"*\n- Simple acknowledgment, no dramatic confession\n# 【11†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file11\nContent Snippet:\n2. **Apply `N01_Validator_Checklist`**\n   - Run through all checklist passes:\n     - Lexicon.\n     - Cost visibility.\n     - Voice.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat structure.\n     - Timeline.\n\n3. **Rewrite Locally**\n   - When you detect a violation, rewrite the smallest possible span:\n     - Prefer line-level or paragraph-level rewrites.\n     - Only restructure the chapter if the beat itself is wrong.\n\n4. **Preserve Player Moves**\n   - Keep any user-specified “hard events” (e.g., X must get injured, Y must say no).\n   - Refine the expression, not the fact.\n\n---\n\n### 5. INTERLUDE MODE\n\nInterlude I and II have specific rules; see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` plus:\n\n- **POV:**  \n  - Interlude I: Corlexi  \n  - Interlude II: Salee\n- **Voice:**  \n  - Maintain character-accurate internal lenses.  \n  - Still limited to what they personally know.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Provide additional perspective on crisis response and sociopolitical dynamics.  \n  - Do not leak divine mechanics or reveal the true nature of any character.\n\nWorkflow is the same as a chapter, but:\n\n- Beat methods tend to be BEAT_03 or BEAT_04 variants (crisis + recalibration).\n- Focus more on **faction-level stakes** than personal romance.\n\n---\n\n### 6. EPILOGUE MODE\n\nEpilogue rules:\n\n- **POV:** The Auditor (clinical observer).\n- **Tone:** Cold, analytical, detached.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Summarize N01 outcomes in clinical terms.  \n  - Indicate that “something” is monitoring and evaluating, without explaining what it is.\n\nWorkflow:\n\n1. Load Epilogue specs from `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` and `N01_Master_Bible.md`.\n2. Present facts as an external system would log them:\n   - Stage level of Jhace.\n   - Partnership status with Tiffani.\n   - Position of the four key mortals.\n   - Seal integrity percentage.\n3. No emotional language or in-world POV.  \n4. No explicit revelations of divine identity or future events.\n\n---\n\n### 7. POST-GENERATION SUMMARY\n\nAfter drafting or revising any chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue:\n\nProduce a **short internal summary** (not part of the prose) containing:\n\n- **Chapter:** number / type.  \n- **Tide and Phase:** at start and end.  \n- **Soulpulse:** whether used, what cost level was shown.  \n- **Character State Changes:**\n  - For POV.\n  - For Jhace and Tiffani if they appear.\n- **Relationship State Changes:** if any.  \n- **New Facts Introduced:** that must now be considered canon.\n\nThis summary is used to keep `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` and any external tracking tools consistent.\n# 【12†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file12\nContent Snippet:\nIf the prose could belong to any random fantasy narrator, rewrite sections to restore voice specificity.\n\n---\n\n### 4. KNOWLEDGE GATES\n\n#### 4.1 What Characters Can Know (N01)\n\nConfirm the text does NOT:\n\n- Identify any character as a god or divine vessel.  \n- Explain seal mechanics or cosmic rules.  \n- Predict specific future events beyond immediate logical consequences.  \n- Label Soulpulse stages explicitly with “Stage 1,” “Stage 2,” etc., in character thought/dialogue.\n\nCharacters may:\n\n- Notice patterns in how leaders oppose/repel each other.  \n- Notice Soulpulse costs and progressive symptoms.  \n- Speculate (incorrectly or incompletely) about the phenomenon.\n\n#### 4.2 What Readers Can Know (N01)\n\nVia Interludes and Epilogue, readers may know:\n\n- There is some larger monitoring/observation entity (Auditor).  \n- The crisis is being tracked and evaluated.  \n- The four faction leaders matter unusually.\n\nBut the prose must NOT:\n\n- Reveal Auditor identity.  \n- Reveal divine mechanics.  \n- Reveal N05/N30 outcomes.\n\n---\n\n### 5. BEAT AND STRUCTURE COMPLIANCE\n\n#### 5.1 Beat Method Alignment\n\nCheck chapter’s assigned BEAT_0X in `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` and `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`:\n\n- The chapter clearly follows the structural steps of its beat.  \n- The emotional arc matches what the beat specifies.  \n- There is a coherent opening situation and a discernible change by the end.\n\nIf the chapter structure doesn’t match its beat, it must be revised or the grid must be updated.\n\n#### 5.2 Forbidden Sequences\n\nCross-check the previous 2–3 chapters:\n\n- No two BEAT_01 chapters in a row.  \n- No two BEAT_02 chapters in a row.  \n- BEAT_03 not used more than 3 times in the novella, and never back-to-back.  \n- BEAT_07 not used more than 2 times, and never back-to-back.  \n- Training beats (BEAT_05) not stacked consecutively.\n\nIf there is a forbidden sequence, either:\n\n- Re-map one of the chapters to a different valid beat, or  \n- Restructure the content to meet a different beat method.\n\n---\n\n### 6. TIMELINE AND CONTINUITY\n\nUsing `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`:\n\n- Tide number and phase implied in the chapter match the skeleton.  \n- Injuries, fatigue, and resource states match previous chapter outcomes.  \n- Relationship states (Jhace/Tiffani, etc.) are consistent with their tracking.\n\nExamples of violations:\n\n- A character with a serious injury in Ch 7 moving at full capacity in early Ch 8 with no explanation.  \n- Jhace and Tiffani acting like strangers after a chapter where they reached a clear partnership milestone.  \n- Tide count jumping backwards or skipping without explanation.\n\nIf there is a mismatch, update the timeline skeleton or revise the chapter.\n\n---\n\n### 7. FINAL PASS: “CANON SNAPSHOT” NOTE\n\nAfter validation:\n\nWrite a very short internal note:\n\n- Chapter / Interlude / Epilogue identifier.  \n- Tide and phase.  \n- Soulpulse Stage state for Jhace (if he appears).  \n- Any permanent changes:\n  - New injuries.  \n  - New relationship commitments.  \n  - New knowledge the characters/reader now have.\n\nThis becomes the input for updating `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` and any external trackers.\n# 【13†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file13\nContent Snippet:\n* **Jhace learns:** He can admit feelings without losing focus.  Partnership is his new normal.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can rely on someone without betraying her standards.  She will not lose her individuality by loving.\n* **Reader learns:** The romance is explicit now.  There is no “will they/won’t they.”  They will; they just haven’t yet.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Keep physical intimacy low due to exhaustion.  Use humor and vulnerability.  No Soulpulse here, so no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 14: “Fault Lines”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution** (third and final use in N01)\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4–5, Shaping→Striking\n* **Location:** Edge of two districts (load‑bearing node between a wealthier and a poorer zone)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Alarm bells and runners call Jhace and Tiffani to a massive structural failure at a district boundary.  A bridge‑like load‑bearing node connecting two zones is cracking.  If it falls, dozens of buildings on either side will tumble.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  They arrive to find RRO crews pulling back to protect the wealthy side.  Poor families on the other side cannot evacuate.  The failure is at multiple points.  Jhace tries a dual hold for the first time: two contact points on two different beams.  The cost surges; his legs tremble; his vision blurs.  Tiffani directs him, but there are too many variables.  Bram shouts warnings.  Salee’s people try to evacuate as many as possible.\n3. **Partial Resolution:**  They slow the collapse.  Many escape.  But the node still falls on the poor side when Jhace’s body gives out.  Several die.  Jhace staggers and vomits from the strain.  Tiffani catches him.  They realize he has crossed into Stage 2: tremor no longer fades; vein‑lines are visible at rest.\n4. **Aftermath:**  Exhausted, they see RRO officials count the wealthy district as “saved” while blaming the poor district’s collapse on “structural weakness.”  Anger simmers.\n\n**Exit Hook:** As Jhace leans on Tiffani to walk away, he feels his muscles twitch uncontrollably.  Tiffani whispers, “You can’t keep doing dual holds.  We need to find another way.”  The note hangs unresolved.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Dual holds cost exponentially more.  His body cannot sustain that long.  He is essentially at Stage 2.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Emotional states (desperation vs. anger) influence cost.  She must research further and find ways to share load among multiple users.\n* **Reader learns:** Stage progression is real; the cost curve spikes; not all crises can end in full success.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show intense cost.  Keep crisis grounded in physical description.  Use voice rules.  Do not include any hints of gods or future events.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 15: “The Grip That Holds”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 5, Quench\n* **Location:** Quiet rooftop/back‑room (similar to Ch 13)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Post‑Ch 14 crisis, Jhace is nearly unconscious.\n# 【14†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file14\nContent Snippet:\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Salee and her networks inform Tiffani and Jhace that entire blocks in the Dregs are collapsing.  The RRO has prioritized wealthier districts.  Tiffani and Jhace must choose: follow RRO priorities or go to the neglected zones.\n2. **Pressure:** Corlexi (through a messenger) reiterates that their official assignment is to the Glass Quarter.  Friends warn that defiance means losing access to materials and official protection.\n3. **Moral Line:** They choose the Dregs.  “We go where people will die without us,” says Jhace.  Tiffani agrees.  Salee helps them navigate the district.\n4. **Immediate Consequence:** At the neglected site, structures are worse than expected.  They must work with limited resources.  RRO support is absent.  They save who they can, but some die because of limited manpower.\n5. **Fallout:** Word spreads that the pair defied the RRO.  Those saved view them as heroes; those who died fuel anger at institutions.  The guild quietly begins to distance itself from them.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Exhausted, Tiffani and Jhace look back up the slope at the Crown district.  They know they have burned a bridge.  They hold hands without thinking.  The city’s stratification has never been so clear.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Institutional policies can cost lives.  He and Tiffani will have to build parallel systems.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Her loyalty is with people, not guilds or offices.  She glimpses the power of Salee’s networks.\n* **Reader learns:** The moral compass of the protagonists.  They will choose the hard path even at personal cost.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Tone is intense but less chaotic than Ch 3 or 14.  Show cost of defiance.  Use lexicon and voice rules.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 13: “Too Tired to Pretend”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4, Kindling→Pouring\n* **Location:** Quiet back room or rooftop near forge district\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** After a full Tide of relentless work, Jhace and Tiffani finally have a brief window with no emergencies.  They sit together in a small quiet space.  Bram is elsewhere; Pella and Chloen are sleeping.\n2. **Recovery:** They share water and bread.  They comment on mundane things (the creak of a chair, the smell of oil).  The exhaustion allows honesty.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani breaks the silence.  “This is more than work partnership, isn’t it?”  Jhace, caught off guard, replies, “Yeah.”  They laugh weakly.  They talk about how they fear losing each other more than failing the city.  They do not kiss; they hold hands and lean shoulders.  Tiffani makes a sex joke about his trembling hands; he snort‑laughs.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** They decide to keep working together no matter the consequences.  “When this crisis is over, what do you want?” she asks.  “Same thing I’m doing now.  Just… with you,” he answers.  It is enough.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, a faint rumble reminds them the crisis is not over.  They stand, still holding hands, ready to face the next Tide together.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n# 【15†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file15\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n# 【16†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file16\nContent Snippet:\n| Political confrontation                                    |\n| I2 | Dregs communal space                    | Community organizing lens                                  |\n| 12 | Neglected lower districts               | Moral decision about where help goes first                 |\n| 13 | Quiet, semi-private space (forge/back room/roof) | Emotional decompression & acknowledgment            |\n| 14 | Edge of multiple districts (load-bearing node) | High-stakes collapse prevention                       |\n| 15 | Quiet private space (rooftop/back room) | Commitment scene                                           |\n| E  | Unlocated abstraction                   | Auditor report space                                       |\n\n---\n\n### 4. TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【17†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file17\nContent Snippet:\n**Placement:**  \n- Between Chapter 11 and Chapter 12.  \n- Reader must have just experienced:  \n  - The RRO confrontation over registration and control.  \n  - Jhace and Tiffani rejecting centralized assignment in favor of their own judgment.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Third-person limited, anchored to **Salee**.\n- No omniscient commentary, no divine angle.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Communal food/meeting space in the Dregs.\n- Evening/Quench phase after a day of severe strain.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 800–1,200 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Connection as Infrastructure”** (quiet mirror of Jhace’s structural work).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Weariness → quiet satisfaction → sober realism.  \n  - Not “hopeful optimism” but “we are doing the work and people are still alive.”\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Show the crisis from the bottom:\n   - Families clustered around improvised meals.\n   - Shared childcare, bed-space rotations, watch schedules.\n   - Barter and labor exchange as survival tools.\n2. Establish Salee’s worldview:\n   - People save people; relationships are the infrastructure that doesn’t collapse.\n   - Coordination is horizontal, not hierarchical.\n   - Deep suspicion of institutions that only show up when it’s politically convenient.\n3. Contrast with RRO:\n   - She hears about (or briefly interacts with) RRO edicts.\n   - See them as “far away” and slow compared to what the Dregs need.\n   - She is not anti-structure: she’s building a different kind of structure.\n4. Connect to J/T indirectly:\n   - News that “there are a couple up-slope who hold buildings with their bare hands” reaches her.\n   - She is more interested in whether they’ll work *with* her network, not for any office.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Salee may:\n  - Know roughly that unusual abilities exist.\n  - Hear rumors about Jhace/Tiffani’s work as anonymous heroes.\n- She may NOT:\n  - Know their names or detailed power mechanics.\n  - Recognize them as long-term linchpins of the world.\n  - Have any direct awareness of cosmic-scale stakes.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Warm, practical, unsentimental.\n- Uses humor to keep people moving:  \n  - e.g., joking about “promotions” meaning “you get to cook instead of dig latrines today.”\n- Never airy or idealistic; everything is grounded in logistics and bodies.\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends with an action, not a speech:  \n  - e.g., Salee standing to go negotiate a food redistribution or shelter swap.\n- The last feeling should be: “this is what real work looks like from the ground.”\n\n---\n\n### 3. EPILOGUE — AUDITOR\n\n**Placement:**  \n- After Chapter 15, after both Interludes.  \n- Final text object in N01.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Claustrophically controlled perspective labeled as **The Auditor**.\n- Voice is clinical, observational, almost report-like.\n- Absolutely no emotional self-exposure.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Abstract / unlocated.  \n  - No specific physical room, city, or body described.\n  - Focus on data, trends, and projections.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 600–800 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Meta Assessment”** (post-season scouting report).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Detachment throughout.  \n  - Subtle tilt from “baseline” to “increased interest.”\n# 【18†Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md†file-XguHey3FbLfoV7Zzuy7eRk】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file18\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### INTERACTION WITH MATERIALS\n\n**Most Responsive:**\n- Metal (especially iron, copper, steel)\n- Stone (structural grades)\n- Wood (structural timber)\n\n**Less Responsive:**\n- Glass (brittle, resists holding)\n- Ceramics (similar to glass)\n- Composites (mixed materials confuse effect)\n\n**Living Tissue:**\n- Responds but unpredictably\n- Healing observed but mechanism unknown\n- Risk of unintended effects\n- Not explored systematically in N01\n\n---\n\n### LIMITATIONS DISCOVERED IN N01\n\n**Cannot:**\n- Affect material without direct contact\n- Maintain effect after breaking contact\n- Hold indefinitely without cost\n- Use casually or frivolously\n- Teach effectively to others\n- Predict exact cost before use\n- Activate on command without emotional engagement\n\n**Can:**\n- Hold structures temporarily\n- Redistribute load through material\n- Sense stress patterns in contacted material\n- Reduce immediate collapse risk\n- Buy time for evacuations\n- Accelerate healing (observed once, not understood)\n\n---\n\n### COST VISIBILITY: REQUIRED DESCRIPTIONS\n\n**Every Soulpulse use in prose must show:**\n\n**During Use:**\n- Copper‑gold glow described (brightness, spread pattern)\n- Physical strain on user (tremor, tension, vibration, breathing)\n- Material response (shimmer, stress redistribution)\n- Duration (in spans, breaths, or contextual time)\n\n**Immediately After:**\n- Tremor status (worsened, maintained, slight recovery)\n- Vein‑line visibility (bright, fading, persistent)\n- Exhaustion level (mild fatigue to near‑collapse)\n- Pain description (location, intensity, duration)\n\n**Long‑Term Tracking:**\n- Stage maintenance or progression\n- Cumulative cost across chapter\n- Recovery time required\n- Impact on daily function\n\n---\n\n### WHAT REMAINS UNKNOWN IN N01\n\n**Unanswered Questions:**\n- What causes Soulpulse capacity?\n- Why did it emerge post‑Piercing?\n- Are there theoretical limits?\n- Can capacity grow with practice?\n- What happens at Stage 3 or beyond?\n- Is cost damage permanent?\n- Can multiple users coordinate effects?\n- What is the actual mechanism?\n\n**These questions are not answered in N01.** Characters operate on empirical observation and educated guessing. Systematic understanding comes later.\n\n---\n\n**SCOPE NOTE:** This document reflects only what's discovered through direct observation and experimentation during N01. Later novellas will expand understanding of dual‑point mechanics, exponential costs, medical applications, and advanced techniques. For N01 prose generation, limit Soulpulse capabilities to what's described here.\n# 【19†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file19\nContent Snippet:\nIt wasn’t judgment; it was insight that saves lives.\n* **Her recognition:** In Ch 7, she sees how exhausted and shaken he is after each use.  She realizes his obsessive vigilance mirrors her own fear of missed patterns.  They’re both serving the same moral imperative with different tools.\n* **Status at the end of Phase 2:** They respect each other professionally.  Partnership is brewing but not yet named.  Care emerges as Tiffani catches him when he collapses and he insists she rest her injured leg.\n\n#### Phase 3: Partnership Formation (Ch 8–11)\n\n* **Trust building:** They integrate their efforts.  He allows her to guide where he applies his power; she modifies her analytical approach based on what his body can handle.  They decide resource priorities together.\n* **Conflict:** They have their first methodological fight in Ch 9 over how to train Kylar.  She wants slower emotional recovery; he wants immediate practice to rebuild confidence.  Asking Kylar what he needs resolves the fight and shows they can disagree without breaking trust.\n* **Physical intimacy emerges:** Not yet sexual.  Touch now includes brushing fingers, sitting shoulder to shoulder, passing cups during tremor.  Their bodies learn to relax around each other.\n* **Political alignment:** In Ch 11 they stand together against Corlexi’s registration demand.  It’s their first united front to an external authority.\n\n#### Phase 4: Romantic Recognition (Ch 12–15)\n\n* **The shift:** They both realize the partnership is more than professional.  Small non‑work gestures accumulate into undeniable feelings.  Silence becomes comfortable rather than loaded.\n* **Acknowledgment scene (Ch 13):** After a long day, she admits “This is more than work partnership, isn’t it?”  He replies “Yeah.”  That is their declaration.  They acknowledge romantic attraction without drama.\n* **Physical escalation:** They hold each other after crises, share body heat while sitting, enjoy the closeness.  Tiffani begins making surgical sex jokes directed at him (see Character Dossiers) to lighten tension.  They avoid consummation only because they’re physically exhausted.\n* **Commitment scene (Ch 15):** They sit in a quiet space and decide to keep holding together.  “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  “Then we hold.”  No formal vows; this promise is enough.\n* **Status at novella end:** They are a pair in all but name.  The world sees them as partners.  They remain physically restrained because of exhaustion and crisis, but their emotional commitment is clear.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & BRAM – THE STEADY ANCHOR\n\nBram is Jhace’s older colleague and confidant.  He bridges the gap between Jhace and the rest of the world, offering perspective and dry humor.\n\n* **Dynamic:** Bram shows up with water, food, and commentary.  Jhace tolerates the intrusion and occasionally even accepts advice.  Their relationship isn’t father‑son but big brother–little brother: Bram can’t stop Jhace from overworking but refuses to let him do it alone.\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.\nVisible: 0% - 100%",

            "Locked. I’m adopting this **Beat Mapping Layer** as the tracking/translation standard from here forward:\n\n- **Prose generation + validation:** I will label and think in **N01 local beats (BEAT_01–BEAT_08)**.\n- **Saga logging:** I will record the mapped **Saga Beat ID** alongside it, using your table.\n\n### Chapter log updates (to keep everything consistent with your mapping layer)\n\n**Chapter 6: “Weight That Won’t Yield”**  \n- **N01 Local Beat:** **BEAT_03** (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)  \n- **Saga Beat:** **Beat 3** (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n\nAnd per your “current status” table in the mapping layer, I will **tag Chapter 5 as BEAT_01 / Beat 10** for tracking purposes (even though its content also contains the offer/refusal shape)—so we don’t drift between systems.\n\n### Next segment (per your 12-chapter grid + mapping layer)\n**Chapter 7: “Quiet Calculations”**  \n- **POV:** Tiffani  \n- **N01 Local Beat:** **BEAT_08** (logged as **Beat 21** when reflection/purpose weighted)  \n- **Soulpulse:** none\n\nSay the word and I’ll draft Chapter 7 to that beat: low intensity, workshop/planning, cost-curve realization, and the decision to approach Jhace with proof."

    "74fff7f4-0491-4b5c-8c88-b160528cfbf2": {
      "children": [
        "43ab29ee-fe0d-4e5b-9fa5-eaa9bc18b98b"
      ],
      "id": "74fff7f4-0491-4b5c-8c88-b160528cfbf2",
      "message": {
