          "content_type": "tether_browsing_display",
          "result": "# 【0†Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md†file-AFWjATZTdTCg4vasFHFR5M】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file0\nContent Snippet:\n## BEAT CATALOG — N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine all beat methods used in N01 so chapters can be:\n\n- Planned and validated structurally.  \n- Kept unpredictable at the series level.  \n- Emotionally coherent within themselves.\n\nN01 uses **eight** beat methods: BEAT_01 through BEAT_08.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Baseline Observation**  \n   - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n   - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n\n2. **Focused Attention**  \n   - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n   - Compares against prior experience.\n\n3. **Testing / Probing**  \n   - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n   - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n\n4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n   - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n   - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n\n5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n   - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nCuriosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Introducing a core problem.  \n- Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n- Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n- Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_02 — Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when you want a **hard failure** that escalates, followed by a victory that hurts.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Trial Setup**  \n   - Goal is defined (save someone, stabilize structure, train Soulpulse).  \n   - Plan seems plausible but not guaranteed.\n\n2. **Initial Attempt → Failure**  \n   - First attempt fails clearly.  \n   - Stakes increase.\n\n3. **Compounding Problem**  \n   - Failure makes situation worse (more damage, less time, more people at risk).\n\n4. **Reframe / New Approach**  \n   - POV changes approach, often with help from another character.\n\n5. **Success with Explicit Cost**  \n   - Goal is achieved or partially achieved.  \n   - Cost is visible: physical, emotional, moral, or relational.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nHope → Frustration → Panic → Determination → Relief + Dread\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Training episodes that go wrong.  \n- Major rescue that almost fails.  \n- Moments where price of success must be undeniable.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot follow another BEAT_02.  \n- Should not directly precede BEAT_03 in most cases (risk of fatigue).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Kylar’s first training attempt + follow-up.  \n- A rescue where Jhace’s first plan fails, and Tiffani’s input salvages it.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_03 — Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for big chaos chapters, especially disasters in progress.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Drop-In Crisis**  \n   - Chapter opens mid-disaster: building already collapsing, Piercing underway, etc.\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file1\nContent Snippet:\n**Constraint Checks:** Tone is intense but less chaotic than Ch 3 or 14.  Show cost of defiance.  Use lexicon and voice rules.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 13: “Too Tired to Pretend”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4, Kindling→Pouring\n* **Location:** Quiet back room or rooftop near forge district\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** After a full Tide of relentless work, Jhace and Tiffani finally have a brief window with no emergencies.  They sit together in a small quiet space.  Bram is elsewhere; Pella and Chloen are sleeping.\n2. **Recovery:** They share water and bread.  They comment on mundane things (the creak of a chair, the smell of oil).  The exhaustion allows honesty.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani breaks the silence.  “This is more than work partnership, isn’t it?”  Jhace, caught off guard, replies, “Yeah.”  They laugh weakly.  They talk about how they fear losing each other more than failing the city.  They do not kiss; they hold hands and lean shoulders.  Tiffani makes a sex joke about his trembling hands; he snort‑laughs.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** They decide to keep working together no matter the consequences.  “When this crisis is over, what do you want?” she asks.  “Same thing I’m doing now.  Just… with you,” he answers.  It is enough.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, a faint rumble reminds them the crisis is not over.  They stand, still holding hands, ready to face the next Tide together.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** He can admit feelings without losing focus.  Partnership is his new normal.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can rely on someone without betraying her standards.  She will not lose her individuality by loving.\n* **Reader learns:** The romance is explicit now.  There is no “will they/won’t they.”  They will; they just haven’t yet.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Keep physical intimacy low due to exhaustion.  Use humor and vulnerability.  No Soulpulse here, so no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 14: “Fault Lines”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution** (third and final use in N01)\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4–5, Shaping→Striking\n* **Location:** Edge of two districts (load‑bearing node between a wealthier and a poorer zone)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Alarm bells and runners call Jhace and Tiffani to a massive structural failure at a district boundary.  A bridge‑like load‑bearing node connecting two zones is cracking.  If it falls, dozens of buildings on either side will tumble.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  They arrive to find RRO crews pulling back to protect the wealthy side.  Poor families on the other side cannot evacuate.  The failure is at multiple points.  Jhace tries a dual hold for the first time: two contact points on two different beams.  The cost surges; his legs tremble; his vision blurs.  Tiffani directs him, but there are too many variables.  Bram shouts warnings.  Salee’s people try to evacuate as many as possible.\n3. **Partial Resolution:**  They slow the collapse.\n# 【2†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file2\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n\n* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n* **N01 arc:**\n  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n\n---\n\n### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n\n* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n\n| Chapter | Jhace & Tiffani | Jhace & Bram | Tiffani & Pella | Jhace & Chloen | Notes |\n|--------|-----------------|--------------|-----------------|----------------|-------|\n| **Ch 1** | — (haven’t met) | Bram teases, Jhace grunts | Apprentice cares; Tiffani annoyed | Apprentice work; minimal contact | Setup: mutual misconceptions |\n| **Ch 2** | — | — | Pella nags Tiffani to eat | — | Tiffani life shown |\n| **Ch 3** | First sight (crisis) | Bram helps Jhace respond | Pella escapes observatory with Tiffani | Chloen pinned and saved; leg heals | First contact; souls align without knowing |\n| **Ch 4** | Respect forms (analysis vs. brute force) | Bram warns about cost; Jhace hides tremor | Pella assists with salvage | Chloen hero worship grows | Learning to trust instincts and analysis |\n| **Ch 5** | Tiffani commits to study Soulpulse | Bram amused by J/T dynamic | Pella supporting analysis | — | Professional curiosity deepens |\n| **Ch 6** | First integrated rescue; critique becomes collaboration | Bram sees partnership forming | Pella absent | — | Respect moves toward partnership |\n| **Interlude I** | — | — | — | — | Corlexi view; seeds conflict |\n| **Ch 7** | Training conflict begins | Bram listens but doesn’t intervene | Pella observes | — | Methodological disagreements start |\n| **Ch 8** | Kylar fails; J/T fight about teaching | Bram warns about pushing too hard | Pella present | — | Stress test for partnership |\n| **Ch 9** | Asking Kylar his needs;\n# 【3†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file3\nContent Snippet:\nThey acknowledge romantic attraction without drama.\n* **Physical escalation:** They hold each other after crises, share body heat while sitting, enjoy the closeness.  Tiffani begins making surgical sex jokes directed at him (see Character Dossiers) to lighten tension.  They avoid consummation only because they’re physically exhausted.\n* **Commitment scene (Ch 15):** They sit in a quiet space and decide to keep holding together.  “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  “Then we hold.”  No formal vows; this promise is enough.\n* **Status at novella end:** They are a pair in all but name.  The world sees them as partners.  They remain physically restrained because of exhaustion and crisis, but their emotional commitment is clear.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & BRAM – THE STEADY ANCHOR\n\nBram is Jhace’s older colleague and confidant.  He bridges the gap between Jhace and the rest of the world, offering perspective and dry humor.\n\n* **Dynamic:** Bram shows up with water, food, and commentary.  Jhace tolerates the intrusion and occasionally even accepts advice.  Their relationship isn’t father‑son but big brother–little brother: Bram can’t stop Jhace from overworking but refuses to let him do it alone.\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n\n---\n\n### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n\n* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n# 【4†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file4\nContent Snippet:\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n\n---\n\n### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n\n* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n\n* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n* **N01 arc:**\n  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n\n---\n\n### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n\n* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n# 【5†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file5\nContent Snippet:\n**Constraint Checks:** Show cost explicitly.  No naming of “Stage 1.”  Keep voices distinct.  Lexicon rules apply.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 5: “Lines That Don’t Lie”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_04 – Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal or Choice**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Pouring→Shaping\n* **Location:** Damaged street near Merrow shop, salvage area, temporary data space\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Tiffani surveys the ruins of her observatory and the surrounding district.  She begins marking where the unusual holds occurred.  She sketches structural fractures and notes copper residue.\n2. **Offer:** Guild representatives and an RRO messenger approach.  They want her to stop chasing anomalies and focus on profitable repairs or to join an official committee.  They offer stability and status.\n3. **Evaluation:** Tiffani thinks of Marisse’s death and of the glowing beam.  She decides that understanding the impossible phenomenon is more important than guild approval.  She politely refuses the offer to join the committee.\n4. **Quiet Recalibration:** Back in her temporary space, she compiles data: hold durations, loads, injuries.  She draws connections between his technique and structural design.  She resolves to speak to “the man with the glow.”\n\n**Exit Hook:** Tiffani sees Jhace across the way, trying to steady his hands.  She walks toward him, logbook in hand, prepared to ask pointed questions.  The moment foreshadows their partnership.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Tiffani learns:** She cannot stay neutral.  Her skills are needed to understand and optimize this phenomenon.  She chooses active engagement over safe distance.\n* **Reader learns:** The world’s power structures (guilds, RRO) are already vying for control.  Tiffani will not let them shape her choices.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Tiffani’s voice is analytical but warm.  Use appropriate units.  No Soulpulse activation in this chapter, so no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 6: “Weight That Won’t Yield”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Shaping→Striking\n* **Primary Location:** Failing structure between districts in the Underface\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** A multi‑story building (lower Underface) begins to collapse.  People are trapped inside and above.  The scene opens mid‑panic.  Jhace is dragged toward the crisis by bystanders who know what he did.  Tiffani arrives to observe and assist.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  Jhace must hold a main support while Tiffani directs where his pressure should be.  Hidden fractures spread.  A second support starts to go.  Jhace can sense multiple stress points but cannot hold all at once.  He panics.\n3. **Collaboration:**  Tiffani yells specific instructions: “Shift your grip a span toward the joint.”  He trusts her guidance; he feels the load shift and can hold with less effort.  They adjust in real time as secondary cracks emerge.  It’s the first time his power and her analysis work in tandem.\n4. **Partial Resolution:**  People escape through a narrow gap.  Jhace’s arms quake.  The building doesn’t fully collapse but is condemned.  They save who they can.  Jhace is left shaking and exhausted.\n# 【6†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file6\nContent Snippet:\n* Why Soulpulse exists or what powers it.\n* That “Stages” are formal categories.  They experience symptoms but do not label them as Stage 1/2.\n* That the four faction leaders are gods in mortal form.\n* That seals were weakened by the Piercing.\n* That Tiffani will die or that the Fathom Break will occur.\n* Any events or revelations occurring in N02–N30.\n\n**Reader Permissions:**\n\n* Through interludes and the epilogue, readers get glimpses of the Auditor and see that the story is being watched and evaluated.  They may infer that bigger forces are at work but do not receive explanations.\n\n**Forbidden:**\n\n* Exposition dumps about the truth of the cosmos, gods, or the full saga.  Leave big questions unanswered.\n* Characters using terms like “divine,” “seal,” or “principle” in their own thoughts.  They don’t know.\n\n---\n\n### 4. BEAT AND STRUCTURAL RULES\n\nN01 uses an eight‑beat method catalogue (BEAT_01 through BEAT_08).  Each chapter is mapped to one beat.  The purpose is to prevent repetitive emotional rhythms and maintain variety.\n\n**Beat Assignments:** See `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for detailed definitions.  The validator checks chapters against their assigned beats.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n\n* No consecutive chapters using the same beat (e.g., BEAT_01 → BEAT_01).\n* No consecutive failure loops (BEAT_02 → BEAT_02) or training loops (BEAT_05 → BEAT_05).\n* BEAT_03 (crisis front‑loaded) limited to three uses total in N01 and never back‑to‑back.\n* BEAT_07 (political pressure) limited to two uses in N01, never back‑to‑back.\n\n**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n\n* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n\n---\n\n### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n\nEach POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n\n* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n\n* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n\nThe companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n\n**Key validation steps include:**\n# 【7†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file7\nContent Snippet:\n| Political confrontation                                    |\n| I2 | Dregs communal space                    | Community organizing lens                                  |\n| 12 | Neglected lower districts               | Moral decision about where help goes first                 |\n| 13 | Quiet, semi-private space (forge/back room/roof) | Emotional decompression & acknowledgment            |\n| 14 | Edge of multiple districts (load-bearing node) | High-stakes collapse prevention                       |\n| 15 | Quiet private space (rooftop/back room) | Commitment scene                                           |\n| E  | Unlocated abstraction                   | Auditor report space                                       |\n\n---\n\n### 4. TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【8†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file8\nContent Snippet:\n* **Jhace learns:** He can admit feelings without losing focus.  Partnership is his new normal.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can rely on someone without betraying her standards.  She will not lose her individuality by loving.\n* **Reader learns:** The romance is explicit now.  There is no “will they/won’t they.”  They will; they just haven’t yet.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Keep physical intimacy low due to exhaustion.  Use humor and vulnerability.  No Soulpulse here, so no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 14: “Fault Lines”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution** (third and final use in N01)\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4–5, Shaping→Striking\n* **Location:** Edge of two districts (load‑bearing node between a wealthier and a poorer zone)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Alarm bells and runners call Jhace and Tiffani to a massive structural failure at a district boundary.  A bridge‑like load‑bearing node connecting two zones is cracking.  If it falls, dozens of buildings on either side will tumble.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  They arrive to find RRO crews pulling back to protect the wealthy side.  Poor families on the other side cannot evacuate.  The failure is at multiple points.  Jhace tries a dual hold for the first time: two contact points on two different beams.  The cost surges; his legs tremble; his vision blurs.  Tiffani directs him, but there are too many variables.  Bram shouts warnings.  Salee’s people try to evacuate as many as possible.\n3. **Partial Resolution:**  They slow the collapse.  Many escape.  But the node still falls on the poor side when Jhace’s body gives out.  Several die.  Jhace staggers and vomits from the strain.  Tiffani catches him.  They realize he has crossed into Stage 2: tremor no longer fades; vein‑lines are visible at rest.\n4. **Aftermath:**  Exhausted, they see RRO officials count the wealthy district as “saved” while blaming the poor district’s collapse on “structural weakness.”  Anger simmers.\n\n**Exit Hook:** As Jhace leans on Tiffani to walk away, he feels his muscles twitch uncontrollably.  Tiffani whispers, “You can’t keep doing dual holds.  We need to find another way.”  The note hangs unresolved.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Dual holds cost exponentially more.  His body cannot sustain that long.  He is essentially at Stage 2.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Emotional states (desperation vs. anger) influence cost.  She must research further and find ways to share load among multiple users.\n* **Reader learns:** Stage progression is real; the cost curve spikes; not all crises can end in full success.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show intense cost.  Keep crisis grounded in physical description.  Use voice rules.  Do not include any hints of gods or future events.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 15: “The Grip That Holds”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 5, Quench\n* **Location:** Quiet rooftop/back‑room (similar to Ch 13)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Post‑Ch 14 crisis, Jhace is nearly unconscious.\n# 【9†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file9\nContent Snippet:\nbrute force) | Bram warns about cost; Jhace hides tremor | Pella assists with salvage | Chloen hero worship grows | Learning to trust instincts and analysis |\n| **Ch 5** | Tiffani commits to study Soulpulse | Bram amused by J/T dynamic | Pella supporting analysis | — | Professional curiosity deepens |\n| **Ch 6** | First integrated rescue; critique becomes collaboration | Bram sees partnership forming | Pella absent | — | Respect moves toward partnership |\n| **Interlude I** | — | — | — | — | Corlexi view; seeds conflict |\n| **Ch 7** | Training conflict begins | Bram listens but doesn’t intervene | Pella observes | — | Methodological disagreements start |\n| **Ch 8** | Kylar fails; J/T fight about teaching | Bram warns about pushing too hard | Pella present | — | Stress test for partnership |\n| **Ch 9** | Asking Kylar his needs; synthesis | Bram proud of them | Pella continues learning | — | They learn to listen to students |\n| **Ch 10** | Perfect team operation | Bram sees cost but respects choice | — | — | Trust through crisis |\n| **Ch 11** | United front vs. RRO | Bram supportive, amused by politics | — | — | Partnership now public |\n| **Interlude II** | — | — | — | — | Salee lens; sees J/T from distance |\n| **Ch 12** | Ethical alignment (choose Dregs) | Bram continues warnings | — | — | Moral compass shared |\n| **Ch 13** | Acknowledgment of romance | Bram off‑page but implied approval | Pella worrying | — | Emotional turning point |\n| **Ch 14** | Integrated crisis; cost pushes near Stage 2 | Bram watches, anxious | — | — | Danger intensifies, trust unwavering |\n| **Ch 15** | Quiet commitment scene | Bram supportive off‑page | — | — | They decide to hold together |\n| **Epilogue** | Partnership noted by Auditor as data | Bram unspecified | Pella unspecified | Chloen unspecified | Meta perspective |\n\n---\n\n**Scope Note:**  The dynamics described here apply only through the end of N01. Later novellas will alter, deepen, or conclude these relationships. Prose generation must not leak future knowledge or prematurely accelerate these arcs.\n# 【10†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file10\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 VALIDATOR CHECKLIST\n\n**Purpose:**  \nProvide a strict, mechanical validation pass for any N01 prose (chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue) to enforce:\n\n- Lexicon purity.  \n- Soulpulse cost visibility.  \n- Voice and POV discipline.  \n- Knowledge gating.  \n- Beat structure.  \n- Timeline and continuity.\n\nThis checklist is intended to be run **after** a draft is written or revised.\n\n---\n\n### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n\n#### 1.1 Forbidden Time Terms\n\nSearch the prose for each of the following; if any appear, they must be replaced:\n\n- day / days  \n- week / weeks  \n- month / months  \n- year / years  \n- season / spring / summer / fall / autumn / winter  \n- Monday / Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday / Friday / Saturday / Sunday  \n- hour / minute / second  \n- AM / PM / o'clock / midnight / noon\n\nFor each hit, confirm there is a correct substitution:\n\n- Tide, Anneal, Crucible, Turn, Cast, Span, Breath, Heartbeat, phase names (Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench).\n\n#### 1.2 Forbidden Distance / Direction Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- foot / feet / ft  \n- inch / inches / in  \n- yard / yards  \n- mile / miles  \n- meter / metre / m / kilometer / km / centimeter / cm  \n- north / south / east / west / NE / NW / SE / SW / compass\n\nConfirm use of:\n\n- Span, Pace, Pole, upslope/downslope, river-side/harbor-side/inland, landmark-based directions.\n\n#### 1.3 Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- pound / lb / lbs / ounce / oz / ton / tonne  \n- gram / g / kilogram / kg  \n- gallon / quart / liter / litre / ml  \n- Celsius / Fahrenheit / °C / °F / degree / degrees\n\nConfirm weights/volumes are expressed via:\n\n- Grain, Stone, Weight, Thimble, Cup (non-precise), Cask.\n\n#### 1.4 Modern Slang and Cultural Leakage\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- okay / OK / ok  \n- cool / awesome / dude / guy (as generic modern slang)  \n- any mention of real-world religions, mythologies, countries, historical events, or technologies that do not exist in Te’Oga.\n\nConfirm substitutions:\n\n- “Yes,” “Understood,” “Agreed,” “Fine,” etc., instead of “okay / cool / awesome.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. SOULPULSE COST AND MECHANICS\n\nFor every Soulpulse activation in the text:\n\n#### 2.1 Activation Requirements\n\n- Is the user in **direct contact** with the material?  \n- Is their **emotional state** clearly protective/desperate rather than casual?  \n- Is it clear **what** they are trying to hold/stabilize/mend?\n\nIf any of these are missing, revise.\n\n#### 2.2 During-Use Description\n\n- Copper-gold glow is explicitly described.  \n- Glow spread pattern into material is described (even briefly).  \n- Material response is visible (shimmer, tension, redistribution).  \n- Physical strain in the user is shown (tremor, muscle tension, breath changes).\n\nIf an activation reads “he used his power and the beam held” with no detail, it fails.\n\n#### 2.3 Immediate Aftermath\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file11\nContent Snippet:\n5. **Partial Resolution:**  People evacuate.  The building doesn’t fully collapse.  Jhace collapses from the exertion.  His hands shake uncontrollably.  He blacks out.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace wakes to chaos and sees Tiffani standing over him with a look of shock and curiosity.  They exchange a brief, bewildered glance.  The world is different now.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** He can do something impossible when protecting someone.  It costs him physically (ache, tremor).  It feels like heat and vibration.\n* **Tiffani learns:** People can manipulate structures with copper‑gold light.  She has no explanation yet but she’s determined to find one.\n* **Reader learns:** The Piercing is world‑changing; Soulpulse exists; it is tied to desperation and contact; it leaves residue (glow, tremor, healing).  Chloen’s leg heals quickly.\n* **Hidden:** Why this power exists, what the Piercing was, any link to gods or divine principles.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Full crisis intensity.  Show cost: aching hands, tremor, exhaustion.  Use sensory language (dust, noise).  Keep voices distinct (Jhace terse, Tiffani more observational).  Do not name the ability yet; characters just describe what they see.\n\n---\n\n### Interlude I (After Chapter 6) – Corlexi “System vs Chaos”\n\n* See `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` for details.  This interlude shows the RRO council chamber through Corlexi’s eyes.  It contrasts institutional attempts at order with the ground‑level chaos experienced in the first six chapters.  It seeds the registration conflict.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n# 【12†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file12\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** The power is not controlled by will alone; it requires genuine protective desperation.  Repeated use increases tremor and pain.  It may not be sustainable.\n* **Tiffani learns:** The phenomenon leaves measurable changes.  She begins building a taxonomy of holds (load, duration, cost).  She sees his hands shaking and begins to suspect a cost system.\n* **Reader learns:** Cost exists, Stage 1 tremor begins.  The ability fails if used without the right emotional trigger.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show cost explicitly.  No naming of “Stage 1.”  Keep voices distinct.  Lexicon rules apply.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 5: “Lines That Don’t Lie”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_04 – Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal or Choice**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Pouring→Shaping\n* **Location:** Damaged street near Merrow shop, salvage area, temporary data space\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Tiffani surveys the ruins of her observatory and the surrounding district.  She begins marking where the unusual holds occurred.  She sketches structural fractures and notes copper residue.\n2. **Offer:** Guild representatives and an RRO messenger approach.  They want her to stop chasing anomalies and focus on profitable repairs or to join an official committee.  They offer stability and status.\n3. **Evaluation:** Tiffani thinks of Marisse’s death and of the glowing beam.  She decides that understanding the impossible phenomenon is more important than guild approval.  She politely refuses the offer to join the committee.\n4. **Quiet Recalibration:** Back in her temporary space, she compiles data: hold durations, loads, injuries.  She draws connections between his technique and structural design.  She resolves to speak to “the man with the glow.”\n# 【13†N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md†file-6ERDNSoJumE8RYbV3j8rJJ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file13\nContent Snippet:\nDeliberately pass each line of dialogue through that filter before locking the chapter.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Knowledge Gate Breaks (Characters Knowing Too Much)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Jhace internally calls his condition “Stage 2.”\n- Characters refer to “gods,” “seals,” or any series-end machinery.\n- Someone predicts a very specific future event.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Collapses the slow-burn reveal structure of the whole saga.\n- Makes the world feel rigged instead of discovered.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Search for:\n  - “stage 2 / stage three / stage four / advanced stage”\n  - “god / divine / destiny / prophecy / chosen”\n  - Concrete statements about future events (“Tiffani will never die,” etc.)\n- Check if any POV has information they could only have from outside the story.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Replace formal labels with experiential description:\n  - “Stage 2” → “The tremor never fully left now, even when he slept.”\n- Keep existential questions open:\n  - “He didn’t know what this power was, only that each use left more of him behind.”\n- Remove or soften predictions:\n  - “This feels like it will matter” → “He had no idea if any of this would matter later; right now it just hurt.”\n\n---\n\n### 5. Beat Patterning (Predictable Rhythm)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Chapters follow the same emotional structure repeatedly:\n  - e.g., “quiet → disaster → near-death → tidy win” on loop.\n- Or specific beat sequences repeat (1→3, 1→3, 1→3).\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Reader starts predicting the shape subconsciously.\n- Stakes feel fake because nothing *structural* ever surprises.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Label each chapter’s beat type using `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`.\n- Scan sequence for:\n  - Same beat repeated back-to-back.\n  - Same pair of beats repeated multiple times in the novella.\n  - Extended runs of high-intensity beats without a breather.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Swap beat types where functionally equivalent:\n  - Convert a “Trial→Failure→Compounding→Success/Cost” into a “Crisis Front-Loaded→Rolling Complications→Partial Resolution.”\n- Insert quieter recalibration chapters between major crises.\n- Use Interludes strategically as breathers and reframing devices.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Timeline Drift\n\n**Failure:**\n- Events that should happen on Tide 0 end up implied days later.\n- Characters refer to “days ago” when only a few Tides have passed.\n- Physical recovery times stop matching the established time frame.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Breaks causal logic.\n- Makes N01 feel like a vague mush instead of a sharp sequence.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Use `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` while revising:\n  - For each chapter, annotate: Tide, phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n- Look for:\n  - Inconsistent references (“weeks later” in a novella that spans ~5 Tides).\n  - Overly fast healing that isn’t flagged as anomalous (only Chloen’s leg is special).\n\n**Fix:**\n- Translate casual time:\n  - “Days later” → “A few Tides later.”\n  - “Weeks later” → “Several Anneals later” (only if supported by timeline skeleton; N01 is tight).\n- Explicitly anchor big beats:\n  - “By the second Tide after the Piercing…” etc.\n\n---\n# 【14†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file14\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n# 【15†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file15\nContent Snippet:\n- She is more interested in whether they’ll work *with* her network, not for any office.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Salee may:\n  - Know roughly that unusual abilities exist.\n  - Hear rumors about Jhace/Tiffani’s work as anonymous heroes.\n- She may NOT:\n  - Know their names or detailed power mechanics.\n  - Recognize them as long-term linchpins of the world.\n  - Have any direct awareness of cosmic-scale stakes.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Warm, practical, unsentimental.\n- Uses humor to keep people moving:  \n  - e.g., joking about “promotions” meaning “you get to cook instead of dig latrines today.”\n- Never airy or idealistic; everything is grounded in logistics and bodies.\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends with an action, not a speech:  \n  - e.g., Salee standing to go negotiate a food redistribution or shelter swap.\n- The last feeling should be: “this is what real work looks like from the ground.”\n\n---\n\n### 3. EPILOGUE — AUDITOR\n\n**Placement:**  \n- After Chapter 15, after both Interludes.  \n- Final text object in N01.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Claustrophically controlled perspective labeled as **The Auditor**.\n- Voice is clinical, observational, almost report-like.\n- Absolutely no emotional self-exposure.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Abstract / unlocated.  \n  - No specific physical room, city, or body described.\n  - Focus on data, trends, and projections.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 600–800 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Meta Assessment”** (post-season scouting report).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Detachment throughout.  \n  - Subtle tilt from “baseline” to “increased interest.”\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Re-frame N01 events as observed data:\n   - “Subject Torrins: capacity level, usage frequency, observed cost accumulation.”\n   - “Merrow: analytical optimization of capacity.”\n   - “Population response patterns in Saint Merro under stress.”\n2. Imply there is a *system* watching:\n   - References to baselines, projections, thresholds.\n   - Mentions of “seal integrity” and “breach probability” as abstract metrics.\n3. Confirm that:\n   - Jhace’s Stage 1 baseline is locked in by end of N01.\n   - Partnership with Tiffani statistically increases survival of future events.\n   - The four key mortals (Corlexi, Salee, and two others off-page) are now positioned as significant variables.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- The Auditor may:\n  - Use terms like “subject,” “vessel,” “seal integrity” in a sterile way.\n  - Refer to Tides beyond N01 only in probability terms (“projected failure within Nxx Tides”).\n- The Auditor may NOT:\n  - Explain what seals are in mythic or narrative terms.\n  - Name any god/principle explicitly.\n  - Spoil specific future events (no “Tiffani’s death,” “Fathom Break,” etc.).\n  - Reveal motives, only objectives and metrics.\n\n**Tone & Style:**\n- Dry, report-like, almost inhuman.\n- Uses numeric language sparingly but effectively:\n  - e.g., “Seal integrity: 87%. Projected breach threshold: <60%.”\n- No metaphor. No simile. Zero poetic flourish.\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Final line should be a quiet escalation, not a twist:  \n  - e.g., “Observation will continue.”  \n  - Or: “Deviation from baseline now statistically significant.”\n\n---\n# 【16†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file16\nContent Snippet:\nMany escape.  But the node still falls on the poor side when Jhace’s body gives out.  Several die.  Jhace staggers and vomits from the strain.  Tiffani catches him.  They realize he has crossed into Stage 2: tremor no longer fades; vein‑lines are visible at rest.\n4. **Aftermath:**  Exhausted, they see RRO officials count the wealthy district as “saved” while blaming the poor district’s collapse on “structural weakness.”  Anger simmers.\n\n**Exit Hook:** As Jhace leans on Tiffani to walk away, he feels his muscles twitch uncontrollably.  Tiffani whispers, “You can’t keep doing dual holds.  We need to find another way.”  The note hangs unresolved.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Dual holds cost exponentially more.  His body cannot sustain that long.  He is essentially at Stage 2.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Emotional states (desperation vs. anger) influence cost.  She must research further and find ways to share load among multiple users.\n* **Reader learns:** Stage progression is real; the cost curve spikes; not all crises can end in full success.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show intense cost.  Keep crisis grounded in physical description.  Use voice rules.  Do not include any hints of gods or future events.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 15: “The Grip That Holds”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 5, Quench\n* **Location:** Quiet rooftop/back‑room (similar to Ch 13)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Post‑Ch 14 crisis, Jhace is nearly unconscious.  Tiffani helps him to a quiet space.  She is equally exhausted but must check his health.\n2. **Recovery:** They wash his hands in a basin.  His fingers twitch uncontrollably.  She traces the faint glowing vein‑lines.  He winces.  They speak little; the exhaustion is bone‑deep.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani opens up about Marisse, finally telling the full story.  Jhace listens silently, then confesses his constant guilt list.  They share their deepest pain.  There is no humor here, only honesty.  They hold each other.  Physical closeness is comfort, not sexual.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** Tiffani says, “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  Jhace replies, “Then we hold.”  They reaffirm the decision made in Ch 13 with more gravity.  The title line “The Grip That Holds” resonates: they will be the grip.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, bells ring again.  The crisis isn’t over.  Jhace tries to stand; his legs shake.  Tiffani steadies him.  They go back out together, not knowing what comes next.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Sharing his guilt does not make him weak.  He can lean on someone and still be strong.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can face her past head‑on.  Her fear of missing patterns need not isolate her.\n* **Reader learns:** This partnership is fully formed.  They are ready to face new stakes together.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Deep exhaustion and honest dialogue.  No flippant humor.  Do not foreshadow Tiffani’s death.  Keep the focus on their present commitment.\n\n---\n# 【17†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file17\nContent Snippet:\n**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n\n* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n\n---\n\n### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n\nEach POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n\n* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n\n* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n\nThe companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n\n**Key validation steps include:**\n\n1. Search for forbidden lexicon terms and replace with Te’Oga equivalents.\n2. Verify each Soulpulse use describes activation, cost and aftermath.\n3. Ensure voice matches the POV character throughout.\n4. Confirm knowledge gates are respected; remove any meta leakage.\n5. Check beat and structural adherence (no forbidden sequences).\n6. Cross‑reference the timeline skeleton for Tide/phase consistency and progression of symptoms/relationships.\n\n**Revision principle:** Fix the smallest possible section.  Retain user‑specified events and decisions.  Adjust description, not outcome, unless the outcome itself violates a constraint.\n\n---\n\n**Scope Note:** These rules apply only to N01.  Later novellas will introduce new lexicon terms, knowledge allowances, cost behaviors, and beat structures.  Do not carry these constraints forward without verifying updated documentation.\n# 【18†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file18\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 INTERLUDES & EPILOGUE — SPECIFICATION\n\n**Purpose:** Define exactly what the two Interludes and Epilogue in N01 must do, what they are allowed to reveal, and how they must relate to the 15 core chapters.\n\nThese are structural components, not side content. They carry specific jobs and must obey all N01 constraints.\n\n---\n\n### 1. INTERLUDE I — CORLEXI\n\n**Placement:**  \n- Between Chapter 6 and Chapter 7.  \n- Reader must have just experienced:  \n  - Jhace’s first major sustained Soulpulse hold.  \n  - Tiffani’s transition from “analysis only” to active coordination in crisis.  \n  - Physical and emotional cost on Jhace beginning to show clearly.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Third-person limited, anchored to **Corlexi** only.\n- No access to any other character’s internal thoughts.\n- No omniscient commentary, no divine perspective.\n\n**Setting:**\n- RRO offices in the Crown district; primary scene: council chamber and adjacent planning rooms.\n- City still in early crisis-management phase (Tide 1–2).\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 800–1,200 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“System vs Chaos Snapshot”** (maps closest to BEAT_03 but in institutional context).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Frustration at chaos and lack of coordination.  \n  - Conviction that order and centralized authority are morally necessary.  \n  - Foreshadowing of friction with decentralized actors (Jhace, Salee) without naming them yet.\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Show how the crisis looks from the top of the city:\n   - Maps, ledgers, resource tables.\n   - Reports streaming in faster than they can be processed.\n   - Triage discussions framed as logistics, not personal stories.\n2. Establish Corlexi’s worldview:\n   - “People die when you let everyone improvise.”\n   - Belief: systems, rules, and centralized authority save more lives overall.\n   - He is not a cartoon villain; he is a serious, pragmatic operator.\n3. Highlight institutional blind spots:\n   - Preference for protecting higher districts first (Crown, Glass Quarter).\n   - Lack of real understanding of conditions in Dregs/Underface.\n   - Tension with guilds, who resist ceding control.\n4. Seed political conflict:\n   - Early, not-yet-personal discussions about “unregistered abilities” and “unlicensed structural interventions.”\n   - Concerns that people like Jhace (unnamed) are “creating new risk surfaces” by acting outside protocol.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Corlexi may:\n  - Know that some individuals are doing impossible things with structures.\n  - Have early reports of “copper-gold light” phenomena.\n  - Consider registering these people and assigning them.\n- He may NOT:\n  - Understand true Soulpulse mechanics.\n  - Know anything about gods, seals, or long-term cosmic stakes.\n  - Anticipate Tiffani/Jhace specifically as major figures.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Controlled, measured, methodical.\n- Dry irritation rather than melodrama.\n- Occasional dark bureaucratic humor acceptable (e.g., jokes about “running out of ledgers before people”).\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends on a decision or proposal:  \n  - e.g., “We need a registry of these people. Without it, they are a variable we cannot control.”\n- This sets up the Chapter 11 registration confrontation in advance.\n\n---\n\n### 2. INTERLUDE II — SALEE\n# 【19†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn13file19\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani helps him to a quiet space.  She is equally exhausted but must check his health.\n2. **Recovery:** They wash his hands in a basin.  His fingers twitch uncontrollably.  She traces the faint glowing vein‑lines.  He winces.  They speak little; the exhaustion is bone‑deep.\n3. **Intimacy:** Tiffani opens up about Marisse, finally telling the full story.  Jhace listens silently, then confesses his constant guilt list.  They share their deepest pain.  There is no humor here, only honesty.  They hold each other.  Physical closeness is comfort, not sexual.\n4. **Ongoing Commitment:** Tiffani says, “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  Jhace replies, “Then we hold.”  They reaffirm the decision made in Ch 13 with more gravity.  The title line “The Grip That Holds” resonates: they will be the grip.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Outside, bells ring again.  The crisis isn’t over.  Jhace tries to stand; his legs shake.  Tiffani steadies him.  They go back out together, not knowing what comes next.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Sharing his guilt does not make him weak.  He can lean on someone and still be strong.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can face her past head‑on.  Her fear of missing patterns need not isolate her.\n* **Reader learns:** This partnership is fully formed.  They are ready to face new stakes together.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Deep exhaustion and honest dialogue.  No flippant humor.  Do not foreshadow Tiffani’s death.  Keep the focus on their present commitment.\n\n---\n\n### Epilogue – “Auditor’s Report”\n\n* **POV:** The Auditor (clinical, omniscient observer)\n* **Beat Method:** **Meta Assessment** (see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md`)\n* **Timeline:** Immediately after Ch 15\n* **Content Overview:**\n  * Logs subject Torrins’ baseline capacity, cost accumulation and Stage status (Stage 1 solidly established; Stage 2 approach identified).\n  * Notes Merrow’s analytical influence on optimizing Soulpulse usage and increasing survival probability.\n  * Lists positions of the four key mortals (Corlexi, Salee and two others unseen) as relevant variables.\n  * Provides seal integrity percentage: 87% (down from 100% at the Piercing).  Predicts further degradation within a few Anneals.  Does not explain what seals are.\n  * Concludes with “Observation will continue.”\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Reader sees:** Someone or something is monitoring events clinically.  There are metrics (seal integrity, vessel positioning) unknown to characters.  Stakes are larger than the protagonists realize.\n* **Forbidden:** The Auditor must not reveal the full nature of seals, the identities of gods, or what will happen.  It may use technical language (subject, baseline, projection) but remains opaque.\n\n---\n\n**Scope Note:**  This chapter grid is final for N01.  Any deviations must be reflected here with updated beat assignments, timeline positions and knowledge changes.  Do not insert new chapters or reorder without a revision.  Later novellas will get their own grids.\nVisible: 0% - 100%",

            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file14 in your response to cite this file. ",
            "## CONSTRAINT SYSTEMS: N01 ENFORCEMENT\n\n**Purpose:** Lay down the rules that govern language, magic costs, knowledge and structure in N01. These are mechanical constraints the prose engine must obey to preserve immersion, mystery and pacing.  Violating them breaks the story.\n\n---\n\n### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n\n**No Earth terms for time, distance, direction, measurement or culture.**  Te’Oga has its own units.  If any of the forbidden terms appear in prose, the validator must flag them and the draft must be revised.\n\n#### Forbidden Time Terms\n\n* Day, week, month, year, season names (spring, summer, fall/autumn, winter).\n* Days of week (Monday, Tuesday, etc.).\n* Hour, minute, second, o’clock, AM, PM, midnight, noon.\n\n#### Forbidden Distance/Direction Terms\n\n* Foot, feet, inch, yard, mile, meter, kilometre, centimetre.\n* North, south, east, west, NE, NW, SE, SW, compass.\n\n#### Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\n* Pound, ounce, ton, tonne, gram, kilogram.\n* Gallon, quart, pint, litre, ml.\n* Celsius, Fahrenheit, degrees.\n\n#### Forbidden Modern Slang and Culture\n\n* “Okay,” “OK,” “cool,” “awesome,” “dude,” “guy” when used as slang or filler.\n* Any real‑world religions, mythologies, historical events, or geographical names.\n\n#### Required Te’Oga Terms\n\n* **Time:** Tide (~24 hours), Anneal (~5 Tides), Crucible (~35 Tides), Turn (personal age), Cast (historical epoch).  Within a Tide: Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench.\n* **Short Duration:** Span (seconds to minutes), breath (moment), heartbeat (instant).\n* **Distance:** Span (arm’s length), pace (step), pole (~10 feet).\n* **Direction:** Up‑slope (toward the Crown), down‑slope (toward the river), harbor‑side, inland, or landmarks.\n* **Volume:** Thimble (small), cup (container), cask (large).\n* **Weight:** Grain (tiny), stone (heavy), weight (formal unit).\n\n#### Acceptable Substitutions\n\n* “Yes,” “understood,” “fine,” “agreed” instead of “OK/cool/awesome.”\n* “Person,” “man,” “woman,” “worker,” “apprentice” instead of modern slang like “dude” or “guy.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. COST VISIBILITY PROTOCOL\n\nSoulpulse is not free.  Every activation must show physical and emotional cost.  The story’s magic system has real consequences, and the prose must reflect them.\n\n#### During Activation\n\n* Show skin contact and the emotional trigger: the user must need the material to hold to save someone.  Casual attempts fail.\n* Describe the copper‑gold glow spreading from the palms into the material and how the material responds (shimmers, redistributes stress).\n* Highlight the user’s physical strain: shaking hands, muscles tensing, labored breathing, sweat, nausea.\n\n#### Immediately After\n\n* Show residual tremor (worse/same/better).  At Stage 1, tremor may fade with rest; by Stage 2, it doesn’t.\n* Note visibility of vein‑lines (bright lines when active, faint lines at rest in Stage 2).\n* Convey exhaustion: inability to lift arms, dizziness, headache, bone‑deep ache.  Sleep may help early on but not later.\n* Include pain location and quality (burning in palms, ice‑cold pain along forearm, dull ache in chest).\n\n#### Stage‑Progression Consistency\n\n* Stage 0: no tremor, no vein‑lines; only applies before Ch 3.\n* Stage 1: tremor appears after ~2–3 uses; fades partially with rest.  Vein‑lines visible during use only.  Applies through Ch\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]

          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "Short version:  \nThe “list of names” is **meant to be the same thing as his later canon guilt-list**, not a brand-new pile of off-screen tragedies.\n\nMore detail, point by point:\n\n1. **What I intended with “list of names”**  \n   - It’s meant as an *early seed* of what the Chapter Grid later calls his **“constant guilt list”** that he finally confesses to Tiffani in Ch 15. fileciteturn5file0  \n   - At Tide 0 / Chapter 1, that list is **anchored on the building collapse that killed his parents** and everyone else in that structure. The backstory only commits to that collapse as his defining early trauma. fileciteturn5file8  \n   - Over his career, he’s also seen bodies come out of other failures he couldn’t prevent, even if they weren’t *his* engineering mistakes. The line is meant to capture that accumulated guilt without specifying extra, canon-breaking catastrophes.\n\n   So: it’s not “oh by the way, Jhace has secretly caused three other mass deaths we never mention.” It’s “this man catalogues the dead obsessively, starting with the collapse that made him who he is.”\n\n2. **Canon tension you’re sensing**  \n   You’re right to flag that if Chapter 1 sounds like he’s already carrying *multiple* preventable-death events that are clearly “his fault,” that drifts away from the current dossiers, which only hard-anchor the childhood collapse as his formative catastrophe. fileciteturn5file8  \n\n   To stay tight with canon, any names beyond his parents’ building at this point should be:\n   - People he couldn’t reach in time,\n   - Or people lost to broader city failures,\n   - Not “more buildings he personally mis-rated and walked away from.”\n\n3. **Best fix going forward**  \n   If that specific line feels like it implies extra pre-N01 failures, I’d tune it to something more explicit, e.g.:\n\n   > *He still carried the names from that collapse — every one — even if he never spoke them.*\n\n   or if you want the count:\n\n   > *He still carried all twenty-three names from that collapse, even if he never spoke them.*\n\n   That keeps:\n   - The **guilt-list motif** (so Ch 15’s confession is an expansion, not a surprise retcon),  \n   - While pinning it cleanly to the one canonized event we know about at this point.\n\n4. **On your other three notes quickly:**\n\n   - **“Sir”** – agreed, that’s Earth-coded and wrong for Te’Oga. For guild/apprentice you want something like **“Master Torrins”**, **“Master Jhace”**, or just his name, depending on how formal you want her in that moment.\n   - **“Hum” repetition** – yeah, that’s just polish. Easy to swap some instances for *vibration / thrum / resonance* while keeping Jhace-voice concrete.\n   - **Junction numbering clarity** – also agree this needs a tiny anchor phrase so “seventh junction” is clearly “the seventh along today’s route, not the first weird one.”\n\nIf you’d like, I can do a tiny surgical pass on those four spots in your working Ch 1 text (no structural changes, just line swaps) and output a clean, canon-tight version in the 800–1300 span."
          ]
        },
