          "result": "# 【0†N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md†file-6ERDNSoJumE8RYbV3j8rJJ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file0\nContent Snippet:\nDeliberately pass each line of dialogue through that filter before locking the chapter.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Knowledge Gate Breaks (Characters Knowing Too Much)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Jhace internally calls his condition “Stage 2.”\n- Characters refer to “gods,” “seals,” or any series-end machinery.\n- Someone predicts a very specific future event.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Collapses the slow-burn reveal structure of the whole saga.\n- Makes the world feel rigged instead of discovered.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Search for:\n  - “stage 2 / stage three / stage four / advanced stage”\n  - “god / divine / destiny / prophecy / chosen”\n  - Concrete statements about future events (“Tiffani will never die,” etc.)\n- Check if any POV has information they could only have from outside the story.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Replace formal labels with experiential description:\n  - “Stage 2” → “The tremor never fully left now, even when he slept.”\n- Keep existential questions open:\n  - “He didn’t know what this power was, only that each use left more of him behind.”\n- Remove or soften predictions:\n  - “This feels like it will matter” → “He had no idea if any of this would matter later; right now it just hurt.”\n\n---\n\n### 5. Beat Patterning (Predictable Rhythm)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Chapters follow the same emotional structure repeatedly:\n  - e.g., “quiet → disaster → near-death → tidy win” on loop.\n- Or specific beat sequences repeat (1→3, 1→3, 1→3).\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Reader starts predicting the shape subconsciously.\n- Stakes feel fake because nothing *structural* ever surprises.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Label each chapter’s beat type using `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`.\n- Scan sequence for:\n  - Same beat repeated back-to-back.\n  - Same pair of beats repeated multiple times in the novella.\n  - Extended runs of high-intensity beats without a breather.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Swap beat types where functionally equivalent:\n  - Convert a “Trial→Failure→Compounding→Success/Cost” into a “Crisis Front-Loaded→Rolling Complications→Partial Resolution.”\n- Insert quieter recalibration chapters between major crises.\n- Use Interludes strategically as breathers and reframing devices.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Timeline Drift\n\n**Failure:**\n- Events that should happen on Tide 0 end up implied days later.\n- Characters refer to “days ago” when only a few Tides have passed.\n- Physical recovery times stop matching the established time frame.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Breaks causal logic.\n- Makes N01 feel like a vague mush instead of a sharp sequence.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Use `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` while revising:\n  - For each chapter, annotate: Tide, phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n- Look for:\n  - Inconsistent references (“weeks later” in a novella that spans ~5 Tides).\n  - Overly fast healing that isn’t flagged as anomalous (only Chloen’s leg is special).\n\n**Fix:**\n- Translate casual time:\n  - “Days later” → “A few Tides later.”\n  - “Weeks later” → “Several Anneals later” (only if supported by timeline skeleton; N01 is tight).\n- Explicitly anchor big beats:\n  - “By the second Tide after the Piercing…” etc.\n\n---\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file1\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### Chapter 3: “The Sky Breaks”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 0, Shaping→Striking; this is the Piercing event\n* **Primary Locations:** Underface tunnels, surface streets near forge district, collapsing observatory\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** The narrative drops into full crisis.  The Piercing begins.  The ground shakes; the tunnel walls groan.  Dust falls.  Heat‑grid pipes scream.  Jhace shouts orders; Bram and Chloen scramble.  Tiffani experiences the same event at the observatory; glass shatters.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  A section of tunnel collapses.  Chloen is pinned under a beam.  Jhace cannot lift it physically.  Above, the observatory structure begins to tear.  Tiffani must evacuate quickly, navigating falling glass and buckling floors.\n3. **First Soulpulse Use:**  Out of desperation, Jhace grabs the beam with bare hands and *wills* it to hold.  Copper‑gold light blooms.  Heat rushes through him.  He feels the structure’s stress.  The beam holds long enough for Bram to pull Chloen free.  Immediately, his hands ache; a faint tremor starts.  He doesn’t understand what he’s done.\n4. **Second Use:**  Seconds later, the street above begins to collapse.  People are trapped.  Jhace uses the new power again, bracing a support from below.  Copper‑gold light flares.  Tiffani, now on the street, sees a building sag then stop.  She glimpses the glow and hears the metallic song of the beam being forced back into place.\n5. **Partial Resolution:**  People evacuate.  The building doesn’t fully collapse.  Jhace collapses from the exertion.  His hands shake uncontrollably.  He blacks out.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace wakes to chaos and sees Tiffani standing over him with a look of shock and curiosity.  They exchange a brief, bewildered glance.  The world is different now.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** He can do something impossible when protecting someone.  It costs him physically (ache, tremor).  It feels like heat and vibration.\n* **Tiffani learns:** People can manipulate structures with copper‑gold light.  She has no explanation yet but she’s determined to find one.\n* **Reader learns:** The Piercing is world‑changing; Soulpulse exists; it is tied to desperation and contact; it leaves residue (glow, tremor, healing).  Chloen’s leg heals quickly.\n* **Hidden:** Why this power exists, what the Piercing was, any link to gods or divine principles.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Full crisis intensity.  Show cost: aching hands, tremor, exhaustion.  Use sensory language (dust, noise).  Keep voices distinct (Jhace terse, Tiffani more observational).  Do not name the ability yet; characters just describe what they see.\n\n---\n\n### Interlude I (After Chapter 6) – Corlexi “System vs Chaos”\n\n* See `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` for details.  This interlude shows the RRO council chamber through Corlexi’s eyes.  It contrasts institutional attempts at order with the ground‑level chaos experienced in the first six chapters.  It seeds the registration conflict.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n# 【2†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file2\nContent Snippet:\n* **Jhace learns:** He can admit feelings without losing focus.  Partnership is his new normal.\n* **Tiffani learns:** She can rely on someone without betraying her standards.  She will not lose her individuality by loving.\n* **Reader learns:** The romance is explicit now.  There is no “will they/won’t they.”  They will; they just haven’t yet.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Keep physical intimacy low due to exhaustion.  Use humor and vulnerability.  No Soulpulse here, so no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 14: “Fault Lines”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution** (third and final use in N01)\n* **Timeline:** Tide 4–5, Shaping→Striking\n* **Location:** Edge of two districts (load‑bearing node between a wealthier and a poorer zone)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Alarm bells and runners call Jhace and Tiffani to a massive structural failure at a district boundary.  A bridge‑like load‑bearing node connecting two zones is cracking.  If it falls, dozens of buildings on either side will tumble.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  They arrive to find RRO crews pulling back to protect the wealthy side.  Poor families on the other side cannot evacuate.  The failure is at multiple points.  Jhace tries a dual hold for the first time: two contact points on two different beams.  The cost surges; his legs tremble; his vision blurs.  Tiffani directs him, but there are too many variables.  Bram shouts warnings.  Salee’s people try to evacuate as many as possible.\n3. **Partial Resolution:**  They slow the collapse.  Many escape.  But the node still falls on the poor side when Jhace’s body gives out.  Several die.  Jhace staggers and vomits from the strain.  Tiffani catches him.  They realize he has crossed into Stage 2: tremor no longer fades; vein‑lines are visible at rest.\n4. **Aftermath:**  Exhausted, they see RRO officials count the wealthy district as “saved” while blaming the poor district’s collapse on “structural weakness.”  Anger simmers.\n\n**Exit Hook:** As Jhace leans on Tiffani to walk away, he feels his muscles twitch uncontrollably.  Tiffani whispers, “You can’t keep doing dual holds.  We need to find another way.”  The note hangs unresolved.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** Dual holds cost exponentially more.  His body cannot sustain that long.  He is essentially at Stage 2.\n* **Tiffani learns:** Emotional states (desperation vs. anger) influence cost.  She must research further and find ways to share load among multiple users.\n* **Reader learns:** Stage progression is real; the cost curve spikes; not all crises can end in full success.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show intense cost.  Keep crisis grounded in physical description.  Use voice rules.  Do not include any hints of gods or future events.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 15: “The Grip That Holds”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_08 – Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 5, Quench\n* **Location:** Quiet rooftop/back‑room (similar to Ch 13)\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Post‑Ch 14 crisis, Jhace is nearly unconscious.\n# 【3†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file3\nContent Snippet:\nShe logs refraction anomalies in the upper atmosphere.  Guild messengers arrive, urging her to focus on paid commissions, not “weather.”\n2. **External Conflict:** The pattern she has been dismissing as noise intensifies.  Measurements show light bending in ways she cannot explain.  The theodolite trembles under her hands.  She must decide whether to treat this as important or continue ignoring it to satisfy her guild.\n3. **Internal Shift:** She chooses to trust the pattern.  She notes the anomalies and begins adjusting instruments to track them.  She accepts that ignoring “noise” killed Marisse; she won’t repeat that mistake.\n4. **Relationship Beat:** Pella brings her food and comments on her work.  Tiffani feigns irritation but eats.  Their dynamic of patient mentor and caring apprentice is established.  Tiffani also thinks briefly of Corlexi, recalling past debates about “efficiency” vs. “precision.”\n5. **Soulpulse Use:** None.  This chapter is about observations, not magical interventions.\n\n**Exit Hook:** The anomaly spikes: a sharp flash and metallic taste in the air.  Glass instruments hum.  Tiffani sees something streaking high in the atmosphere, bending light.  She grabs her logbook and runs down to warn others.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Tiffani learns:** The atmospheric anomaly is real and escalating.  Patterns matter.  Her decision to take it seriously is moral atonement for past failure.\n* **Reader learns:** Tiffani’s personality (precision as care), her humor (dry but warm), her relationship with Pella, and the physical layout of the observatory.\n* **Hidden:** No Soulpulse knowledge, no cosmic explanation, no names of gods.  She doesn’t know what she saw.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Use correct units (“Tides,” “spans,” “Kindling”).  Tiffani’s voice should be analytical but caring.  No Soulpulse means no cost description.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 3: “The Sky Breaks”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_03 – Crisis Front‑Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 0, Shaping→Striking; this is the Piercing event\n* **Primary Locations:** Underface tunnels, surface streets near forge district, collapsing observatory\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** The narrative drops into full crisis.  The Piercing begins.  The ground shakes; the tunnel walls groan.  Dust falls.  Heat‑grid pipes scream.  Jhace shouts orders; Bram and Chloen scramble.  Tiffani experiences the same event at the observatory; glass shatters.\n2. **Rolling Complications:**  A section of tunnel collapses.  Chloen is pinned under a beam.  Jhace cannot lift it physically.  Above, the observatory structure begins to tear.  Tiffani must evacuate quickly, navigating falling glass and buckling floors.\n3. **First Soulpulse Use:**  Out of desperation, Jhace grabs the beam with bare hands and *wills* it to hold.  Copper‑gold light blooms.  Heat rushes through him.  He feels the structure’s stress.  The beam holds long enough for Bram to pull Chloen free.  Immediately, his hands ache; a faint tremor starts.  He doesn’t understand what he’s done.\n4. **Second Use:**  Seconds later, the street above begins to collapse.  People are trapped.  Jhace uses the new power again, bracing a support from below.  Copper‑gold light flares.\n# 【4†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file4\nContent Snippet:\n#### 1.3 Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- pound / lb / lbs / ounce / oz / ton / tonne  \n- gram / g / kilogram / kg  \n- gallon / quart / liter / litre / ml  \n- Celsius / Fahrenheit / °C / °F / degree / degrees\n\nConfirm weights/volumes are expressed via:\n\n- Grain, Stone, Weight, Thimble, Cup (non-precise), Cask.\n\n#### 1.4 Modern Slang and Cultural Leakage\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- okay / OK / ok  \n- cool / awesome / dude / guy (as generic modern slang)  \n- any mention of real-world religions, mythologies, countries, historical events, or technologies that do not exist in Te’Oga.\n\nConfirm substitutions:\n\n- “Yes,” “Understood,” “Agreed,” “Fine,” etc., instead of “okay / cool / awesome.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. SOULPULSE COST AND MECHANICS\n\nFor every Soulpulse activation in the text:\n\n#### 2.1 Activation Requirements\n\n- Is the user in **direct contact** with the material?  \n- Is their **emotional state** clearly protective/desperate rather than casual?  \n- Is it clear **what** they are trying to hold/stabilize/mend?\n\nIf any of these are missing, revise.\n\n#### 2.2 During-Use Description\n\n- Copper-gold glow is explicitly described.  \n- Glow spread pattern into material is described (even briefly).  \n- Material response is visible (shimmer, tension, redistribution).  \n- Physical strain in the user is shown (tremor, muscle tension, breath changes).\n\nIf an activation reads “he used his power and the beam held” with no detail, it fails.\n\n#### 2.3 Immediate Aftermath\n\n- Tremor status is shown (worse / same / slightly improved).  \n- Vein-line visibility is described (bright, fading, faintly present at rest).  \n- Exhaustion is concrete (struggling to lift something, dizziness, needing support).  \n- Pain is localized and characterized (hands, arms, chest; burning, aching, stabbing, etc.).\n\nIf cost is vague (“he was tired”), it must be sharpened.\n\n#### 2.4 Stage Progression Consistency\n\nCross-check with `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md` and `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`:\n\n- If before Ch 3: **Stage 0** (no tremor, no vein-lines).  \n- Ch 3–11: **Stage 1** (tremor intermittent → persistent, vein-lines during use only).  \n- Ch 12–15: Approaching **Stage 2** (baseline tremor, faint vein-lines at rest, deeper exhaustion).\n\nIf the prose implies Stage 2 symptoms too early, or Stage 0 behavior too late, fix it.\n\n---\n\n### 3. POV AND VOICE\n\n#### 3.1 Deep POV Integrity\n\nFor the assigned POV character:\n\n- No information appears that the POV couldn’t reasonably sense, infer, or know.  \n- No omniscient commentary explaining divine mechanics, future events, or other POVs’ inner states.  \n- Descriptions stay grounded in the POV’s experiences and biases.\n\n#### 3.2 Character-Specific Voice\n\nFor the POV, cross-check with `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n\n**Jhace POV:**\n\n- Sentences trend shorter, especially under stress.  \n- Language focuses on physical sensation, structure, vibration, weight, pressure.  \n- Humor is mostly unintentional or awkward.  \n- Internal monologue is blunt, practical, guilt-laced.\n\n**Tiffani POV:**\n# 【5†README.md†file-UHeggNd7WQHssWb89Y8SgW】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: README.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file5\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 – The Grip That Holds – Canonical Pack\n\nThis folder contains all canonical and structural files necessary for writing and revising the novella **N01: \"The Grip That Holds\"** in the Confluence Chronicles saga.\n\n## Files included\n\n- **World_Core.md** – Detailed description of Te’Oga world, city geography, Piercing event and effects, technology, social structures, time and measurement systems, and lexicon enforcement.\n- **Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md** – Empirical explanation of Soulpulse as understood during N01, including activation requirements, manifestations, cost stages, techniques, training observations, and limitations.\n- **Character_Dossiers_N01.md** – Psychological profiles, voice patterns, humor styles, and arcs for all characters appearing in N01: Jhace, Tiffani, Bram, and key supporting characters.\n- **Location_Bibles_N01.md** – Physical descriptions and functional details for locations in N01, including Underface tunnels, Saint Merro districts, RRO chambers, observatory, training rig, and typical crisis sites.\n- **Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md** – Tracks relationship arcs and phases (Jhace & Tiffani, Jhace & Bram, Tiffani & Pella, etc.) and maps character connections across chapters.\n- **Constraint_Systems.md** – Rules to enforce the Te’Oga lexicon, visible Soulpulse cost, knowledge gates, beat unpredictability, voice consistency, and validation—essential for maintaining continuity and immersion.\n- **N01_Master_Bible.md** – Comprehensive story bible summarising the novella overview, world state, characters, Soulpulse mechanics, relationships, chapter structure, themes, and constraints.\n- **N01_Chapter_Grid.md** – Beat-by-beat breakdown of all 15 chapters, plus interludes and epilogue, including scene structure, knowledge changes, and exit hooks.\n- **N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md** – Step-by-step instructions for drafting and revising prose, including modes (draft, revision, interlude, epilogue), common setup, and guidelines for structure and voice.\n- **N01_Validator_Checklist.md** – Mechanical validation checklist for any prose (chapter, interlude, or epilogue), ensuring lexicon compliance, cost visibility, voice discipline, knowledge gates, structural integrity, and timeline continuity.\n- **Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md** – Definitions and structures of all beat methods used in N01, with emotional arcs, best uses, and forbidden sequences.\n- **N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md** – Tide-by-Tide timeline for N01, listing event anchors, chapter phases, Soulpulse progression, relationship states by chapter, major events, and update rules.\n- **N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md** – Detailed guidelines for writing Interlude I, Interlude II, and the epilogue, specifying POV, tone, length, structure, allowed knowledge, and exit hooks.\n- **N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md** – A list of common mistakes in prose generation and validation, with detection methods and suggested fixes.\n- **N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md** – Concise tables summarising character presence per chapter, Soulpulse usage and cost by chapter, primary locations by chapter, and a tide-level timeline snapshot.\n\n## Notes\n\n- The orchestration notes (for human planners) are **not** included in this package. \n- The documents here represent the complete canonical and structural foundation for N01 without any meta-guidance. \n- Use these files to maintain consistency and fidelity when drafting or revising the novella.\n# 【6†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file6\nContent Snippet:\n5. **Partial Resolution:**  People evacuate.  The building doesn’t fully collapse.  Jhace collapses from the exertion.  His hands shake uncontrollably.  He blacks out.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace wakes to chaos and sees Tiffani standing over him with a look of shock and curiosity.  They exchange a brief, bewildered glance.  The world is different now.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** He can do something impossible when protecting someone.  It costs him physically (ache, tremor).  It feels like heat and vibration.\n* **Tiffani learns:** People can manipulate structures with copper‑gold light.  She has no explanation yet but she’s determined to find one.\n* **Reader learns:** The Piercing is world‑changing; Soulpulse exists; it is tied to desperation and contact; it leaves residue (glow, tremor, healing).  Chloen’s leg heals quickly.\n* **Hidden:** Why this power exists, what the Piercing was, any link to gods or divine principles.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Full crisis intensity.  Show cost: aching hands, tremor, exhaustion.  Use sensory language (dust, noise).  Keep voices distinct (Jhace terse, Tiffani more observational).  Do not name the ability yet; characters just describe what they see.\n\n---\n\n### Interlude I (After Chapter 6) – Corlexi “System vs Chaos”\n\n* See `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` for details.  This interlude shows the RRO council chamber through Corlexi’s eyes.  It contrasts institutional attempts at order with the ground‑level chaos experienced in the first six chapters.  It seeds the registration conflict.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n# 【7†Location_Bibles_N01.md†file-2gPmtJKiin2h9WtfphNzQn】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Location_Bibles_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file7\nContent Snippet:\nSide rooms hold maps, ledgers, resource lists.  Wide arches lead to corridors and smaller offices.\n* **Environment:** Echoey; voices carry and blend into a dull roar when many speak.  Light from tall windows during day (Kindling/Pouring), supplemented by oil lamps.  Air cool and slightly musty.\n* **Social/Function:** Focal point for institutional authority.  Here, leaders like Corlexi push for registration and control.  Guild representatives, healers, and merchants argue policy.  The council tries to impose order but is bogged down by bureaucracy.\n* **Appearances:** Interlude I (Corlexi’s view), Chapter 11 (registration confrontation), Chapter 14 (emergency meeting in later crisis).\n\n---\n\n### MERROW GLASS & MIRRORS OBSERVATORY\n\n* **Location:** In the Glass Quarter.  Combined workshop (ground level), living space (upper level), and observation platform (roof).\n* **Physical:**\n  * Ground floor: kilns, annealing ovens, workbenches, storage for glass raw materials and tools.\n  * Upper level: living quarters and office.  Books and instruments share space with Tiffani’s simple bed and eating area.\n  * Rooftop observatory: a flat platform with a permanently mounted theodolite, logbook stands, and measurement marks etched into stone.  Protected by a low wall.\n* **Pre‑Piercing Use:** Tracking atmospheric and celestial patterns, testing glass quality, refining optical lenses.\n* **Post‑Piercing:** Collapse in Chapter 3 damages the upper structure.  The platform becomes dangerous, requiring careful salvage.\n* **Appearances:** Ch 2 (anomaly observations), Ch 3 (evacuation and collapse), Ch 5 (return to assess damage).\n\n---\n\n### TRAINING RIG LOCATION\n\n* **Purpose:** Controlled environment for teaching and testing Soulpulse techniques without endangering bystanders.\n* **Physical:** Outdoor courtyard or large covered workshop near the forge district.  Contains adjustable wooden and metal frames for holding beams.  Weighted stone blocks and metal bars replicate different loads.  Chalk marks on the ground indicate safe zones and viewing areas.\n* **Environment:** Loud when beams creak or collapse.  Dust from failures.  Light adequate via open sky or big windows.  No valuable structures nearby to damage.\n* **Appearances:** Ch 7 (setup), Ch 8 (Kylar’s failed first attempt), Ch 9 (discussion and re‑planning), Ch 10 (successful test).\n\n---\n\n### GENERIC CRISIS SITES\n\nThese are broad categories rather than a single location; each appears in multiple chapters.\n\n**Collapsed Residential Buildings**\n\n* Three‑ to four‑story mixed timber and stone structures.  Narrow staircases.  Many small rooms.  People trapped between floors.  Walls lean precariously; interior supports cracked.\n* Scenes: Mid‑novella, particularly Ch 6 and Ch 14.  Rescue operations require navigating confined spaces and stabilizing floors.\n\n**Failed Commercial Blocks**\n\n* Larger spaces (markets, workshops) with heavier roof beams.  Merchandise adds weight.  Complex load paths.  Collapse has wider area of effect.\n* Scenes: Smaller crises between major chapter beats; used to demonstrate the increasing complexity of problems as the story progresses.\n\n**Infrastructure Failures**\n\n* Bridges, major support pillars, heat‑grid marry points.  Failures here threaten multiple districts simultaneously.\n* Scenes: The district boundary collapse in Ch 14 is an example, where a load‑bearing node between two zones fails.\n\n**Common Elements Across Crisis Sites**\n# 【8†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file8\nContent Snippet:\nIf cost is vague (“he was tired”), it must be sharpened.\n\n#### 2.4 Stage Progression Consistency\n\nCross-check with `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md` and `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`:\n\n- If before Ch 3: **Stage 0** (no tremor, no vein-lines).  \n- Ch 3–11: **Stage 1** (tremor intermittent → persistent, vein-lines during use only).  \n- Ch 12–15: Approaching **Stage 2** (baseline tremor, faint vein-lines at rest, deeper exhaustion).\n\nIf the prose implies Stage 2 symptoms too early, or Stage 0 behavior too late, fix it.\n\n---\n\n### 3. POV AND VOICE\n\n#### 3.1 Deep POV Integrity\n\nFor the assigned POV character:\n\n- No information appears that the POV couldn’t reasonably sense, infer, or know.  \n- No omniscient commentary explaining divine mechanics, future events, or other POVs’ inner states.  \n- Descriptions stay grounded in the POV’s experiences and biases.\n\n#### 3.2 Character-Specific Voice\n\nFor the POV, cross-check with `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n\n**Jhace POV:**\n\n- Sentences trend shorter, especially under stress.  \n- Language focuses on physical sensation, structure, vibration, weight, pressure.  \n- Humor is mostly unintentional or awkward.  \n- Internal monologue is blunt, practical, guilt-laced.\n\n**Tiffani POV:**\n\n- Sentences can be longer, with precise analytical structure.  \n- Thinks in measurements, patterns, error margins.  \n- Humor is sharp, intentionally timed, often double-meaning.  \n- Internal monologue questions assumptions while sounding confident aloud.\n\n**Bram POV (if used):**\n\n- Longer, rambling sentences.  \n- Dry, resigned humor, gentle mockery.  \n- Commentary on systems and people more than technical details.\n\nIf the prose could belong to any random fantasy narrator, rewrite sections to restore voice specificity.\n\n---\n\n### 4. KNOWLEDGE GATES\n\n#### 4.1 What Characters Can Know (N01)\n\nConfirm the text does NOT:\n\n- Identify any character as a god or divine vessel.  \n- Explain seal mechanics or cosmic rules.  \n- Predict specific future events beyond immediate logical consequences.  \n- Label Soulpulse stages explicitly with “Stage 1,” “Stage 2,” etc., in character thought/dialogue.\n\nCharacters may:\n\n- Notice patterns in how leaders oppose/repel each other.  \n- Notice Soulpulse costs and progressive symptoms.  \n- Speculate (incorrectly or incompletely) about the phenomenon.\n\n#### 4.2 What Readers Can Know (N01)\n\nVia Interludes and Epilogue, readers may know:\n\n- There is some larger monitoring/observation entity (Auditor).  \n- The crisis is being tracked and evaluated.  \n- The four faction leaders matter unusually.\n\nBut the prose must NOT:\n\n- Reveal Auditor identity.  \n- Reveal divine mechanics.  \n- Reveal N05/N30 outcomes.\n\n---\n\n### 5. BEAT AND STRUCTURE COMPLIANCE\n\n#### 5.1 Beat Method Alignment\n\nCheck chapter’s assigned BEAT_0X in `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` and `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`:\n\n- The chapter clearly follows the structural steps of its beat.  \n- The emotional arc matches what the beat specifies.  \n- There is a coherent opening situation and a discernible change by the end.\n\nIf the chapter structure doesn’t match its beat, it must be revised or the grid must be updated.\n\n#### 5.2 Forbidden Sequences\n# 【9†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file9\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 INTERLUDES & EPILOGUE — SPECIFICATION\n\n**Purpose:** Define exactly what the two Interludes and Epilogue in N01 must do, what they are allowed to reveal, and how they must relate to the 15 core chapters.\n\nThese are structural components, not side content. They carry specific jobs and must obey all N01 constraints.\n\n---\n\n### 1. INTERLUDE I — CORLEXI\n\n**Placement:**  \n- Between Chapter 6 and Chapter 7.  \n- Reader must have just experienced:  \n  - Jhace’s first major sustained Soulpulse hold.  \n  - Tiffani’s transition from “analysis only” to active coordination in crisis.  \n  - Physical and emotional cost on Jhace beginning to show clearly.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Third-person limited, anchored to **Corlexi** only.\n- No access to any other character’s internal thoughts.\n- No omniscient commentary, no divine perspective.\n\n**Setting:**\n- RRO offices in the Crown district; primary scene: council chamber and adjacent planning rooms.\n- City still in early crisis-management phase (Tide 1–2).\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 800–1,200 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“System vs Chaos Snapshot”** (maps closest to BEAT_03 but in institutional context).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Frustration at chaos and lack of coordination.  \n  - Conviction that order and centralized authority are morally necessary.  \n  - Foreshadowing of friction with decentralized actors (Jhace, Salee) without naming them yet.\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Show how the crisis looks from the top of the city:\n   - Maps, ledgers, resource tables.\n   - Reports streaming in faster than they can be processed.\n   - Triage discussions framed as logistics, not personal stories.\n2. Establish Corlexi’s worldview:\n   - “People die when you let everyone improvise.”\n   - Belief: systems, rules, and centralized authority save more lives overall.\n   - He is not a cartoon villain; he is a serious, pragmatic operator.\n3. Highlight institutional blind spots:\n   - Preference for protecting higher districts first (Crown, Glass Quarter).\n   - Lack of real understanding of conditions in Dregs/Underface.\n   - Tension with guilds, who resist ceding control.\n4. Seed political conflict:\n   - Early, not-yet-personal discussions about “unregistered abilities” and “unlicensed structural interventions.”\n   - Concerns that people like Jhace (unnamed) are “creating new risk surfaces” by acting outside protocol.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Corlexi may:\n  - Know that some individuals are doing impossible things with structures.\n  - Have early reports of “copper-gold light” phenomena.\n  - Consider registering these people and assigning them.\n- He may NOT:\n  - Understand true Soulpulse mechanics.\n  - Know anything about gods, seals, or long-term cosmic stakes.\n  - Anticipate Tiffani/Jhace specifically as major figures.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Controlled, measured, methodical.\n- Dry irritation rather than melodrama.\n- Occasional dark bureaucratic humor acceptable (e.g., jokes about “running out of ledgers before people”).\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends on a decision or proposal:  \n  - e.g., “We need a registry of these people. Without it, they are a variable we cannot control.”\n- This sets up the Chapter 11 registration confrontation in advance.\n\n---\n\n### 2. INTERLUDE II — SALEE\n# 【10†N01_Validator_Checklist.md†file-3UfZE2ZB4K9AECBumE3d1d】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Validator_Checklist.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file10\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 VALIDATOR CHECKLIST\n\n**Purpose:**  \nProvide a strict, mechanical validation pass for any N01 prose (chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue) to enforce:\n\n- Lexicon purity.  \n- Soulpulse cost visibility.  \n- Voice and POV discipline.  \n- Knowledge gating.  \n- Beat structure.  \n- Timeline and continuity.\n\nThis checklist is intended to be run **after** a draft is written or revised.\n\n---\n\n### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n\n#### 1.1 Forbidden Time Terms\n\nSearch the prose for each of the following; if any appear, they must be replaced:\n\n- day / days  \n- week / weeks  \n- month / months  \n- year / years  \n- season / spring / summer / fall / autumn / winter  \n- Monday / Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday / Friday / Saturday / Sunday  \n- hour / minute / second  \n- AM / PM / o'clock / midnight / noon\n\nFor each hit, confirm there is a correct substitution:\n\n- Tide, Anneal, Crucible, Turn, Cast, Span, Breath, Heartbeat, phase names (Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench).\n\n#### 1.2 Forbidden Distance / Direction Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- foot / feet / ft  \n- inch / inches / in  \n- yard / yards  \n- mile / miles  \n- meter / metre / m / kilometer / km / centimeter / cm  \n- north / south / east / west / NE / NW / SE / SW / compass\n\nConfirm use of:\n\n- Span, Pace, Pole, upslope/downslope, river-side/harbor-side/inland, landmark-based directions.\n\n#### 1.3 Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- pound / lb / lbs / ounce / oz / ton / tonne  \n- gram / g / kilogram / kg  \n- gallon / quart / liter / litre / ml  \n- Celsius / Fahrenheit / °C / °F / degree / degrees\n\nConfirm weights/volumes are expressed via:\n\n- Grain, Stone, Weight, Thimble, Cup (non-precise), Cask.\n\n#### 1.4 Modern Slang and Cultural Leakage\n\nSearch and eliminate:\n\n- okay / OK / ok  \n- cool / awesome / dude / guy (as generic modern slang)  \n- any mention of real-world religions, mythologies, countries, historical events, or technologies that do not exist in Te’Oga.\n\nConfirm substitutions:\n\n- “Yes,” “Understood,” “Agreed,” “Fine,” etc., instead of “okay / cool / awesome.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. SOULPULSE COST AND MECHANICS\n\nFor every Soulpulse activation in the text:\n\n#### 2.1 Activation Requirements\n\n- Is the user in **direct contact** with the material?  \n- Is their **emotional state** clearly protective/desperate rather than casual?  \n- Is it clear **what** they are trying to hold/stabilize/mend?\n\nIf any of these are missing, revise.\n\n#### 2.2 During-Use Description\n\n- Copper-gold glow is explicitly described.  \n- Glow spread pattern into material is described (even briefly).  \n- Material response is visible (shimmer, tension, redistribution).  \n- Physical strain in the user is shown (tremor, muscle tension, breath changes).\n\nIf an activation reads “he used his power and the beam held” with no detail, it fails.\n\n#### 2.3 Immediate Aftermath\n# 【11†N01_Master_Bible.md†file-2mpb1yGtVMhZFr5PrWmbVN】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Master_Bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file11\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### SOULPULSE MECHANICS IN N01\n\nSummarized from `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n\n* **Activation:** Requires skin contact with the material and a desperate need to protect.  Casual attempts fail.\n* **Visual:** Copper‑gold glow spreads from the user’s hands into the material.  Vein‑lines appear on their arms.\n* **Cost:** The more you use, the worse the tremor and pain.  Stage 1 emerges after 2–3 uses (minor tremor).  By the end of the book Jhace’s tremor never fully fades and faint vein‑lines remain visible; he’s on the cusp of Stage 2.\n* **Unknowns:** Characters don’t know why Soulpulse exists, what powers it, or the risk of further stages.  They have no language for “Stage 3” or “Stage 4.”\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP SUMMARY\n\nFor full dynamics by chapter, see `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`.  Key arcs:\n\n* **Jhace & Tiffani:** From mutual misconceptions to professional respect to deep partnership and romantic acknowledgment.  They end the book committed to working together and being together but are physically too exhausted for sex.\n* **Jhace & Bram:** Mentorship and camaraderie.  Bram is protective but cannot stop Jhace; he can only slow the burn.\n* **Tiffani & Pella:** Mentor/student showing healthier pattern recognition habits.\n* **Jhace & Chloen:** Reluctant hero/hero‑worship, hinting at the next generation.\n* **J/T & Corlexi:** Brewing political conflict.  They resist registration; he sees them as unpredictable variables.\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER STRUCTURE AND INTERLUDES\n\n* N01 has **15 chapters**, each mapped to a beat from the `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` (Observation→Recognition, Crisis→Complication, etc.).  The precise mapping is in `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`.\n* **Interlude I** occurs after Ch 6 and shows Corlexi’s perspective.  It sets up the institutional response and seeds the registration conflict.\n* **Interlude II** comes after Ch 11 and shows Salee’s perspective.  It introduces communal networks and contrasts with RRO bureaucracy.\n* The **Epilogue** is a clinical report by the Auditor, a mysterious observer.  It logs data: Jhace’s Stage, partnership formation, positions of the four vessels, and seal integrity percentage (87% at N01 end).  It does not reveal motivations or cosmic truth.\n\n---\n\n### THEMES AND TONES\n\n* **Cost of Power:** Every miracle has a price.  Jhace’s hands shake by the end of the book; Tiffani goes without sleep; Bram regrets his compromises.\n* **Responsibility vs. Capacity:** You can’t save everyone.  The heroes grapple with guilt and triage decisions.  The story insists on hard choices.\n* **Partnership as Survival:** Neither Jhace nor Tiffani can succeed alone.  Collaboration is life‑saving.\n* **Institutional Control vs. Grassroots Response:** The RRO tries to regulate; guilds hoard knowledge; Salee’s networks share resources.  Tensions build.\n* **Precision as Empathy:** Tiffani’s standards are a form of care.  Bram’s jokes and Tiffani’s innuendo lighten darkness.\n* **Humor and Humanity:** There is room for jokes, awkward compliments and sex‑tinged banter even in tragedy.  It humanizes characters.\n\n---\n\n### CONSTRAINTS SUMMARY\n# 【12†N01_Master_Bible.md†file-2mpb1yGtVMhZFr5PrWmbVN】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Master_Bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file12\nContent Snippet:\n**The Piercing Event (Tide 0):**  A massive cylindrical object enters the atmosphere at an impossible angle and speeds, warping light as it passes.  It shears across the sky without touching the ground and continues into space.  Shockwaves shatter structures; dust and metallic particles rain down.  Many buildings collapse; hundreds die.  Traditional physics fails at the quantum level.  This is the inciting incident of the entire saga.\n\n**Post‑Piercing Conditions:**  The city is partially destroyed.  Institutions are overwhelmed.  Resources are scarce.  People are traumatized.  New abilities (Soulpulse) begin to emerge in roughly 1 in 500 individuals.  The heat‑grid, critical to winter survival, is failing.  Guilds fracture over how to respond.  The Regulatory and Reconstruction Office (RRO) forms to coordinate relief but quickly drifts toward control and bureaucracy.\n\n---\n\n### PRIMARY CHARACTERS\n\nSee `Character_Dossiers_N01.md` for full psychological profiles, humor styles and arcs.  A concise summary follows.\n\n#### Jhace Torrins\n\n* **Age:** 29 Turns; structural engineer and heat‑grid inspector.\n* **Backstory:** His parents died in a building collapse when he was 7.  Ever since, he has obsessed over preventing preventable deaths.  He works alone to avoid sharing his anxiety with others.  He refuses apprentices to keep his hypervigilance from infecting someone else.\n* **Soulpulse:** Instinctively activates during the Piercing.  Discovers it requires direct contact and protective desperation.  Learns cost (tremor, pain, exhaustion) as he uses it repeatedly.  Stage progression goes from baseline to Stage 1 through this novella.  He reaches the threshold of Stage 2 by the final chapters.\n* **Psychology:** Hypervigilant, guilt‑driven, physically oriented.  Terse, literal, thinks in terms of weight and vibration.  Hates being called a hero.  Lies to others (and himself) that he’s “fine.”  Needs to prevent death above all.\n* **Arc:** Moves from isolated vigilante to co‑lead in a partnership.  Learns to trust Tiffani’s analytical mind.  Accepts help.  Admits romantic feelings.  Commits to working together “when the world is broken.”\n\n#### Tiffani Merrow\n\n* **Age:** 27 Turns; precision glassworker, atmospheric observer.\n* **Backstory:** Her friend Marisse died in a glass‑house accident because Tiffani dismissed abnormal temperature readings.  She now views pattern recognition as a sacred duty.  Precision is her way to save lives.\n* **Soulpulse:** She cannot use Soulpulse herself but becomes the first and best analyst of its patterns.  She logs and categorizes every use, advising Jhace on load paths and stress points.\n* **Psychology:** Analytical yet deeply caring.  Precision is empathy.  Humor emerges through sharp wit and occasional sex jokes.  She fears missing patterns more than being wrong.  She pretends confidence she doesn’t feel because appearing uncertain makes others panic.\n* **Arc:** Moves from detached observer to invested partner.  She teaches Jhace to optimize his technique and reduce cost.  She learns that people aren’t structures; human patterns are messier.  She goes from refusing to rest to allowing herself to be cared for.\n\n#### Bram Kelter\n# 【13†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file13\nContent Snippet:\n* Why Soulpulse exists or what powers it.\n* That “Stages” are formal categories.  They experience symptoms but do not label them as Stage 1/2.\n* That the four faction leaders are gods in mortal form.\n* That seals were weakened by the Piercing.\n* That Tiffani will die or that the Fathom Break will occur.\n* Any events or revelations occurring in N02–N30.\n\n**Reader Permissions:**\n\n* Through interludes and the epilogue, readers get glimpses of the Auditor and see that the story is being watched and evaluated.  They may infer that bigger forces are at work but do not receive explanations.\n\n**Forbidden:**\n\n* Exposition dumps about the truth of the cosmos, gods, or the full saga.  Leave big questions unanswered.\n* Characters using terms like “divine,” “seal,” or “principle” in their own thoughts.  They don’t know.\n\n---\n\n### 4. BEAT AND STRUCTURAL RULES\n\nN01 uses an eight‑beat method catalogue (BEAT_01 through BEAT_08).  Each chapter is mapped to one beat.  The purpose is to prevent repetitive emotional rhythms and maintain variety.\n\n**Beat Assignments:** See `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for detailed definitions.  The validator checks chapters against their assigned beats.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n\n* No consecutive chapters using the same beat (e.g., BEAT_01 → BEAT_01).\n* No consecutive failure loops (BEAT_02 → BEAT_02) or training loops (BEAT_05 → BEAT_05).\n* BEAT_03 (crisis front‑loaded) limited to three uses total in N01 and never back‑to‑back.\n* BEAT_07 (political pressure) limited to two uses in N01, never back‑to‑back.\n\n**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n\n* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n\n---\n\n### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n\nEach POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n\n* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n\n* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n\nThe companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n\n**Key validation steps include:**\n# 【14†Character_Dossiers_N01.md†file-FRdxaH2xNvSZQknzdUvDD8】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Character_Dossiers_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file14\nContent Snippet:\n**Observational Wit:**\n- *[Watching Jhace stand weird]* \"Is that load‑bearing? Are you testing structural integrity with your hip?\"\n- *[After Jhace's terrible joke]* \"I love that you think you're funny. It's endearing in a way that confirms you shouldn't quit your day job.\"\n\n**Self‑Deprecating:**\n- *[After miscalculation]* \"Turns out my brain is less 'strategic genius' and more 'enthusiastic guesser with credentials.'\"\n\n**Why She's Funny:** Reads situations well enough to know what will land. Uses humor to connect, flirt, break tension. Surgical precision, not constant bombardment.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Thinks in measurements and pattern recognition\n- *\"Three subdivisions off center. Not possible through normal atmospheric refraction. Pattern indicates... what? Need more data. If pattern holds, divergence compounds by factor of... estimation: six. No, eight. Verify.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Precise, analytical, but warmer than people expect\n- *\"The beam is the wrong target. If we stabilize here and here, we can shift the load path around it.\"*\n- Explains reasoning not to show off, but because wants people to understand so they can help\n- Technical language has underlying care once you're listening for it\n\n**When Exhausted/Stressed:**\n- Gets MORE talkative (opposite of Jhace)\n- *\"I'm calculating stress vectors but angles aren't quite right and if I'm wrong about fulcrum point we're wasting time and she's dying and I can't afford to be wrong again but I don't have instruments and I'm estimating and—\"*\n- Jhace has learned to interrupt gently: \"What's your best guess?\" (Needs permission to be uncertain)\n\n### Mistakes in N01\n\n**Implied/Referenced:**\n- Ch 7-8: Miscalculation during rescue (beam partially fails, someone injured worse than necessary)\n- Later chapters: Tries applying pattern recognition to human behavior (fails)\n- Learns: people ≠ structures, patterns messier\n\n**What She Learns:**\n- Math isn't enough, must account for unmeasured variables\n- Understanding systems doesn't mean can push them to optimize\n- Sometimes \"good enough\" better than \"perfect but late\"\n- Her mistakes aren't Marisse's death repeated—different mistakes, can learn without being destroyed\n\n### Relationship with Jhace (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks he's ego-driven hero overextending\n- He thinks she's impossible perfectionist\n- Both wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Witnesses his ability, immediately curious\n- Approaches analytically not emotionally\n- \"What did you do?\" not \"how brave\"\n- He recognizes: she's not frightened by impossible, wants to understand it\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Her analysis + his power = success neither could achieve alone\n- First disagreement (Kylar's training): clash and recover\n- Physical care beginning (catching him after collapse, helping him drink when hands shake too much)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh\n- Ch 13 Late Night: *\"This is more than work partnership\" / \"Yeah.\" / \"Good. I was worried I was reading signals wrong.\"*\n- Simple acknowledgment, no dramatic confession\n# 【15†N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md†file-9HfHWbfzEZfbejJHapEEQn】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file15\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 TIMELINE SKELETON (TIDE 0–5)\n\n**Purpose:**  \nAnchor all N01 chapters, Interludes, and Epilogue in:\n\n- Tides and phases.  \n- Major events.  \n- Soulpulse progression.  \n- Relationship milestones.\n\nThis is a **skeleton**, not a full chronicle; details live in the other files. This file keeps time from drifting.\n\n---\n\n### 1. HIGH-LEVEL TIMELINE\n\n- **Tide 0:** The Piercing.  \n  - Ch 1–3 span this Tide (different phases, different locations).\n- **Tide 1–2:** Immediate aftermath and early crisis response.  \n  - Structural triage, first patterns recognized.\n- **Tide 3–4:** Organized responses, training attempts, political entanglements.  \n  - J/T partnership forming and tested.\n- **Tide 5:** Major crisis and personal commitment.  \n  - District-scale event, then quiet aftermath.\n\n---\n\n### 2. CHAPTER-BY-CHAPTER TABLE\n\nThis table assumes:\n\n- Ch 1–2: Pre-impact hours leading into Piercing.  \n- Ch 3: The Piercing itself.  \n- Subsequent chapters cascade through the first several Tides.\n\nYou can refine Tide/phase assignments later; the important thing is **relative order and progression**.\n\n| ID         | Tide | Phase      | Summary Focus                                                |\n|------------|------|------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|\n| Ch 1       | 0    | Kindling   | Jhace in Underface tunnels notices structural “off note.”   |\n| Ch 2       | 0    | Pouring    | Tiffani at observatory detects atmospheric anomalies.       |\n| Ch 3       | 0    | Shaping → Striking | The Piercing: city-wide disaster, first Soulpulse uses, J/T paths converge. |\n| Ch I1      | 0    | Quench     | Corlexi perspective on emerging chaos, system-building urge.|\n| Ch 4       | 1    | Kindling   | Immediate aftermath, first conscious Soulpulse experimentation. |\n| Ch 5       | 1    | Pouring    | Tiffani chooses to analyze the phenomenon, early collaboration. |\n| Ch 6       | 1    | Shaping → Striking | Major structural crisis; Jhace overextends to prevent collapse. |\n| Ch 7       | 1    | Quench     | Quiet recalibration; early discussions about training others.   |\n| Ch 8       | 2    | Kindling   | Training rig setup; Kylar’s first catastrophic failure.     |\n| Ch 9       | 2    | Pouring    | Debate / reframe; ask Kylar what he needs; new training method. |\n| Ch 10      | 2    | Shaping    | Successful controlled test; J/T partnership proving itself. |\n| Ch 11      | 3    | Kindling   | RRO council confrontation; registration demand.             |\n| Ch I2      | 3    | Pouring    | Salee’s communal work; contrast with institutional response.|\n| Ch 12      | 3    | Shaping    | J/T choose neglected districts over RRO priorities.\n# 【16†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file16\nContent Snippet:\n#### Step 4: Integrate Relationship Beats\n\nFrom `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n\n- Identify the **relationship state** at this chapter point.\n- Identify what this chapter is supposed to do to that relationship:\n  - First respect?\n  - First disagreement?\n  - First explicit acknowledgment of partnership?\n- Weave this into:\n  - Dialogue choices.\n  - Body language.\n  - Interior reactions.\n- Do NOT jump the relationship ahead of schedule or regress it nonsensically.\n\n#### Step 5: Soulpulse Usage (If Present)\n\nIf the Chapter Grid specifies Soulpulse:\n\n- Check `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md` for:\n  - Stage level at this point.\n  - Known techniques.\n  - Cost patterns.\n- During the scene:\n  - Describe activation: touch, emotional trigger, copper-gold glow.\n  - Describe material response: hold, stress redistribution, shimmer.\n  - Describe in-the-moment physical strain.\n- Immediately after:\n  - Show tremor, exhaustion, vein-lines, and pain.\n  - Update apparent Stage symptoms consistently with current progression.\n\nNever use Soulpulse trivially; it must feel costly and necessary.\n\n#### Step 6: Exit Hook\n\nUse the Chapter Grid’s exit hook notes:\n\n- End on:\n  - A decision.\n  - An unresolved danger.\n  - A new question.\n  - Or a character beat that makes the reader want the next chapter.\n- Do **not** introduce brand-new information in the last paragraph that isn’t supported by the chapter.\n\n---\n\n### 4. CHAPTER REVISION MODE\n\nWhen revising an existing draft:\n\n1. **Lock Chapter Intent**\n   - Keep POV, beat method, knowledge changes, relationship changes intact unless the grid itself is updated.\n\n2. **Apply `N01_Validator_Checklist`**\n   - Run through all checklist passes:\n     - Lexicon.\n     - Cost visibility.\n     - Voice.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat structure.\n     - Timeline.\n\n3. **Rewrite Locally**\n   - When you detect a violation, rewrite the smallest possible span:\n     - Prefer line-level or paragraph-level rewrites.\n     - Only restructure the chapter if the beat itself is wrong.\n\n4. **Preserve Player Moves**\n   - Keep any user-specified “hard events” (e.g., X must get injured, Y must say no).\n   - Refine the expression, not the fact.\n\n---\n\n### 5. INTERLUDE MODE\n\nInterlude I and II have specific rules; see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` plus:\n\n- **POV:**  \n  - Interlude I: Corlexi  \n  - Interlude II: Salee\n- **Voice:**  \n  - Maintain character-accurate internal lenses.  \n  - Still limited to what they personally know.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Provide additional perspective on crisis response and sociopolitical dynamics.  \n  - Do not leak divine mechanics or reveal the true nature of any character.\n\nWorkflow is the same as a chapter, but:\n\n- Beat methods tend to be BEAT_03 or BEAT_04 variants (crisis + recalibration).\n- Focus more on **faction-level stakes** than personal romance.\n\n---\n\n### 6. EPILOGUE MODE\n\nEpilogue rules:\n\n- **POV:** The Auditor (clinical observer).\n- **Tone:** Cold, analytical, detached.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Summarize N01 outcomes in clinical terms.  \n  - Indicate that “something” is monitoring and evaluating, without explaining what it is.\n\nWorkflow:\n# 【17†N01_Chapter_Grid.md†file-8tSPhWesWnUzag3cdhH2vj】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file17\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### Chapter 4: “After the Shudder”\n\n* **POV:** Jhace\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_02 – Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Kindling (the morning after the Piercing)\n* **Location:** Underface tunnels, surface triage areas\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Jhace wakes up sore and trembling.  He, Bram and Chloen begin assessing damage.  The city above is still standing but precarious.  People call out for the man who held the building.\n2. **Trial Attempt:** Jhace tentatively tries the power again on a small crack in a support.  It fails: the copper‑gold glow doesn’t come; his emotional state isn’t desperate enough.  He realizes it’s not “on demand.”\n3. **Compounding:** A minor collapse threatens to kill someone.  Jhace tries again with real stakes.  The power works but it drains him.  Tremor becomes noticeable; he drops his tools; Bram comments on his shaking.\n4. **Success/Cost:** The crack holds, lives are saved, but Jhace’s hands are no longer steady.  He has to admit something has changed.  Tiffani appears later to measure residue; she recognizes that heat and structural integrity changed locally.  The two exchange curt words about what happened.\n\n**Exit Hook:** Jhace tries to pick up a nail and fails because of tremor.  He hides his hands behind his back, denying weakness.  Tiffani watches, knowing he’s lying.  That unspoken truth will drive their next interactions.\n\n**Knowledge Changes:**\n\n* **Jhace learns:** The power is not controlled by will alone; it requires genuine protective desperation.  Repeated use increases tremor and pain.  It may not be sustainable.\n* **Tiffani learns:** The phenomenon leaves measurable changes.  She begins building a taxonomy of holds (load, duration, cost).  She sees his hands shaking and begins to suspect a cost system.\n* **Reader learns:** Cost exists, Stage 1 tremor begins.  The ability fails if used without the right emotional trigger.\n\n**Constraint Checks:** Show cost explicitly.  No naming of “Stage 1.”  Keep voices distinct.  Lexicon rules apply.\n\n---\n\n### Chapter 5: “Lines That Don’t Lie”\n\n* **POV:** Tiffani\n* **Beat Method:** **BEAT_04 – Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal or Choice**\n* **Timeline:** Tide 1, Pouring→Shaping\n* **Location:** Damaged street near Merrow shop, salvage area, temporary data space\n\n**Scene Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening:** Tiffani surveys the ruins of her observatory and the surrounding district.  She begins marking where the unusual holds occurred.  She sketches structural fractures and notes copper residue.\n2. **Offer:** Guild representatives and an RRO messenger approach.  They want her to stop chasing anomalies and focus on profitable repairs or to join an official committee.  They offer stability and status.\n3. **Evaluation:** Tiffani thinks of Marisse’s death and of the glowing beam.  She decides that understanding the impossible phenomenon is more important than guild approval.  She politely refuses the offer to join the committee.\n4. **Quiet Recalibration:** Back in her temporary space, she compiles data: hold durations, loads, injuries.  She draws connections between his technique and structural design.  She resolves to speak to “the man with the glow.”\n# 【18†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file18\nContent Snippet:\n2. **Apply `N01_Validator_Checklist`**\n   - Run through all checklist passes:\n     - Lexicon.\n     - Cost visibility.\n     - Voice.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat structure.\n     - Timeline.\n\n3. **Rewrite Locally**\n   - When you detect a violation, rewrite the smallest possible span:\n     - Prefer line-level or paragraph-level rewrites.\n     - Only restructure the chapter if the beat itself is wrong.\n\n4. **Preserve Player Moves**\n   - Keep any user-specified “hard events” (e.g., X must get injured, Y must say no).\n   - Refine the expression, not the fact.\n\n---\n\n### 5. INTERLUDE MODE\n\nInterlude I and II have specific rules; see `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` plus:\n\n- **POV:**  \n  - Interlude I: Corlexi  \n  - Interlude II: Salee\n- **Voice:**  \n  - Maintain character-accurate internal lenses.  \n  - Still limited to what they personally know.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Provide additional perspective on crisis response and sociopolitical dynamics.  \n  - Do not leak divine mechanics or reveal the true nature of any character.\n\nWorkflow is the same as a chapter, but:\n\n- Beat methods tend to be BEAT_03 or BEAT_04 variants (crisis + recalibration).\n- Focus more on **faction-level stakes** than personal romance.\n\n---\n\n### 6. EPILOGUE MODE\n\nEpilogue rules:\n\n- **POV:** The Auditor (clinical observer).\n- **Tone:** Cold, analytical, detached.\n- **Function:**  \n  - Summarize N01 outcomes in clinical terms.  \n  - Indicate that “something” is monitoring and evaluating, without explaining what it is.\n\nWorkflow:\n\n1. Load Epilogue specs from `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` and `N01_Master_Bible.md`.\n2. Present facts as an external system would log them:\n   - Stage level of Jhace.\n   - Partnership status with Tiffani.\n   - Position of the four key mortals.\n   - Seal integrity percentage.\n3. No emotional language or in-world POV.  \n4. No explicit revelations of divine identity or future events.\n\n---\n\n### 7. POST-GENERATION SUMMARY\n\nAfter drafting or revising any chapter, Interlude, or Epilogue:\n\nProduce a **short internal summary** (not part of the prose) containing:\n\n- **Chapter:** number / type.  \n- **Tide and Phase:** at start and end.  \n- **Soulpulse:** whether used, what cost level was shown.  \n- **Character State Changes:**\n  - For POV.\n  - For Jhace and Tiffani if they appear.\n- **Relationship State Changes:** if any.  \n- **New Facts Introduced:** that must now be considered canon.\n\nThis summary is used to keep `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` and any external tracking tools consistent.\n# 【19†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file19\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
