        "content": {
          "content_type": "multimodal_text",
          "parts": [
            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file15 in your response to cite this file. ",
            "## CHARACTER DOSSIERS: N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:** Psychological profiles, voice patterns, humor styles, and relationship dynamics for all characters appearing in N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"\n\n---\n\n## JHACE TORRINS\n\n**Age:** 29 Turns  \n**Profession:** Structural engineer, heat-grid inspector, forgemaster (unaffiliated with guild leadership)  \n**Physical:** Wiry build, weathered hands, permanent shoulder tension. Dark hair in short tail. Canvas work clothes, heavy leather tool belt. Moves efficiently, no wasted motion.\n\n### Core Psychology\n\n**Primary Driver: Obsessive Prevention Through Vigilance**\n- Parents died in building collapse when he was 7 Turns (23 people killed total)\n- Investigation concluded \"material fatigue\" – no one caught it in time\n- Has spent 22 Turns trying to be vigilant enough\n- Sees thousands of ways buildings can kill people\n- Cannot unsee potential failures\n\n**The Knot:**\n\"There are thousands of people in this city. I will spend the rest of my life unable to save most of them. I will feel responsible for all of them. This is what it means to have opened my eyes to what I can do.\"\n\n**Isolation as Armor:**\n- Works alone because teaching means inflicting his hypervigilance on others\n- Refuses apprentices (\"That's not a skill, that's anxiety that happens to be useful\")\n- Professional isolation is both choice and inability to connect\n\n### What He Fears\nFailing to prevent a death he could have prevented. Not death itself—*preventable* death. The idea that someone died because he wasn't vigilant enough, wasn't there in time, didn't check the right structure.\n\n### What He Regrets\nEvery person he walked past while responding to another crisis. Every time he chose who to help because he couldn't help everyone. Specific names of people who died while he was saving someone else. The list grows longer.\n\n### What He Lies About\n\"I'm fine.\" He's not. Hasn't been since age 7. The obsessive vigilance is exhausting. The guilt is crushing. But if he admits he's not fine, people might try to make him stop, and stopping means people die.\n\n### What He Pretends to Know\nHow to connect with people normally. He's been so focused on preventing deaths that he's forgotten how to just be with someone without cataloging the ways their environment might kill them. Pretends isolation is practical choice. Reality: doesn't know how to be close to people anymore and is terrified to try.\n\n### His \"Humor\" (Thinks He's Funny, Isn't)\n\n**Style:** Deadpan observations delivered with zero awareness they're not landing.\n\n**Examples:**\n- *Someone: \"Could this get worse?\"*\n- *Jhace: \"The building could catch fire. Or the adjacent structure could fail and crush us. Or—\"* [Complete sincerity, doesn't realize they wanted reassurance]\n\n**Embarrassing Hiccups (Running Traits):**\n\n1. **Physical Awareness Gap:** Constantly bumping into people, backing into things, standing too close or too far. When Tiffani tries to position near him, he moves away unconsciously.\n\n2. **Failed Compliments:** *To Tiffani after brilliant analysis: \"Your brain works good.\"* [Immediate cringe from everyone. He meant it genuinely.]\n\n3. **Literalism:** Cannot detect sarcasm. *Someone: \"Great, another crisis, exactly what we needed.\" Jhace: \"We didn't need this. This is bad actually.\"*\n\n4. **The Snort:** When genuinely amused (rare), snort-laughs before he can stop himself. Hates this. Tiffani loves it and deliberately tries to make him snort.\n\n5. **Standing Weird:** When thinking hard, stands in bizarre poses—balanced on one foot, tilted at odd angle, hand on hip like perpetually about to sit but never does. Completely unaware.\n\n### His Actual Laugh\nWhen something is genuinely funny (rare), he CACKLES. Loud, unrestrained, completely unselfconscious. Sharp, almost alarming. Echoes. The only time he's not performing control. Tiffani discovers she loves this and makes it her mission.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Sparse, body-focused\n- Thinks in physical sensations and structural metaphors\n- *\"Hands know before mind does. Copper-gold warmth spreading. The beam needs to hold. Make it hold.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Terse, functional, defensive\n- *\"The wall's failing.\" / \"How do you know?\" / \"I know.\"*\n- Doesn't explain intuition because doesn't fully understand it\n- When exhausted: even more clipped (*\"Can't.\" / \"Can't what?\" / \"Just. Can't.\"*)\n\n**When Actually Safe (Rare):**\n- Sentences get longer\n- Explains what he sees\n- Opens up emotionally\n- These moments precious and brief (alone with Tiffani, late night, too tired for walls)\n\n### Soulpulse Relationship in N01\n\n**Discovery Phase (Ch 1-3):**\n- First use purely instinctive (saving Chloen)\n- Second use semi-aware (holding building)\n- Doesn't understand what he's doing\n- Terrified but driven by need\n\n**Learning Phase (Ch 4-10):**\n- Experiments with deliberate activation\n- Learns emotional state matters\n- Discovers cost is real and accumulating\n- Stage 1 tremor develops and persists\n\n**Partnership Phase (Ch 10-15):**\n- Accepts Tiffani's optimization guidance\n- Learns to trust analysis over instinct\n- Stage 2 approaching by Ch 14\n- Recognizes he's breaking himself but can't stop\n\n### Relationship with Tiffani (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks she's perfectionist who prevents work\n- She thinks he's overextending ego-driven hero\n- Both completely wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Her critique helps him survive\n- His ability + her analysis = complementary\n- First mutual respect\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Working as integrated unit\n- Arguing about methods but recovering\n- Physical care developing (her catching him, him noticing her injuries)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh (her sex jokes, his accidental humor)\n- Ch 15 commitment: \"We keep doing this together\"\n\n---\n\n## TIFFANI MERROW\n\n**Age:** 27 Turns  \n**Profession:** Precision glassworker, atmospheric observer, structural analyst  \n**Physical:** Lean (forgets to eat when focused). Dark brown hair with sun-streaks in practical knot. Canvas apron over simple clothes. Hands scarred from glass-work (thin white lines on palms/fingers). Moves with deliberate efficiency. Injured leg post‑Piercing (splinted, refuses to rest properly).\n\n### Core Psychology\n\n**Primary Driver: Pattern Recognition as Atonement**\n- Best friend Marisse died in glasshouse accident 4 years ago\n- Thermal stress in oven walls—Tiffani noted fluctuations, dismissed as \"normal noise\"\n- Glass shattered during cooldown, through Marisse's throat\n- Dead before Tiffani could reach her\n- Investigation: \"Material fatigue, stress fracture from uneven cooling\"\n- Tiffani knew: she'd seen the pattern and dismissed it as irrelevant\n- She'd been wrong\n\n**Precision Disguises Care:**\n- Not trying to be perfect—trying to notice small wrong things before they become large deadly things\n- Precision isn't about perfection, it's about early warning\n- Turns down commissions not because they're beneath her standards, but because accepting inadequate specifications means people could die\n- Would rather be called impossible than responsible for another death\n\n**Connection-Oriented (Hidden Under Analysis):**\n- Fundamentally driven by desire to connect and mend\n- Wants to understand systems to explain them so everyone can be safer together\n- Analytical approach is service: \"If I understand this, I can teach it, we're all less vulnerable\"\n\n### What She Fears\nMissing another pattern. Specifically: noticing something is wrong, dismissing it as unimportant,\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]

              "content": "Since Tiffani is still unsure about calling \"Soulpulse\" by that name in Chapter 5, I'll stick to terms like \"the hold\" or \"copper-gold grip.\" I’ll update her potential use of \"pulse\" while avoiding metaphors, keeping everything precise.             "**Perfect. Yet.\")\n\n### **Beat Method (BEAT_01):** Clean execution\n- Observation → Pattern recognition → Investigation → Escalation → Decision to move\n\n### **Relationships:** All established\n- ✅ Pella (caring apprentice, measurement jokes)\n- ✅ Guild messengers (dismissive, prioritize commissions)\n- ✅ Corlexi reference (efficiency vs. precision conflict seeded)\n\n### **Marisse Trauma:** Integrated naturally\n- \"Noise killed Marisse, or my choice to call signal noise\"\n- Oven whispered, she dismissed it, cost of assuming safety\n- Motivation for hypervigilance established\n\n### **Foreshadowing:** Earned progression\n1. Star displacement (measurement)\n2. Deviation increasing (pattern forming)\n3. Metallic taste (physical symptom)\n4. Glass vibration (environmental response)\n5. Bright line across sky (object visible)\n6. Exit: \"She knew only that she would not call it noise\"\n\n---\n\n## ✅ PRODUCTION CHECKLIST:\n\n- [x] Word count within target (1,300)\n- [x] Lexicon compliance\n- [x] Voice distinct from Jhace\n- [x] Beat method executed\n- [x] Relationships established\n- [x] Trauma integrated\n- [x] Foreshadowing earned\n- [x] No metaphors/personification\n- [x] Tense consistency\n- [x] No repetition issues\n\n---\n\n**This is locked Chapter 2.**\n\n**You now have:**\n- Chapter 1 (Jhace, ~1,260 words, pre-Piercing setup)\n- Chapter 2 (Tiffani, ~1,300 words, parallel pre-Piercing setup)\n\n**Both chapters end with their characters sensing the wrongness approaching. Chapter 3 will be the Piercing event itself.**\n\nlets create a document for this prose voice like we did for chapter 1"

            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file2 in your response to cite this file. ",
            "## SOULPULSE: MECHANICS AS DISCOVERED IN N01\n\n**Purpose:** This document describes Soulpulse as it's understood and experienced during N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\" This is empirical knowledge built from observation and experimentation, NOT complete system understanding.\n\n---\n\n### WHAT IS SOULPULSE?\n\n**Observed Phenomenon:**\nFollowing the Piercing, certain individuals discovered they could affect physical reality through focused intention combined with direct contact. This wasn't magic in any traditional sense—no incantations, no reagents, no mystical gestures.\n\n**Common Element:**\nProtective intention crystallized through physical contact. The user must desperately need the material to hold/mend/stabilize AND must be touching it.\n\n---\n\n### ACTIVATION REQUIREMENTS (As Understood in N01)\n\n**Physical Contact:**\n- Requires skin-to-material contact (stone, metal, flesh, wood)\n- Works through thin materials (gloves reduce effectiveness)\n- Must maintain contact during active use\n- Breaking contact ends effect immediately\n\n**Emotional State:**\n- Demands genuine protective need\n- “I need this to hold so people survive” works\n- Casual desire or intellectual will alone doesn't work\n- Fear for others' lives seems to enhance activation\n- Anger or self-preservation less effective\n\n**Awareness:**\n- First activations always instinctive/desperate\n- Conscious activation requires practice\n- User must \"feel\" the material's stress/structure\n- Cannot activate if distracted or panicked\n\n---\n\n### VISUAL MANIFESTATION\n\n**Copper‑Gold Glow:**\n- Distinctive color, visible to observers\n- Spreads from point of contact into material\n- Intensity varies with effort/effect\n- Brighter during active use, dimmer in residue\n\n**Vein‑Lines:**\n- Tracery appears on user's forearms\n- Follows vein patterns but extends beyond normal anatomy\n- Faint when inactive, bright when active\n- Becomes more persistent with repeated use\n- First appears after 2–3 uses\n\n**Material Changes:**\n- Metal/stone shows faint shimmer during hold\n- Stress patterns visible as light/dark areas\n- Effect persists briefly after contact broken (seconds to spans)\n- Leaves subtle changes in material structure\n\n---\n\n### TACTILE EXPERIENCE (User Perspective)\n\n**During Activation:**\n- Intense pressure in palms (like pushing against resistance)\n- Vibration extending from hands through arms into chest\n- Sensation of being \"connected\" to material\n- Can feel stress points, structural integrity, weakness\n- Overwhelming when first experienced\n\n**Physical Sensation:**\n- Warmth spreading from palms\n- Muscle tension throughout arms and shoulders\n- Bone‑deep ache develops during sustained use\n- Breath becomes labored under heavy loads\n- Nausea if pushing too hard\n\n---\n\n### COST SYSTEM: STAGE PROGRESSION (N01 Scope)\n\nSoulpulse use carries a physical cost that escalates with frequency and intensity. Characters in N01 do not explicitly label stages; this section labels them for author reference.\n\n**Stage 0: Baseline (Pre‑Use)**\n- No tremor\n- Normal physical function\n- No visible vein‑lines\n- No chronic symptoms\n\n**Stage 1: Minor Tremor (After 2–3 Uses)**\n- Slight tremor in hands when attempting fine motor tasks\n- Noticeable when picking up small objects, detailed work\n- Fades partially after several spans of rest\n- Vein‑lines appear during use, fade when inactive\n- User can still function normally in non‑precision tasks\n- Most users don't progress past this if they use sparingly\n\n**Progression to Stage 1 in N01:**\n- Jhace: Ch 3 (after first two instinctive uses)\n- Maintained through Ch 4–7\n- Solidifies by Ch 10\n\n**Stage 2: Baseline Tremor (After 5–10 Uses in Short Timeframe)**\n- Tremor becomes constant, doesn't fully fade with rest\n- Vein‑lines remain faintly visible even when not using power\n- Deep exhaustion that sleep doesn't completely resolve\n- Fine motor control compromised (difficulty with precision work)\n- Can still work but efficiency drops significantly\n- This becomes new normal—body fundamentally changed\n- Reversible with extended rest (multiple Anneals) but body sensitized\n\n**Progression to Stage 2 in N01:**\n- Jhace: Beginning Ch 12 (after political conflicts force heavy use)\n- Solidified by Ch 14 (major district‑spanning crisis)\n- Maintained through\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]

            "## N01 — QUICK REFERENCE TABLES\n\n**Purpose:** Fast-look tables so a prose engine (or human) can answer “who/where/when/what cost?” without re-reading all structural docs.\n\nThese tables summarize; the full truth remains in the other files.\n\n---\n\n### 1. CHARACTER PRESENCE & POV BY CHAPTER\n\n| Ch | Title (Working)           | POV      | Key Supporting Characters        | Relationship Beat Focus                       |\n|----|---------------------------|----------|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|\n| 1  | Something Wrong           | Jhace    | Bram, Chloen                     | Jhace + Bram baseline; heroism vs survival        |\n| 4  | After the Shudder        | Jhace    | Bram, Chloen                     | Chloen’s healing; Jhace’s emerging responsibility to her |\n| 5  | Lines That Don’t Lie     | Tiffani  | Pella, guild figures             | Tiffani chooses to study the phenomenon       |\n| 6  | Weight That Won’t Yield  | Jhace    | Bram, Tiffani, rescuers          | First fully integrated J/T crisis collaboration |\n| I1 | Interlude I (Corlexi)    | Corlexi  | RRO officials                    | Institutional worldview vs chaos               |\n| 7  | Teaching the Grid        | Tiffani  | Jhace, Kylar, Chloen             | J/T methodological conflict beginnings        |\n| 8  | First Break, First Lesson| Jhace    | Kylar, Tiffani, Bram             | Kylar’s failure; J/T clash                     |\n| 9  | Asking the Right Question| Tiffani  | Kylar, Jhace                     | “What do you need?” synthesis; trust deepen   |\n| 10 | Hold Together            | Jhace    | Tiffani, Bram, team              | Seamless team operation; success with heavy cost |\n| 11 | Terms of Control         | Jhace    | Tiffani, Corlexi, council        | J/T united front against registration         |\n| I2 | Interlude II (Salee)     | Salee    | Dregs community                  | Connection-based infrastructure                |\n| 12 | Where Help Goes First    | Tiffani  | Jhace, Salee (brief), locals     | Ethical choice: neglected districts           |\n| 13 | Too Tired to Pretend     | Jhace    | Tiffani, Pella (off-page mention)| First clear romantic acknowledgment           |\n| 14 | Fault Lines              | Jhace    | Tiffani, Bram, Kylar, Salee      | Major crisis; Jhace near Stage 2              |\n| 15 | The Grip That Holds      | Tiffani  | Jhace                            | Quiet commitment; “we keep doing this together” |\n| E  | Epilogue (Auditor)       | Auditor  | –                                | Meta assessment; no direct relationships      |\n\n(Titles can change; functional roles should not.)\n\n---\n\n### 2. SOULPULSE USAGE & COST BY CHAPTER\n\n| Ch | User    | Technique Type         | Stage State      | Immediate Cost Shown                                  | New Info Gained                         |\n|----|---------|------------------------|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|\n| 1  | –       | –                      | Stage 0          | –                                                     | Baseline of non-magic structural work   |\n| 2  | –       | –                      | Stage 0          | –                                                     | Atmospheric anomalies noticed           |\n| 3  | Jhace   | Structural hold + side-effect healing | 0 → 1 onset | Full-body strain, collapse after, Chloen’s leg healed | First activation, healing side-effect   |\n| 4\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"

            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file3 in your response to cite this file. ",
            "## RELATIONSHIP DYNAMICS: N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:** Track how the key relationships form, develop and function during N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"  These summaries guide prose generation by clarifying which emotional beats occur where and what level of intimacy exists at any given point.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & TIFFANI – THE CORE PARTNERSHIP\n\nThis is the emotional spine of N01.  They move from strangers with misconceptions to partners who know they can’t survive the crisis without each other.\n\n#### Phase 1: Mutual Judgment (Ch 1–3)\n\n* **Jhace’s view:** Tiffani is the perfectionist who prevents work.  Guild gossip paints her as someone who refuses commissions if specifications aren’t perfect, causing projects to stall.  He assumes that sort of person will get good workers killed by refusing to act.\n* **Tiffani’s view:** Jhace is the overextending hero who thinks only he can save people.  She believes he takes on too much out of ego.  From a distance it looks like savior complex disguised as quality control.\n* **Reality:** Both are wrong.  They haven’t met yet and are projecting their own fears.  This phase ends when the Piercing forces their paths to cross.\n\n#### Phase 2: Competence Recognition (Ch 4–7)\n\n* **First contact (Ch 3):** Tiffani sees Jhace holding a collapsing building.  Her first reaction isn’t awe but analytical curiosity.  “What did you do?” matters more than “how brave.”\n* **His recognition:** During the major hold in Ch 6, Tiffani’s critique (“You’re applying force uniformly when the load is uneven”) annoys him until he realizes she’s right.  It wasn’t judgment; it was insight that saves lives.\n* **Her recognition:** In Ch 7, she sees how exhausted and shaken he is after each use.  She realizes his obsessive vigilance mirrors her own fear of missed patterns.  They’re both serving the same moral imperative with different tools.\n* **Status at the end of Phase 2:** They respect each other professionally.  Partnership is brewing but not yet named.  Care emerges as Tiffani catches him when he collapses and he insists she rest her injured leg.\n\n#### Phase 3: Partnership Formation (Ch 8–11)\n\n* **Trust building:** They integrate their efforts.  He allows her to guide where he applies his power; she modifies her analytical approach based on what his body can handle.  They decide resource priorities together.\n* **Conflict:** They have their first methodological fight in Ch 9 over how to train Kylar.  She wants slower emotional recovery; he wants immediate practice to rebuild confidence.  Asking Kylar what he needs resolves the fight and shows they can disagree without breaking trust.\n* **Physical intimacy emerges:** Not yet sexual.  Touch now includes brushing fingers, sitting shoulder to shoulder, passing cups during tremor.  Their bodies learn to relax around each other.\n* **Political alignment:** In Ch 11 they stand together against Corlexi’s registration demand.  It’s their first united front to an external authority.\n\n#### Phase 4: Romantic Recognition (Ch 12–15)\n\n* **The shift:** They both realize the partnership is more than professional.  Small non‑work gestures accumulate into undeniable feelings.  Silence becomes comfortable rather than loaded.\n* **Acknowledgment scene (Ch 13):** After a long day, she admits “This is more than work partnership, isn’t it?”  He replies “Yeah.”  That is their declaration.  They acknowledge romantic attraction without drama.\n* **Physical escalation:** They hold each other after crises, share body heat while sitting, enjoy the closeness.  Tiffani begins making surgical sex jokes directed at him (see Character Dossiers) to lighten tension.  They avoid consummation only because they’re physically exhausted.\n* **Commitment scene (Ch 15):** They sit in a quiet space and decide to keep holding together.  “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  “Then we hold.”  No formal vows; this promise is enough.\n* **Status at novella end:** They are a pair in all but name.  The world sees them as partners.  They remain physically restrained because of exhaustion and crisis, but their emotional commitment is clear.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & BRAM – THE STEADY ANCHOR\n\nBram is Jhace’s older colleague and confidant.  He bridges the gap between Jhace and the\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]

            "Received. From this point forward, I will treat **your “N01 Chapter Grid v2.0 (12-chapter structure)”** as the **active drafting law** for N01: POV assignments, beat methods, interlude placements, Soulpulse counts, stage progression, and the chapter-by-chapter required outcomes.\n\nOne important practical note: your **uploaded canon pack still contains the old grid**, so to make this change “real canon” for this GPT, you’ll want to **save your text as a new markdown file and replace the old Chapter Grid file in the project files**. I can’t overwrite the uploaded files myself, but I can hand you a clean, ready-to-drop-in document.\n\n## Ready-to-save canon file\n\nSave the following as:\n\n**`N01_Chapter_Grid_v2_12Ch.md`**\n\n(Then upload it into your GPT’s canon files, and remove/retire the prior grid file so there’s no conflict.)\n\n---\n\n## N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS — CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)  \n**REVISED EDITION — MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n### CRITICAL NOTICE  \nThis grid must be consulted before writing or revising any prose, making structural decisions, adjusting beat assignments, or moving content between chapters. Any deviation requires explicit authorization and a grid update.\n\n### NOVELLA PARAMETERS  \n- **Novella ID:** N01  \n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds  \n- **Turning:** 1 — GLASS  \n- **Structure:** **12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments**  \n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000–21,000 words total  \n- **Chapter Range:** **Default 800–1400; BEAT_03 crisis chapters may extend up to 2200**  \n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 through Tide 20\n\n### ACT STRUCTURE  \n- **ACT I: THE CUT (Ch 1–4)** — Setup + Piercing + immediate aftermath  \n- **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Ch 5–9 + Interlude I after Ch 7)** — partnership formation, institutional pressure  \n- **ACT III: THE GRIP (Ch 10–12 + Interlude II after Ch 10)** — bond under stress, commitment, foundation locked\n\n---\n\n# CHAPTER GRID\n\n## CH 1 — Something Wrong  \n**POV:** Jhace | **Tide:** 0 Kindling | **Beat:** BEAT_01 | **Status:** LOCKED  \n**Soulpulse:** none\n\n## CH 2 — Glass That Listens  \n**POV:** Tiffani | **Tide:** 0 Pouring | **Beat:** BEAT_01 | **Status:** LOCKED  \n**Soulpulse:** none\n\n## CH 3 — The Sky Breaks  \n**POV:** Jhace | **Tide:** 0 Striking | **Beat:** BEAT_03 | **Status:** LOCKED  \n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses; Stage 1 onset\n\n## CH 4 — After the Shudder  \n**POV:** Jhace | **Tide:** 1 Kindling | **Beat:** BEAT_02 | **Status:** LOCKED  \n**Soulpulse:** 1 success + 1 failed attempt; Stage 1 baseline established\n\n## CH 5 — Lines That Don’t Lie  \n**POV:** Tiffani | **Tide:** 2–3 | **Beat:** BEAT_04 | **Status:** LOCKED  \n**Soulpulse:** none (observation only)\n\n## CH 6 — Weight That Won’t Yield  \n**POV:** Jhace | **Tide:** 3–4 | **Beat:** BEAT_03 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use; cost increases; first choreographed hold with Tiffani marking points\n\n## CH 7 — Quiet Calculations  \n**POV:** Tiffani | **Tide:** 5–6 | **Beat:** BEAT_20 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** none; cost curve analysis; decision to approach with proof\n\n## INTERLUDE I — Corlexi: System vs Chaos  \n**POV:** Corlexi | **Tide:** ~6–7 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Purpose:** Form lens; institutional geometry; anomaly flagged; Auditor frame\n\n## CH 8 — Proof and Resistance  \n**POV:** Jhace | **Tide:** 7–8 | **Beat:** BEAT_12 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test; reduced cost confirmed; resistance → acceptance\n\n## CH 9 — Ash and Claims  \n**POV:** Tiffani | **Tide:** 10–12 | **Beat:** BEAT_16 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** none; political alignment pressure; explicit partnership choice\n\n## CH 10 — Near Collapse  \n**POV:** Both | **Tide:** 14–15 | **Beat:** BEAT_03 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use; near Stage 2 warning signs; Tiffani override triggers relationship rupture\n\n## INTERLUDE II — Mobel: Purity Rising  \n**POV:** Mobel | **Tide:** ~15–16 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Purpose:** Purity lens; inter-god awareness; Auditor frame\n\n## CH 11 — Holding Together  \n**POV:** Both | **Tide:** 17–18 | **Beat:** BEAT_17 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** none; vulnerability exchange; first deliberate non-crisis contact\n\n## CH 12 — Choosing the Weight  \n**POV:** Both | **Tide:** 19–20 | **Beat:** BEAT_21 | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Soulpulse:** none; three priorities written; community marks; shared tremor moment\n\n## EPILOGUE — Auditor Observation  \n**POV:** Auditor | **Status:** TO WRITE  \n**Purpose:** meta-metrics; anomaly flag; setup pressure for N02\n\n---\n\n# ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS (unchanged)\n- Te’Oga lexicon only; no Earth units/time/culture  \n- No metaphors/similes; minimal personification  \n- Soulpulse requires contact + protective need; copper-gold visible; cost must show (tremor, vein-lines, exhaustion)  \n- Knowledge gates: no later-novella reveals\n\n---\n\n## Operational production rule so we stop trimming forever\n**Default chapter length = 800–1400 words.**  \nOnly BEAT_03 crisis chapters may go longer, **hard capped at 2200**.\n\n---\n\nIf you want, I’ll proceed next with **Chapter 6 (Jhace POV, BEAT_03)** using this grid as the controlling reference and keeping it in the **~1600–2000** band unless you tell me to stay tighter."
